## PAGE 00982: Rules of Stereotypes that cannot be Allowed to Apply

- page_id: `169679121`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679121/Rules+of+Stereotypes+that+cannot+be+Allowed+to+Apply
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-01-24T11:59:05.635+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Creating Customization Data
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When applying a stereotype to a DSL element, the list of the available stereotypes that may be applied is filtered according to the following rules:

- Only one DSL stereotype (with Hide Metatype=*true*) may be applied to an element. For example, if «Block» is already applied, the «Requirement» stereotype will not appear in the stereotype shortcut menu.
- In order to apply a new DSL stereotype (e.g. when changing from «Requirement» to «businessRequirement»), clear the currently applied stereotype and apply the change. The stereotype shortcut menu will then show all DSL stereotypes. Tip!Quickly change the stereotype by converting one element type to other (in the DSL element shortcut menu, click **Refactor**and then **Convert**).

- DSL ownership rules are checked and it is not allowed to apply a stereotype not allowed in the parent element of DSL element.
- DSL relationship rules are checked and it is not allowed to apply a stereotype if a relationship exists and the source or target element violates DSL rules for the existing DSL relationship.
- DSL rules are also checked when drawing new relationships in a diagram.

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Element Properties' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating your First Customization' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673793 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00983: Running the CATIA FLXML Exporter plugin in command line

- page_id: `169673793`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673793/Running+the+CATIA+FLXML+Exporter+plugin+in+command+line
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-20T14:25:00.489+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > CATIA applications > CATIA FLXML Exporter
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can execute the CATIA FLXML Exporter plugin by using the command line interface both on the server and on the client machine.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

- Before starting, learn how to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Running programs in batch mode' space='MD2024xR1'] .
- When running the CATIA FLFML Exporter plugin in the command line, follow the recommended usage of the plugin described above. Any other usage of the plugin must be avoided
- Before starting, you must study the model and find out what data you want to export to an FLXML file.
- Each client can have a different configuration (e.g., Windows, Linux, etc.), so the execution of the plugin should be adapted to the client configuration.

To run the plugin and export model data to an FLXML file by using the command line

1. Download the [ATTACHMENT filename='generate_flxml.properties'] file.
2. In the generate_flxml.proprties file, specify [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Running the CATIA FLXML Exporter plugin in command line' space='MD2024xR1'] .
3. Runthe CATIA FLXML Exporter plugin and export model data in one of the following ways:
  - Download and execute the *[ATTACHMENT filename='script_commandline.bat']* script. Executing the script_comandline.bat scriptBefore executing the script, make sure to replace the placeholders between <> with the actual values for the following properties:**%MAGICDRAW_HOME%** - specify the path to the modeling tool installation directory.**properties** - specify the path to the *generate_flxml.properties* file.
  - Implement the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction*interface as described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Running programs in batch mode' space='MD2024xR1'].

##### The CATIA FLXML Exporter plugin options

To export model data to an FLXML file by using the command line interface you have to specify the following properties in the *generate_flxml.properties* file.

| Property | Mandatory | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| project | Yes | Specify the path to the project you want to use to generate an FLXML file, e.g., e:\\data\\Aircraft Avionic.mdzip |
| schema | Yes | Specify the RFLP schema used to convert model elements to FL objects during export. You can use the default RFLP schema - SysML to FL or specify a custom schema. |
| destination_folder | Yes | Specify the path to the folder where the FLXML file should be saved. If the folder does not exist, it will be created during file generation. |
| scope_elements | Yes | Specify the list of elements to be exported to an FLXML file. Separate each element by a semicolon (;). Elements can be specified as:Element qualified name.Element ID. When specifying an element ID, use this format: id:<element_ID>Example:White box::Logical architecture::Logical architecture;id:_19_0_1_1a800cf_1559826887570_81532_41786;id:_19_0_1_1a800cf_1559826887379_499848_41443 |
| no_flxml_if_error | No | Set to true (the default value) for the command line execution to stop automatically if an error occurs while generating an FLXML file. In addition, the error text will be displayed in the console.Set to false to generate the FLXM file even if any generation errors occur. |
| log_results | No | Set to true if you want to generate a file with model data export results. The file includes export errors and both converted and not converted objects. It is saved in the same directory as the generated FLXML file. Otherwise, set to false (the default value). |
| log_format | No | The log_format property is only valid if the log_results property is set to true.Specify the file format for the model data export results (the log_results property). The possible values are:xls - an MS Excel file is generated.txt - a text file is generated.xlstxt - both MS Excel and text files are generated. |
| log_info | No | The log_info property is only valid if the log_results property is set to true.Set to true (the default value) if you do not want the annotations of converted and not converted objects to be included in the model data export results (the log_results property). Otherwise, set to false. |
| option_auto_ports | No | Set to true to automatically generate interfaces in the FLXML file if ports or pins do not exist in the UML/SysML model. Otherwise, set to false (the default value). |
| option_escalated_links | No | Set to true to generate and manage the escalated link. Otherwise, set to false (the default option). |
| option_reference_out_of_scope_elements | No | Set to true (the default value) to automatically manage the references to elements located outside of the model data export scope (the scope_elements property). Otherwise, set to false. |
| option_dependecy_relationships | No | Set to true to automatically discover dependency relationships for each element (can be time-consuming). Otherwise, set to false (the default value). |
| option_create_top_refref_implement_links | No | Set to true to automatically create REF-REF implement links for an OCC-OCC link if the REF-REF implement links do not exist. Otherwise, set to false (the default value). |
| option_manage_effectivity | No | Set to true to generate effectivity files and a modified FLXML file to support effectivity. Otherwise, set to false (the default value). |
| option_export_image_for_diagrams | No | Set to true to generate images for symbol diagrams that you can later import to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. Otherwise, set to false (the default value). |
| option_flxml_preview | No | Set to true (the default value) to generate an RFLXML file to be imported to the CATIA Systems Traceability in the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. Otherwise, set to false. |
| option_export_element_image | No | Set to true to generate images for FL references of the elements with the «CustomImageHolder» stereotype. Otherwise, set to false (the default value). |
| import_xml_file | No | Specify the path to the file that defines the XMLModel used by CATFLEditorImportBatch. This will allow you to modify XmlPath (and ImagePath if required).For more information about the file, see https://dsdoc.dsone.3ds.com/3DEXPERIENCER2021x/en/English/DSDocDSExa.htm?show=SadUserMap/fle-r-BatchForImportExport.htm&XMLToc=DSDocDS.xml&contextscope=onpremise |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676157 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00985: Sample of the Profile Migration Transformation

- page_id: `169676157`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676157/Sample+of+the+Profile+Migration+Transformation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-01-11T10:45:36.842+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Profile Migration Transformation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This sample describes step-by-step instructions how to create profile migration mapping rules and perform transformation. In this sample we will change one stereotype to other.

1. Create Book stereotype with Class metaclass.
2. Create your model, for example, create Source package with Source Class diagram. Draw the Sample Class and apply the Book stereotype. To change the Book stereotype to other, for example to Magazine stereotype, you have to create profile migration transformation mapping rules. Follow next steps for creating the mapping.
3. Create the stereotype Magazine with Class metaclass.
4. From the Magazine stereotype to the Book stereotype draw Dependency relationship.
5. Apply ReplaceStereotype stereotype to the Dependency relationship. Profile Migration transformation mapping rule is created. Now you can start transformation.
6. To start the transformation, from the Tools menu, select Model Transformations . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Model Transformation Wizard' space='MD2024xR1'] opens.
7. Select the Profile Migration transformation and click Next .
8. Select the Source package in the Select source/destination step. Click Next .
9. The From and To fields display the mappings of the selected transformation profile in the Check mappings step. Click Finish .

After this transformation stereotype of Sample Class will be changed to Magazine stereotype.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This sample describes step-by-step instructions how to create profile migration mapping rules and perform transformation. In this sample we will change one stereotype to other.</p><ol><li>Create <em>Book</em> stereotype with Class metaclass.</li><li>Create your model, for example, create <em>Source</em> package with <em>Source Class</em> diagram. Draw the <em>Sample Class</em> and apply the <em>Book</em> stereotype.<br />To change the <em>Book</em> stereotype to other, for example to <em>Magazine</em> stereotype, you have to create profile migration transformation mapping rules. Follow next steps for creating the mapping.</li><li>Create the stereotype <em>Magazine</em> with <em>Class</em> metaclass. </li><li>From the <em>Magazine</em> stereotype to the <em>Book</em> stereotype draw Dependency relationship.</li><li>Apply <em>ReplaceStereotype</em> stereotype to the Dependency relationship.<br />Profile Migration transformation mapping rule is created. Now you can start transformation.</li><li>To start the transformation, from the <strong>Tools</strong> menu, select <strong>Model Transformations</strong>. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Model Transformation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Transformation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Select the <strong>Profile Migration</strong> transformation and click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>Select the <em>Source</em> package in the <strong>Select source/destination</strong> step. Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>The <strong>From</strong> and <strong>To</strong> fields display the mappings of the selected transformation profile in the <strong>Check mappings</strong> step. Click <strong>Finish</strong> .</li></ol><p>After this transformation stereotype of Sample Class will be changed to Magazine stereotype.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Profile Migration Transformation mapping" ac:alt="Profile Migration Transformation mapping"><ri:attachment ri:filename="profile_migration_transformation1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sample of the Profile Migration Transformation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674802 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00986: Save As Image dialog

- page_id: `169674802`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674802/Save+As+Image+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-10-21T11:35:21.633+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Saving as image
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

903939278

903939280

903939279

The**Save As Image** dialog allows you to save one or multiple diagrams of your model as images. Also, you can save only the selected symbols instead of an entire diagram, and choose the desired image format.

To open the **Save As Image** dialog

- From the File menu, select Save as Image .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The view of the Save As Image dialog when the Selected diagrams option button is selected.

| Dialog element | Element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Active diagram | Option button | Select to save as image the currently active diagram. The active diagram name is displayed in brackets next to the option button name. |
| Selected symbols | Option button | Select to save as image specific symbols that are selected in the diagram. The option is active only if one or multiple symbols are selected in the diagram. |
| Selected diagrams | Option button | Select the option in order to save as image the selected diagrams in the model tree or diagram list. |
| Select diagrams | Text box | The text box is available only if the Selected diagrams option button is selected.In the Select diagrams box, type the name of the diagram you want to find. Search results are displayed as a model tree or a plain list right under the text box. Click the Tree or List tab in the search result area to switch to the desired view, and select the diagram(s) you want to save as image(s).To optimize your search, use . The Apply Filter check box and the filter options button are located below the search result area. |
| Image File / Working Directory | Text box | Select the working directory in which the image will be saved. |
| Image Format | Drop-down list | Select the image format. |
| Options | Button | Click to open the where you can specify image export properties, such as image resolution and exported image size. The properties provided in the Properties dialog depend on the selected image format. |
| Overwrite existing files | Check box | Select to overwrite already saved image files. |

903939277

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving as image' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677230 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00987: Saving a local copy of Teamwork Cloud project

- page_id: `169677230`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677230/Saving+a+local+copy+of+Teamwork+Cloud+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-29T15:02:29.999+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Collaboration between disconnected teams > Using local project file format
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

756400971

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

756400974

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

756400973

To assign a Teamwork Cloud project to a user who cannot access the project on the server, or to move the project to another server, you can save a local copy on your computer. This enables you to send the local copy by email or add it to another server.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The local copy of the project will not have locking and project version information. You will not be able to commit local project changes to the server.

To save a local copy of a Teamwork Cloud project

1. From the File menu, select Save Project As .
2. The question box appears. If you are sure you want to save a local copy of the server project, click Save .
3. Specify a name and location to save the project.
4. Wait while the local copy is saved to your computer.

756400970

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration between disconnected teams' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Offline modeling' space='MD2024xR1']

## PAGE 00988: Saving an Import Map

- page_id: `169675583`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675583/Saving+an+Import+Map
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:12.271+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2091477233

2091477248

2091477238

**On this page**

**5**

2091477231

##### Introduction

An Import Map is a single instance of mapping between Excel or CSV file data and exported elements. Once you saved the Import Map, you can reuse it when importing data by using a single map or a group of maps. Also, you can override an existing Import Maps instead of creating new ones if changes are required. As an example, in the image below, the new *CarBreak Requirements Import* map is saved under the *Excel/C**SV i**mports* Package. This Package is created automatically when saving your first Import Map.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The CarBreak requirements, structure and satisfy relationship Import Maps saved under the *Excel/CSV imports* package in the **Save Import Map** dialog.

##### Saving a new Import Map

To save an Import Map

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog specify mandatory options. You must select the file in the **Select Excel/CSV file** areaand specify the**Element Type**,**Properties to Map**and **Target Scope** mapping option values to import data.
4. Click the Save Map button. The Save Import Map dialog opens.
5. In the **Tree** tab select the location to save a map. We highly recommend saving Import Maps to an Excel/CSV imports Package.
6. Click the Create button.
7. In the Specification window, type a map name in the Name property value. [ATTACHMENT filename='import_map_specification.png']
8. Click Close .
9. Click OK . Your map is saved under the selected Package.

##### Overriding an existing Import Map

To override an existing Import Map

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > **Import Using Existing Map** .
2. In the **Select Import Map** dialog, select the existing Import Map that you want to override.
3. Click OK .
4. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog change mapping options.
5. Click the**Save Map** button. The **Save Import Map** dialog opens.
6. Select an Import Map you want to override. By default, the Import Map you chose to load in #2 step is selected in the **Tree** tab.
7. Cl ick OK . The Import Map is overridden.

2091485544

**Related pages**

****

- [Importing data from Excel or CSV files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files)

- Excel and CSV Import dialog

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674801 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00989: Saving as image

- page_id: `169674801`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674801/Saving+as+image
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:24:17.854+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Selected diagrams

903890865

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Selected diagrams

903890868

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Selected diagrams

903890867

You can save an image as an active diagram, selected symbols, or selected diagrams.

Diagrams and symbols that were created in the model can be saved as images in the following formats:

- Enhanced Metafile Format (*.emf) (supports language specific symbols)
- Encapsulated PostScript (*.eps)
- Joint Photographic Experts Group (*.jpg, *.jpeg)
- Portable Network Graphics (*.png)
- Scalable Vector Graphics (*.svg)
- Tagged Image File Format (*.tif, *.tiff)
- Windows Metafile Format (*.wmf)

To save an image

1. Open the diagram you want to save as an image. If you want to save only particular symbols, select them on the diagram.
2. On the main menu, select File > Save as Image . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Save As Image** dialog opens.
3. In the dialog:
  - Select whether to save an **Active diagram**, **Selected symbols**, or **Selected diagrams**. Selected diagrams**Selected diagrams** allow you to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024xR1'] in your model.
  - Specify the directory you want to save the image to.
  - Select the image format. Click Options [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1'] according to the selected format.
4. Click Save . The image is saved in the directory you have specified.

You can specify the diagram export options, such as dimensions and building time (the latter applies only to tables and matrices), in the **Project Options** dialog. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram image export' space='MD2024xR1'] page to learn more.

903890864

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024xR1']Diagramming
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024xR1'] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Printing' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram image export' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd6163f0-0ed3-4c1c-a290-c0c19d469628"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">903890865</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe4288b7-07b9-4756-92bc-2234c74efbbe"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">903890868</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="748a14a8-3c43-4dd5-852c-778818bb62ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">903890867</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can save an image as an active diagram, selected symbols, or selected diagrams.</p><p>Diagrams and symbols that were created in the model can be saved as images in the following formats:</p><ul><li>Enhanced Metafile Format (*.emf) (supports language specific symbols)</li><li>Encapsulated PostScript (*.eps)</li><li>Joint Photographic Experts Group (*.jpg, *.jpeg)</li><li>Portable Network Graphics (*.png)</li><li>Scalable Vector Graphics (*.svg)</li><li>Tagged Image File Format (*.tif, *.tiff)</li><li>Windows Metafile Format (*.wmf)</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To save an image</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the diagram you want to save as an image. If you want to save only particular symbols, select them on the diagram.</li><li>On the main menu, select <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Save as Image</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Save As Image dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Save As Image</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>In the dialog:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select whether to save an <strong>Active diagram</strong>, <strong>Selected symbols</strong>, or <strong>Selected diagrams</strong>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4d6f1f2-7008-40ac-aa9b-912d4c92a548"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Selected diagrams</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Selected diagrams</strong> allow you to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[search for a particular diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in your model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Specify the directory you want to save the image to.</li><li>Select the image format. Click <strong>Options </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Properties dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[to specify image options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> according to the selected format.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong>. The image is saved in the directory you have specified.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="519fb3a1-8e91-45c4-8872-407c6de756da"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify the diagram export options, such as dimensions and building time (the latter applies only to tables and matrices), in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram image export" /></ac:link> page to learn more.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="15559376-dd89-4898-89d1-8508f57d09a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">903890864</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="6bc351b0-4030-4f2f-b352-cc128a1ac39f" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /><ac:link-body>Diagramming<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Printing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rinting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagram image export" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673421 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00990: Saving projects

- page_id: `169673421`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673421/Saving+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T14:57:22.445+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The native modeling tool format is **.mdzip* and **.mdxml*. You can also save projects as **.xml* and **.xml.zip*.

To save changes for later sessions, projects must be saved. While saving, you can edit the name of the project and its file format.

To save the project

1. From the File menu, select Save Project or Save Project As . Alternatively, you can click the Save button on the main toolbar or press the shortcut keys Ctrl + S. The Save dialog opens.
2. Select the destination directory (where you want the project to be saved) and type the chosen file name. The default project saving location is set according to your operating system:**Windows**: My Documents/**Linux**: /home/<username>/**OS X**: Users/Documents/

3. Select the format for saving a project:

- 
  - Packed MagicDraw File Format (**.mdzip*) (default). **.mdzip* is a MagicDraw native format. It contains various parts of the project compressed. You should select it if you intend to work with the project only in the modeling tool's environment.
  - MagicDraw File Format (**.mdxml*). You should choose it if you intend to analyze the project's structure, xml, or for other purposes. You can open *.mdxml projects with other UML tools as well.
  - XML (**.xml*). It is the same file format as **.mdxml*, only that **.mdxml* files can be opened with a modeling tool, whereas **.xml* files can only be opened with an XML editor.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- If the Create Backup File check box is selected in the Environment Options dialog, the modeling tool automatically creates a backup file of the previously saved project. The backup is held in a file with a name identical to that of the project.
- If you want to save maximum additional information to an *.xmi file, select the Rich XMI check box in the Environment Options dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can also save projects as <em>*.xml</em> and <em>*.xml.zip</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To save changes for later sessions, projects must be saved. The <strong>Save</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p>Select the destination directory (where you want the project to be saved) and type the chosen file name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59ad429d-26bf-4f57-85ff-888ba8ede374"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default project saving location is set according to your operating system:</p><ul><li><strong>Windows</strong>: My Documents/</li><li><strong>Linux</strong>: /home/&lt;username&gt;/</li><li><strong>OS X</strong>: Users/Documents/</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">3. Select the format for saving a project:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Packed MagicDraw File Format (<em>*.mdzip</em>) (default). <em>*.mdzip</em> is a MagicDraw native format. You should choose it if you intend to analyze the project's structure, xml, or for other purposes. You can open *.mdxml projects with other UML tools as well.</span></li><li class="_mce_tagged_br"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">XML (<em>*.xml</em>). It is the same file format as <em>*.mdxml</em>, only that <em>*.mdxml</em> files can be opened with a modeling tool, whereas <em>*.xml</em> files can only be opened with an XML editor.</span></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="_mce_tagged_br"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span><ac:image ac:title="Save dialog" ac:alt="Save dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Saving projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b21069a7-3b65-449c-a02b-e39bedf3ebac"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the <strong>Create Backup File</strong> check box is selected in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog, the modeling tool automatically creates a backup file of the previously saved project. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677212 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00991: Saving server project to exchange format

- page_id: `169677212`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677212/Saving+server+project+to+exchange+format
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-29T14:59:03.640+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Collaboration between disconnected teams > Using server project file format
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To save a server project to an exchange format

1. From the File menu, select Export To > Server Project File and one of the following options:
  - Without Used Projects: export the main project without used projects. All of the content from used projects will still be embedded into the main project, but importing or updating used projects will not be available;
  - With Used Projects: export the full project with used projects.
2. Specify a name and location to save the project.
3. Wait while the local copy is saved to your computer.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To save a server project to an exchange format</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>File</strong> menu, select <strong>Export To &gt; Server Project File </strong>and one of the following options:<br /><ul><li><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8312fec2-2efb-4681-8e36-2b188b6d56eb">Without Used Projects: </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong>export the main project without used projects. All of the content from used projects will still be embedded into the main project, but importing or updating used projects will not be available;</li><li><strong>With Used Projects: </strong>export the full project with used projects.</li></ul></li><li>Specify a name and location to save the project.</li><li>Wait while the local copy is saved to your computer.</li></ol>
````

To save the filter criteria

- On the Options toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] and then select Save Filter Criteria .

When exporting the table, only the filtered out columns and rows will be included in the result.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678213 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00993: Scaling images

- page_id: `169678213`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678213/Scaling+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-08T04:06:40.047+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Alternative scaling methods

2050246330

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Alternative scaling methods

2050246342

2050246328

**On this page**

42

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Alternative scaling methods

2050246332

There are two scaling methods that you can use to scale an image using a given factor. For example, Method 1 provides height and width parameters (scaleWidth and scaleHeight), while Method 2 provides a single parameter that will scale both axes equally (scaleFactor). These three parameters must be positive real numbers.

###### **$image.scale(image, scaleWidth, scaleHeight)**

Return an image icon for an element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | scaleWidth | java.lang.Double | The width parameter of the image. |
|  | scaleHeight | java.lang.Double | The height parameter of the image. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.scale(image, scaleFactor)**

Return an image icon for an element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | scaleFactor | java.lang.Double | The scaling parameter of the image. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

Use **$image.scale($diagram.image, 0.5)**, for example, to scale down the image to half the original size. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image scale function.

Use **$image.scale($diagram.image, 0.8, 0.6)**to scale the image’s width down to 80% and height to 60%. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image scale function.

#### NOTE: Alternative scaling methods

Alternative scaling methods

The images used in generated reports can also be scaled by specifying the **Exported Image Size** property in one of the following locations:

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Properties** dialog , which is used to define image export properties for the Save as Image functionality.
- The Environment Option dialog. To open the dialog, in the main menu, select Options → Environment . Image export properties are located under the General group.

Note that the **Exported Image Size** setting is applied in generated reports only if you select **No resize** from the **Auto image size** drop down list located in the **Output Options** area of the **Report Wizard**.

#### Scaling Quality

Because the image tool provides several methods for scaling images, the quality of the scaled image depends on the algorithm that is used. To set the image scaling quality, $image.setScalingQuality() code must be inserted before scaling images. For example:

```text
$image.setScalingQuality(5)$image.scale(0.5)
```

The code above will set the image scaling quality to ‘highest’. The possible values are from 1 to 5, where 5 is ‘highest’ and 1 is ‘lowest’. The following table provides the description of each value.

| Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | lowest' – Use one-step nearest neighbor interpolation. |
| 2 | 'low' – Use one-step bi-cubic interpolation. |
| 3 | 'medium – Use multi-step, bi-linear interpolation. |
| 4 | 'high' – Use area average image scaling algorithm. |
| 5 | 'highest' – Use a lossless transformation when the template is ODF, DOCX, or RTF; otherwise, area average image scaling algorithm will be used instead. |

Sample pictures from different image scaling qualities with their sizes scaled down to 50% are indicated in the pictures below (ranging from the value 1 ‘lowest’ to 5 ‘highest’):

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Different results of using the image set scaling quality function.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
Different results of using the image set scaling quality function.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

For example, Method 1 provides height and width parameters (scaleWidth and scaleHeight), while Method 2 provides a single parameter that will scale both axes equally (scaleFactor). These three parameters must be positive real numbers.</p><h4><strong>$image.scale(image, scaleWidth, scaleHeight)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object <br />for the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>scaleWidth</td><td>java.lang.Double </td><td>The width parameter <br />of the image. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>scaleHeight</td><td>java.lang.Double </td><td>The height parameter <br />of the image. </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td>- </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>The resized image <br />object for the element icon. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$image.scale(image, scaleFactor)</strong></h4><p>Return an image icon for an element.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object for <br />the element icon. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>scaleFactor</td><td> java.lang.Double</td><td>The scaling parameter <br />of the image.</td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td>- </td><td> com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>The resized image <br />object for the element icon. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>Use <strong>$image.scale($diagram.image, 0.5)</strong>, for example, to scale down the image to half the original size. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="1.1.scale_0.5.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Scaling images" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Results of using the image scale function.</h6><p>Use <strong>$image.scale($diagram.image, 0.8, 0.6) </strong>to scale the image’s width down to 80% and height to 60%. The following photos show the result.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1.2.scale_0.8x0.6.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Scaling images" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Results of using the image scale function.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c4f99e8-9980-4424-a00b-a84ad480004a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Alternative scaling methods</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The images used in generated reports can also be scaled by specifying the <strong>Exported Image Size</strong> property in one of the following locations:</p><ul><li>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, which is used to define image export properties for the <strong>Save as Image</strong> functionality.</li><li>The <strong>Environment Option</strong> dialog. To open the dialog, in the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> → <strong>Environment</strong>. Image export properties are located under the <strong>General</strong> group.</li></ul><p>Note that the <strong>Exported Image Size</strong> setting is applied in generated reports only if you select <strong>No resize</strong> from the <strong>Auto image size</strong> drop down list located in the <strong>Output Options</strong> area of the <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><br />Scaling Quality</h2><p>Because the image tool provides several methods for scaling images, the quality of the scaled image depends on the algorithm that is used. To set the image scaling quality, $image.setScalingQuality() code must be inserted before scaling images. For example:</p><pre>$image.setScalingQuality(5)<br />$image.scale(0.5)</pre><p>The code above will set the image scaling quality to ‘highest’. The possible values are from 1 to 5, where 5 is ‘highest’ and 1 is ‘lowest’. The following table provides the description of each value.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Value </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>1 </td><td>lowest' – Use one-step nearest <br />neighbor interpolation. </td></tr><tr><td>2 </td><td>'low' – Use one-step <br />bi-cubic interpolation.</td></tr><tr><td>3 </td><td>'medium – Use multi-step, <br />bi-linear interpolation. </td></tr><tr><td>4 </td><td>'high' – Use area average <br />image scaling algorithm. </td></tr><tr><td>5 </td><td>'highest' – Use a lossless transformation <br />when the template is ODF, DOCX, or RTF; <br />otherwise, area average image scaling <br />algorithm will be used instead.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>Sample pictures from different image scaling qualities with their sizes scaled down to 50% are indicated in the pictures below (ranging from the value 1 ‘lowest’ to 5 ‘highest’):</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ScaleQuality1-3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Scaling images" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Different results of using the image set scaling quality function.</h6><p><br /><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ScaleQuality4-5.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Scaling images" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br />Different results of using the image set scaling quality function.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679541 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00994: Scripting languages performance

- page_id: `169679541`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679541/Scripting+languages+performance
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-05T13:05:36.730+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Macro Engine Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When choosing a scripting language, we advise paying attention to its performance. For this, we have prepared performance measurements for built-in scripting languages.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The measurements are based on comparing the execution time of a script written in different languages and executed for 5000 elements. The example script is designed to retrieve a stereotype tag value and compare it to a string. All scripting languages execute the same logic using a different syntax for each scripting language. See the script example written in Groovy language below:

```groovy
0 && value[0] == "tagValue"]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">When choosing a scripting language, we advise paying attention to its performance. For this, we have prepared performance measurements for built-in scripting languages.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Scripting_languages_performance.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Scripting languages performance" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The measurements are based on comparing the execution time of a script written in different languages and executed for 5000 elements. The example script is designed to retrieve a stereotype tag value and compare it to a string. All scripting languages execute the same logic using a different syntax for each scripting language. See the script example written in Groovy language below: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a13b45f-048d-4ec4-bf74-dd7a8a92ae0c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">groovy</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Groovy</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//retrieve a value from a stereotype tag and compare it with the string "tagValue" 
def value = com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.StereotypesHelper.getStereotypePropertyValue(element, "stereotypeName", "propertyTagName") 
value.size() > 0 && value[0] == "tagValue"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673302 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00995: Search Results tab

- page_id: `169673302`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673302/Search+Results+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-04-22T07:37:07.012+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

635526173

635526175

635526174

INLINE

The Search Results tab is used to display search results. This tab opens only after [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Find dialog' space='MD2024xR1'] by pressing Ctrl+Shift+F, or by clicking **Edit** > **Find**.

The tab is not displayed when performing a **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quick Find dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Search Results** tab consists of two components:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Search Results tab' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Search Results tab' space='MD2024xR1']

##### #searchresulttoolbarSearch Results tab toolbar

The Search Results tab toolbars contains the following buttons:

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all of the current elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Find... | Opens the Find dialog. |
|  | Clear Results | Clears the results of the previous search. |
|  | Rerun Search | Reruns a search with the already specified search criteria. Search results are updated according to changes done in the model. |
|  | Save Search Results | Saves as a smart package either the search options or the search results. |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favoritesNavigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Options | Click to open the Options menu and then select:Show Applied Stereotypes - shows or hides the applied stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree.Show Full Types - shows or hides the full types of typed elements (for example, operations, attributes, relationships, and other) in the Containment tree. |

The same actions you may perform using the shortcut menu of the Search Results tree. For more information about commands available from the shortcut menu in the Model Browser, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements in the Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'].

##### #searchresulttreeWorking with model elements in the Search Results tree

The search results in the **Search Results** tree are displayed in the following two packages:

- Found in loaded diagrams – symbols that have been found in the diagram(s) are displayed in this package.
- Found in model – elements that have been found in the model data are displayed in this package.

635526171

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673579 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00996: Searching

- page_id: `169673579`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673579/Searching
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:38:02.561+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

468780347

468780350

468780349

INLINE

Searching the modeling tool is designed to search within model elements, as well as element properties. You can also perform a quick search without defining any advanced options, or you can replace any text quickly using the **Find and Replace** type of search.

You can read more about types of search dialogs in the related pages.

The search is performed as a background task; while searching, you may still read the model and browse it. If you make changes in the model, the search stops and presents incomplete search results in the **Search Results** tab of the **Model Browser**. You need to restart the search to get the full list of results.

If there is a large number of search results to display (more than 1000), the following message appears:

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Click **Yes** to continue searching, or click **No** to stop searching and correct the query.

If the indexing of model elements is in progress, the following **Question** dialog appears before performing a search in the **Find** and**Find and Replace** dialogs:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Click**Yes** to perform a search while indexing is still in progress, or click**No** to stop searching and wait for the indexing operation to complete in order to prevent slow searching.

468780346

**Related pages**

You can also perform a quick search without defining any advanced options, or you can replace any text quickly using the <strong>Find and Replace</strong> type of search.</p><p>You can read more about types of search dialogs in the related pages.</p><p>The search is performed as a background task; while searching, you may still read the model and browse it. If you make changes in the model, the search stops and presents incomplete search results in the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab of the <strong>Model Browser</strong>. You need to restart the search to get the full list of results.</p><p>If there is a large number of search results to display (more than 1000), the following message appears:</p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Many results found message" ac:alt="Many results found message"><ri:attachment ri:filename="many_results_found_message.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Searching" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Yes</strong> to continue searching, or click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">No</strong> to stop searching and correct the query.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p>If the indexing of model elements is in progress, the following <strong>Question</strong> dialog appears before performing a search in the <strong>Find</strong> and<strong> Find and Replace</strong> dialogs:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="question_dialog_when_searching.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Searching" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: left;">Click<strong> Yes</strong> to perform a search while indexing is still in progress, or click<strong> No</strong> to stop searching and wait for the indexing operation to complete in order to prevent slow searching.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34f2d1df-33c6-47f6-a220-dded2080479b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">468780346</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="5917640c-094e-4012-864f-2b7f7b4e8686" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673624 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00997: Searching in diagrams

- page_id: `169673624`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673624/Searching+in+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:11:35.771+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Searching
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Searching tips

586610874

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Searching tips

586610876

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Searching tips

586610875

The **Find in Diagram** command enables you to search for textual information in all types of diagrams, including tables, matrices, and maps. Use this command to find a specific text phrase in a diagram and navigate through the highlighted search results.

To find text in a diagram

1. Open the diagram in which you want to perform the search.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='find_in_diagram_button.png'] in the diagram toolbar.
  - On the main menu, click Edit > Find in Diagram .
  - Press Ctrl+F.
3. In the search box , type the text you want to find.
4. Use [ATTACHMENT filename='find_previous.png'] (Shift+F3) / [ATTACHMENT filename='find_next.png'] (F3) to navigate through highlighted search results.

#### TIP: Searching tips

Searching tips

- You can search in several open diagrams at once. Typing a search phrase in a subsequent diagram does not undo the search results in a previous diagram.
- When you press Ctrl+F with multiple open diagrams, the search starts in an active diagram.
- To search for textual information in the whole model, click [ATTACHMENT filename='find_button.png'] in the diagram search bar and specify the search criteria in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Find dialog' space='MD2024xR1'] .

After typing a search phrase, the search results are highlighted in yellow and the diagram or table scrolls to the first search result (highlighted in orange), as shown below. Diagram symbols or table rows that do not contain your search phrase are shaded. A diagram symbol is also shaded if the search phrase is found in the symbol compartment.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### How to use the Find feature in diagrams and navigate the search results.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3787c8e2-ff1c-4b0c-bb3e-7957de168ea7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">586610874</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1660885-fdde-4a18-a029-4279e16b1883"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">586610876</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="415c1c49-17d3-48be-aad1-3b0a6b9b8f19"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">586610875</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Find in Diagram</strong> command enables you to search for textual <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="99b2bb6f-38e8-4803-9346-c998b2e3d20a">information</ac:inline-comment-marker> in all types of diagrams, including tables, matrices, and maps. Use this command to <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="82535d25-0915-44c2-a949-d768ef89f9b5">find a specific text phrase in a diagram and navigate through the highlighted search results</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To find text in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the diagram in which you want to perform the search.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_in_diagram_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Searching in diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the diagram toolbar.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; Typing a search phrase in a subsequent diagram does not undo the search results in a previous diagram.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="327fddd8-d31d-4385-9099-6e7c1a4d64c7">When you </ac:inline-comment-marker>press Ctrl+F with multiple open diagrams, the search starts in an active diagram.</li><li>To search for textual information in the whole model, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Searching in diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the diagram search bar and specify the search criteria in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Find dialog" /></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>After typing a search phrase, the search results are highlighted in yellow and the diagram or table scrolls to the first search result (highlighted in orange), as shown below. Diagram symbols or table rows that do not contain your search phrase are shaded. A diagram symbol is also shaded if the search phrase is found in the symbol compartment.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_in_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Searching in diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">How to use the Find feature in diagrams and navigate the search results.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678050 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00998: Select Element Scope pane

- page_id: `169678050`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678050/Select+Element+Scope+pane
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T15:41:15.938+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Wizard dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Select Element Scope** pane (see the figure below) allows you to select the scope of the modeling tool's data to generate a report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Select Element Scope pane.

The table below describes the detail of each component in the **Select Element Scope** pane.

| Component | Name description |
| --- | --- |
| Select Element Scope pane | A group for selecting an element scope. |
| Search for scope | Select Package for listing only Package elements in the tree. Select Any Element for listing any element in the tree. |
| Apply Filter option | Select to apply the filter to the tree. |
| Filter button | Select the filter options: Hide Uncommon Elements and Exclude Auxiliary Resources. |
|  | Add the selected element in the tree. |
|  | Recursively add the selected elements in the tree. |
|  | Remove the selected scope element from the list. |
|  | Remove all scope elements from the list. |
| Selected elements pane | List added element(s) in the tree. |
| Selected Scope Options | Generate Recursively - Recursively generate elements from the selected scope elements.Include Auxiliary Resources - Include auxiliary resources found in the selected scope elements. |

You can perform the following operations in the **Select Element Scope** pane:

- Add packages into the Selected elements list.
- Remove packages from the Selected elements list.
- Select or clear the Generate Recursively option.
- Include auxiliary resources.
- Show and search for package elements.

To add packages

- 
  - In the Select Element Scope pane (see the figure above), select the packages from the tree and click Add [ATTACHMENT filename='Add Button.png'] or Add Recursively [ATTACHMENT filename='Add Recursive Button.png'] to add them into the Selected elements list.

To remove packages

- 
  - In the Select Element Scope pane (see figure above), select the packages from the tree and click Remove [ATTACHMENT filename='Remove Selected Scope Button.png'] or Remove All [ATTACHMENT filename='Remove All Scopes Button.png'] to remove them from the Selected elements list.

To select or clear the **Generate Recursively** option

- 
  - Select or clear the Generate Recursively check box (see the figure above).

To include auxiliary resources

- 
  - Select the Include Auxiliary Resources check box and add auxiliary resources into the Selected elements list (see the figure above).

To show and search for package elements

- 
  - In the Search for scope, select the Package option and enter the search keyword in the search field (see the figure above).

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

The figure above shows the **UML 2 Elements** package and the **Generate Recursively** option are selected. It means that the **UML 2 Elements** package and its subpackages will be generated in the report.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673357 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00999: Select Perspective dialog

- page_id: `169673357`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673357/Select+Perspective+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-04-22T08:47:09.997+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you launch a modeling tool for the first time, the **Select Perspective**dialog appears with the ability to switch between perspectives.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Element Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Full FeaturedQuick StartSoftware ArchitectSystem Analyst | List of the available perspectives with the predefined configuration. |
| Expert | Choose Expert mode only if you are an advanced user. All features and commands will be loaded. If you are a new user, you should not select Expert mode. The non-expert mode only loads the basic and most important commands and features. |
| Customize | Open the Customize Perspectives dialog to customize the selected perspective. You can make your tool less complicated by hiding irrelevant features or commands. For example, you can group functional features, show, or hide selected commands. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679545 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01000: Selecting a default macro language

- page_id: `169679545`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679545/Selecting+a+default+macro+language
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-06-21T10:28:47.510+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Macro Engine Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the**Environment Options** dialog to select a default macro language.

To select a default macro language

1. Click Options > Environment on the main menu. The Environment Options dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='tools-options.png']
2. Select the Macros node. [ATTACHMENT filename='macro-env-options.png']
3. Click the box next to the Default Macro Language box to see a list of supported programming languages. [ATTACHMENT filename='macro-env-options-b.png']
4. Select the preferred language.
5. Click OK to save the selected language as the default macro language.

- Groovy is the default macro language. To ensure the best performance experience, it is strongly suggested that you use the recommended language.
- Macro Engine currently supports BeanShell, Groovy, JavaScript Rhino, and Jython only.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Use the<strong> Environment Options</strong> dialog to select a default macro language.</p><p><br /></p><p>To select a default macro language</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong> on the main menu. The <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog opens.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Environment Options Menu" ac:alt="Environment Options Menu"><ri:attachment ri:filename="tools-options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting a default macro language" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select the <strong>Macros</strong> node.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The Environment Options Dialog" ac:alt="The Environment Options Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="macro-env-options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting a default macro language" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click the box next to the <strong>Default Macro Language</strong> box to see a list of supported programming languages.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Selecting Macro Language" ac:alt="Selecting Macro Language"><ri:attachment ri:filename="macro-env-options-b.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting a default macro language" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select the preferred language.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to save the selected language as the default macro language.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eaf72942-6f5c-4c81-ad0f-fd5db9e7e781"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Groovy is the default macro language. To ensure the best performance experience, it is strongly suggested that you use the recommended language.</li><li>Macro Engine currently supports BeanShell, Groovy, JavaScript Rhino, and Jython only.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674461 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01002: Selecting columns to display

- page_id: `169674461`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674461/Selecting+columns+to+display
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-01-25T11:02:01.672+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Instance table
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

By default, the Instance Table shows nine columns (including # (row number), Name (instance name), and the columns for instance slot values) that correspond to the first seven Data type attributes of the specified classifier. The rest of the columns are by default hidden, though you can show them if there is a need.

You can show any column that corresponds to one of the following:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting columns to display' space='MD2024xR1'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting columns to display' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting columns to display' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting columns to display' space='MD2024xR1']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

display_attributesTo display attributes of the classifier

1. On the table toolbar, click the Columns button and then click to select the check boxes beside the attributes you want to show. [ATTACHMENT filename='instance_table_columns.png']

display_properties_tagsTo display properties and tags

1. On the table toolbar, click **Columns** > **Select Columns** >**Metaproperties** tab. In the **Select Columns** dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the Instance Table by selecting the appropriate check boxes of the properties and tags. All columns that you have chosen to display also appears on the menu of the **Columns** button.

nested_propertiesTo display nested properties of the classifier

1. On the table toolbar, click **Columns** > **Select Columns** > **Properties** tab. In the **Select Columns** dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the Instance Table by selecting the appropriate check boxes of the nested properties. To specify nested columns, click the **Create with Parts** button, when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting columns to display' space='MD2024xR1']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you add a new attribute to the classifier and need to display the corresponding column in the Instance Table, open the**Select Columns** dialog and click to select the appropriate check box.

Keep in mind that columns corresponding to newly created attributes at first are available only in the **Select Columns** dialog (until you show them).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673941 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01003: Selecting criteria

- page_id: `169673941`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673941/Selecting+criteria
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-08-23T14:50:49.133+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Using Dependency Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308994112

308994115

308994111

**On this page**

308994113

To create a Dependency Matrix, first of all, you need to define which data you wish to display on its pane. Select row and column element types, row and column scope, the direction of the relationships as well as the dependency criteria for this. Be advised that the easiest way to define all the above-mentioned criteria, except the type of the relationship direction, is using the drag-and-drop operation.

A lot of properties of the Dependency Matrix can be specified in thematrix [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'].

You can open the matrix [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] in one of the following ways:

- From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix shortcut menu' space='MD2024xR1']
- From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix toolbar' space='MD2024xR1']
- Press Enter in the active matrix.

##### Specifying element type

To specify a row/column element type

Do either:

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'] , select one or more elements which types you wish to see on your matrix and drag them to the Row Element Type / Column Element Type box in the Criteria area.

- For the multiple selection Press and hold Shift or Ctrl.
- If you want to add additional elements, press and hold Ctrl while dragging the elements to the **Row Element Type**/**Column Element Type** box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button next to the **Row Element Type**/**Column Element Type** box and in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting element types' space='MD2024xR1']**Select Element Type** dialog select what element types you wish to see on your matrix. Click **OK**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Select the**Include subtypes**check box to display the subtypes of the selected element types.Select the**Include Custom Types**check box to display the custom types extending the selected element types.

- Open the matrix [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] , click the Row Element Type / Column Element Type property value cell, then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] button, and in the opened dialog select what element types you wish to see on your matrix. Click Close .

##### Dependency Criteria dialogSpecifying dependency criterion

To specify which relationships you want to display in the matrix

1. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] button next to Dependency Criteria .
2. Select the relationships you want to display. [ATTACHMENT filename='dependency_criteria_selecting_relationships.png']
3. In the Properties column, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] button to select the properties of the relationship. When you're done, click Close . [ATTACHMENT filename='relationship_properties_updated.png']
  - Include Relationship Subtypes - select to include the subtypes of the selected relationship types.
  - Include Relationship Custom Types - select to include the custom relationship types that extend the selected relationship types.
  - Filter by Property Value - select properties to use as additional relationship filters.
  - **Include Aggregation and Composition** - select to include Aggregation and Composition relationships along with the Association relationship. The **Include Aggregation and Composition** option can be specified in the **Relationship Properties** dialog only if the Association or custom Association relationship is selected as a Relation Criterion.
4. In the Style column, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] button to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying relationship line styles' space='MD2024xR1'] .
5. When you're done, click Close , then OK .

##### Specifying relationship direction

To specify a relationship direction

Do either:

- Select a direction from the Direction drop-down list in the Criteria area. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_criteria_direction.png']
- Open the matrix [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] , click the Direction property value cell and select what direction relationships you wish to see on your matrix. Click Close .

##### Specifying what elements are visible in the matrix

To specify what elements you wish to see in the matrix: related, non-related, or all

Do either:

- From the Show Elements drop-down list in the Criteria area, select
  - With relations if you need to see only related elements from the selected scope.
  - Without relations if you need to see only non-related elements from the selected scope.
  - All if you need to see both related and non-related elements from the selected scope.
- Open the matrix [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'], click the **Show Elements** property value cell and choose one of the values described above. Click **Close**.

After the **Show Elements** value is changed, you should always refresh a matrix.

Once criteria are specified, you can create the matrix. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix toolbar' space='MD2024xR1'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When a Dependency Matrix contains a large number of rows and/or columns, it may become difficult to work with, as you need to scroll in order to see certain parts of it. In this case, you can select to exclude cells (either rows or columns) that contain relationships and make your Dependency Matrix view more compact.

To specify what cells to display on the matrix

1. From the Show Elements drop-down list in the Criteria area, select either [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - **Columns without relations** – to show columns without relationships and all rows.
  - **Rows without relations** – to show rows without relationships and all columns.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to refresh the matrix.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47a3e539-bb37-4951-abb0-b48d6694efd9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308994112</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e182127-85b7-47c6-b05c-a1548d961ee6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308994115</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="086143f4-3bdf-4d55-ab33-3a7b40c718c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308994111</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa5fc39f-c0bb-4855-a8ee-8b8028b42db7" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="750d4234-4f16-4e2b-bdf9-e4a50047d588"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308994113</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a Dependency Matrix, first of all, you need to define which data you wish to display on its pane. Select row and column element types, row and column scope, the direction of the relationships as well as the dependency criteria for this.
````
