<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676129 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01004: Selecting destination model

- page_id: `169676129`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676129/Selecting+destination+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-01-26T16:16:12.249+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Working with Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the third step of the wizard, you can choose the package that will contain the generated model. Additionally, you can specify the following options:

Select the **Place transformation model in package** option button to specify the package into which the source will be transformed. Select an existing package or create a new one.

Select the **Transform in place** option, if you want the source model to be edited.

- If you choose the in-place transformation, the model part selected as the transformation source, will be edited directly, and you will not retain your original model. So, please, be careful with this option.

- If you choose the destination package, the source model will be copied to it and the transformation will be performed on this copy. Hence you will retain your source model and get a resulting model and traces will be established between elements in these model parts.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following operations are available in the**Select source**and**Select destination**steps:

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Back | Return to the previous dialog box. |
| Next | Proceed to the next step (in this case, Select type mappings). |
| Finish | Finish the transformation configuration. All other options will be set by default.The Model Transformations Wizard exits and transformation results appear in the project. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the third step of the wizard, you can choose the package that will contain the generated model. Additionally, you can specify the following options:</p><p>Select the <strong>Place transformation model in package</strong> option button to specify the package into which the source will be transformed. Select an existing package or create a new one.</p><p>Select the <strong>Transform in place</strong> option, if you want the source model to be edited.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca4b2c3b-3966-408e-96e8-bfd1676a8374"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If you choose the in-place transformation, the model part selected as the transformation source, will be edited directly, and you will not retain your original model. So, please, be careful with this option.</li></ul><ul><li>If you choose the destination package, the source model will be copied to it and the transformation will be performed on this copy. Hence you will retain your source model and get a resulting model and traces will be established between elements in these model parts.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_transformation_wizard_3rd_step.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting destination model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a6fd8ece-725b-4aca-87a0-43d90ae2601a">The following operations are available in the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a6fd8ece-725b-4aca-87a0-43d90ae2601a">Select source </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a6fd8ece-725b-4aca-87a0-43d90ae2601a">and</ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a6fd8ece-725b-4aca-87a0-43d90ae2601a"> Select destination</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a6fd8ece-725b-4aca-87a0-43d90ae2601a"> steps:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Back</strong></td><td>Return to the previous dialog box.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Next</strong></td><td>Proceed to the next step (in this case, Select type mappings).</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Finish</strong></td><td><p>Finish the transformation configuration. All other options will be set by default.</p><p>The Model Transformations Wizard exits and transformation results appear in the project.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675966 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01005: Selecting element types

- page_id: `169675966`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675966/Selecting+element+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-08-02T17:19:31.221+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

804386951

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

804386954

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

804386952

In the **Select Element Type** dialog, you can select the check box next to the model element type to be filtered; i.e., displayed or searched for. This dialog opens when filtering items in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024xR1']**Containment** tab and when searching or filtering for elements, as outlined below:

- When [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Searching' space='MD2024xR1'] for items, the purpose of the Select Element Type dialog is to select the type of element to search.
- When [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering' space='MD2024xR1'] items in the Containment tab, the purpose of the Select Element Type dialog is to provide the ability to hide elements you do not want to see in the Containment tab.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Select Element Type dialog.

The following table explains the functions of the tabs, options, and buttons in the **Select Element Type** dialog.

| Button/Tab | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Search text box | Specifies a search term to filter the search results of element types in the List, Inheritance, and Structural tabs.NoteThe appearances of the search term in the search results are highlighted in yellow. |
| List | Click the List button to list the types of elements in one alphabetic list. There are two groups in the list: Element and Symbol. |
| Inheritance | Click the Inheritance button to group the types of elements according to the inheritance. For example, types of elements are grouped to the following inheritances: Named Element, Relations, and others. The Symbol group is listed also. |
| Structural | Click the Inheritance button to group the types of elements according to the following groups: DSL Customization, MagicDraw Profile, UML2 Metamodel, Symbol. |
| Select All | Selects all types of elements in the list – all elements will be displayed after filtering or searching. |
| Clear All | Clears all types of elements in the list – no elements will be displayed after filtering or searching. |
| Specifying optionsInclude Subtypes | Includes the subtypes of the selected element types. For example, if you select a Package, its subtypes (e.g., Model and Profile) will also be included. The option is enabled by default. |
| Include Custom Types | Includes the custom types extending the selected element types. For example, if you select a Package, the custom types (e.g., Smart Package) that extend the Package will also be included. The option is disabled by default. |
| OK | Applies the selected/cleared element types to display or hide them in the Containment tab when you have opened the dialog from the Containment tab.Applies the selected/cleared element types to filter and display them in the Search Results tab in the Model Browser when you have opened the dialog from the Find dialog. |
| Cancel | Cancels the selecting/clearing or filtering of the element types and closes the dialog. |
| Help | Opens the Selecting element types page with the description of the dialog. |

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Search Results tab' space='MD2024xR1']**Search Results** tab opens in the Model Browser to display the search results according to the search criteria you have created from selecting/clearing types of elements in the **Select Element Type** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Search Results tab in the Model Browser displays the search results.

804386950

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Search Results tab' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="baee907a-4d9e-442b-9331-1cb1a9a8fa6d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">804386951</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5397e2d1-093b-49af-b753-2bce450fa384"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">804386954</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="395d77ae-f020-460d-ad77-deb68aae92d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">804386952</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Select Element Type</strong> dialog, you can select the check box next to the model element type to be filtered; i.e., displayed or searched for. This dialog opens when filtering items in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:link-body><strong>Containment</strong> tab</ac:link-body></ac:link> and when searching or filtering for elements, as outlined below:</p><ul><li>When <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Searching" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[searching]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for items, the purpose of the <strong>Select Element Type</strong> dialog is to select the type of element to search.</li><li>When <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filtering]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> items in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab, the purpose of the <strong>Select Element Type</strong> dialog is to provide the ability to hide elements you do not want to see in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_types_dialog_new.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting element types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Select Element Type dialog.</h6><p>The following table explains the functions of the tabs, options, and buttons in the <strong>Select Element Type</strong> dialog.</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 99.8903%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 41.8132%;" /><col style="width: 58.1868%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button/Tab</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Search text box</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specifies a search term to filter the search results of element types in the <strong>List</strong>, <strong>Inheritance</strong>, and <strong>Structural</strong> tabs.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c4120dc-443e-49da-acfd-aff1c082fc14"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The appearances of the search term in the search results are highlighted in yellow.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>List</td><td><p><span>Click the </span><strong>List</strong><span> button to list the types of elements in </span>one alphabetic<span> list. There are two groups in the list: </span><em>Element</em><span> and </span><em>Symbol</em><span>.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>Inheritance</td><td><p><span>Click the </span><strong>Inheritance</strong><span> button to group the types </span>of elements<span> according to the inheritance. For example, </span><span>types of elements are grouped </span>to<span> the following inheritances: </span><em>Named Element</em><span>, </span><em>Relations</em><span>, and others. The </span><em>Symbol</em><span> group is listed also.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>Structural</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Click the </span><strong>Inheritance</strong><span> button to group the types of elements </span><span>according to the following groups: </span><em>DSL Customization</em><span>, </span><em>MagicDraw Profile</em><span>, </span><em>UML2 Metamodel</em><span>, </span><em>Symbol</em><span>.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>Select All</td><td>Selects all types of elements in the list – all elements will be displayed after filtering or searching.</td></tr><tr><td>Clear All</td><td>Clears all types of elements in the list – no elements will be displayed after filtering or searching.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4538af69-da70-4d51-8b7a-db031ecd9366"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Specifying options</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Include Subtypes</p></div></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Includes the subtypes of the selected element types. For example, if you select a Package, its subtypes (e.g., Model and Profile) will also be included. The option is enabled by default.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Include Custom Types</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p style="text-align: left;">Includes the custom types extending the selected element types. For example, if you select a Package, the custom types (e.g., Smart Package) that extend the Package will also be included. The option is disabled by default.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">OK</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><p style="text-align: left;">Applies the selected/cleared element types to display or hide them in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab when you have opened the dialog from the <strong>Containment</strong> tab.</p><p style="text-align: left;">Applies the selected/cleared element types to filter and display them in the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab in the Model Browser when you have opened the dialog from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Find dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Find</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Cancel</td><td colspan="1">Cancels the selecting/clearing or filtering of the element types and closes the dialog.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Help</td><td colspan="1">Opens the <strong>Selecting element types</strong> page with the description of the dialog.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Search Results tab" /><ac:link-body><strong>Search Results</strong> tab</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens in the Model Browser to display the search results according to the search criteria you have created from selecting/clearing types of elements in the <strong>Select Element Type</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Search Results Tab.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting element types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Search Results tab in the Model Browser displays the search results.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25b1d18a-ff6c-4570-97ac-56a9fa3bd144"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">804386950</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filtering" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Search Results tab" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675845 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01006: Selecting elements

- page_id: `169675845`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675845/Selecting+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:19.090+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Match Text Anywhere command

400254601

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Match Text Anywhere command

400254604

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Match Text Anywhere command

400254602

The element Selection dialog is used for selecting elements from available contents and adding them to a certain destination scope. The title of the dialog varies depending on the way it is invoked. Depending on the content, the element Selection dialog is used for selecting an element, diagram, or owner. The types of elements available for selection determine the presence of the specific element type filters in the dialog.

#### WARNING: Teamwork Cloud projects

Teamwork Cloud projects

The following message indicates that the representation text indexing is not complete. It is not recommended that you perform element searches while the indexing is in progress. Otherwise, depending on the project size, the search operation might be slow, and the memory consumption might increase.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The element Selection dialog also opens when assigning a property value - the particular element or elements. For example, the element Selection dialog opens when assigning the base classifier in the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'].

In the element Selection dialog, you can perform the following actions:

- Select one or more elements to assign them as property values.
- Search for an element.
- Create elements.
- Clone a selected element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Select Element dialog.

The table below describes the components of the **Select Element** dialog.

| Dialog element name/image | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Search for:Type and PackagePropertyAny Element | Select one of the predefined element type filters to narrow down the search results:Type and Package - select to search for types and packages only.Property - select to search for properties only.Any Element - select to search among all the elements in the model. |
|  | Click to customize the search options to optimize the search operation.Match Text AnywhereTo search for a matching phrase in any part of the element name, click the command to add the check mark.To explicitly search from the beginning of the element name, click the command to remove the check mark.Use Camel Case To search for a matching phrase typed in a camel case, click the command to add the check mark.To search for a matching phrase typed in a regular case, click the command to remove the check mark.You can use camel case as follows:Type only the first letters (either capitalized or not) of each word, for example, “and” or “AND” to find “Analysis and Design”.Type skipping spacers, for example, “cv3” to find “CV-3”.Perform a partial search, for example, “ibd” to find SysML Internal Block diagram. Be sure the Match Text Anywhere command is checked! |
| More | Click the button to load the next 100 matches. By default, the first 100 matches are shown in the dialog. |
| PreviousNext | Available in the Tree view only.Click to navigate to a previous or next result.Alternatively, you can press the Shift + F3 keyboard shortcut to select the previous match or press F3 to select the next match. |
| Switch to Tree | Click to switch from the List to Tree view.Alternatively, press the Alt+B keyboard shortcut. |
| Apply Filter(Ctrl+Space) | applyfilterClick to clear the check box if you need to remove the search scope filter. If you clear the Apply Filter check box once, your selection is remembered for the next time. |
| Filter Options | Select to customize the filter options to narrow down the search scope. - reduces the search results list by hiding uncommon elements, keeping elements such as Interface, Association Class, Class, and Component in the list.Exclude Auxiliary Resources - reduces the search results list by excluding elements marked as auxiliary. By default, elements in the standard/system profiles are marked as auxiliary. This option does not hide the following:UML datatypes (placed under MagicDraw Profile) and UML2 Metamodel (placed under UML Standard profile) only for UML project.UML2 Metamodel, SysML value types, and stereotypes for SysML project.Elements with <<ignoreAuxFilter>> stereotype applied and all its elements recursively. How to apply stereotype >> - reduces the search results list by showing elements from the imported packages only.Available for the usage element only, i.e., when selecting the type/behavior for properties/actions. |
| Creation Mode | Click to turn on the element creation mode. |
| Create | Click to create a new element. |
| Clone | Click to clone the selected element. |
| Single Selection/Multiple Selection | Click to expand or collapse to select a single element or multiple elements. |

When searching for an element, type the search phrase in the Search By Name box. The following table describes the search types that can be used.

| Search Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Simple search in theList and Tree views | In the Search by Name box, type a phrase to search for element names. Elements matching the search phrase will be displayed in the List or Tree view.When switching between element selection views, the selected elements are remembered. That is, if the element is selected in the Tree view, it will be selected in the List view as well. |
| Search using wild cards | In the Search by Name box, type a phrase using the following wildcard characters:“*” substitutes any range of characters. So if your search phrase is “*dd”, all the elements containing “dd” in their names can be found. For example, “Address” and “ThreadDeath”.“?” substitutes exactly one character. So if your search phrase is “?dd”, only the elements with “dd” in their names starting from the second character can be found. For example, “Address”. “ThreadDeath” cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase “dd”.Match Text Anywhere commandBe sure the Match Text Anywhere command is unchecked, when using wildcard characters! Click to find the command. |

**700500**

400254599

**Related pages**

ac:macro-id="7da234ca-0116-465c-b239-d5a01e80361d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available in the <strong>Tree</strong> view only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p stereotype applied and all its elements recursively. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype" rel="nofollow">How to apply stereotype &gt;&gt;</a></li></ol></li></ol></li></ul><ul><li><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Filter By Package Imports filter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Filter by Package Imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span></strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- r<span>educes the search results list by showing elements from the imported packages only.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="61c45734-7f36-4739-b7f7-f971f53580a6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Available for the usage element only, i.e., when selecting the type/behavior for properties/actions.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Creation Mode</strong></p></div></td><td colspan="1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="61db4b5c-e7b3-48a1-940e-21c2bb3c7d62">Click to turn on the element creation mode.</ac:inline-comment-marker></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Create </strong></td><td colspan="1">Click to create a new element.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Clone</strong></td><td colspan="1">Click to clone the selected element.</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="9"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Single Selection/Multiple Selection</strong></p></div></td><td rowspan="9"><p>Click to expand or collapse to select a single element or multiple elements.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br />When searching for an element, type the search phrase in the Search By Name box. The following table describes the search types that can be used.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 13.0077%;" /><col style="width: 86.9923%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Search Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Simple search in the</strong></p><strong>List and Tree views</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>In the <strong>Search by Name </strong>box, type a phrase to search for element names. Elements matching the search phrase will be displayed in the <strong>List </strong>or <strong>Tree </strong>view.</p><p>When switching between element selection views, the selected elements are remembered. That is, if the element is selected in the <strong>Tree </strong>view, it will be selected in the <strong>List </strong>view as well.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Search using wild cards</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>In the <strong>Search by Name </strong>box, type a phrase using the following wildcard characters:</p><ul><li>“*” substitutes any range of characters. So if your search phrase is “*dd”, all the elements containing “dd” in their names can be found. For example, “A<u>dd</u>ress” and “Threa<u>dD</u>eath”.</li><li>“?” substitutes exactly one character. So if your search phrase is “?dd”, only the elements with “dd” in their names starting from the second character can be found. For example, “A<u>dd</u>ress”. “Threa<u>dD</u>eath” cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase “dd”.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4da044d-4ce8-48ee-bc96-b8d67c2baa2d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Match Text Anywhere command</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Be sure the <strong>Match Text Anywhere </strong>command is unchecked, when using wildcard characters! Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="search_for_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to find the command.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="text-align: center;"><br /> <strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3985e526-3b39-4777-8129-536337758ee3"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LowZFZNtul8" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">500</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eee5d82d-a82b-47cf-a81b-8b66c5085bdd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">400254599</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="5ceeea2d-7922-4e8e-8196-117bbcd5576d" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679159 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01007: Selecting more than one drag-and-drop rule

- page_id: `169679159`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679159/Selecting+more+than+one+drag-and-drop+rule
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-01-27T09:09:49.932+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data > Customizing Drag-and-Drop
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

1254667587

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

1254667598

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

1254667597

In some cases, more than one rule can be applied to a drag-and-drop operation. This enables you to select what to create.

To select a rule

1. Do one of the following:
  - Drag a selected element to another, hold it for a few seconds till the tool-tip opens, and then release. A shortcut menu is displayed.
  - Right-click an element, drag it onto another element, and then drop it. A shortcut menu is displayed. ( Note: this procedure is valid on Windows OS only).
2. On the shortcut menu, click the rule to apply. The rule is applied. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_and_drop8.png']

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

Our modeling tools have a number of predefined drag-and-drop rules.

1254667586

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Numbering Customizations' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This enables you to select what to create.</span></p><p><span> <br /></span></p><p>To select a rule</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Drag a selected element to another, hold it for a few seconds till the tool-tip opens, and then release. A shortcut menu is displayed.</li><li>Right-click an element, drag it onto another element, and then drop it. A shortcut menu is displayed. (<strong>Note:</strong> this procedure is valid on Windows OS only).</li></ul></li><li>On the shortcut menu, click the rule to apply. The rule is applied.<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_and_drop8.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting more than one drag-and-drop rule" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57250a5c-3cda-460e-8f74-cccc44bf6821"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Our modeling tools have a number of predefined drag-and-drop rules. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9dc03ebc-b47b-404a-ae00-61b574ff939f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1254667586</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Customization Data" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using Customization Data" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Numbering Customizations" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675670 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01008: Selecting multiple property values

- page_id: `169675670`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675670/Selecting+multiple+property+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-10-23T14:08:15.976+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Selecting values
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this case you can assign more than one value to a property in the**Select Element**dialog.Examples of properties that may have multiple values: *Classifier*, *Method*.

To assign multiple property values

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Edit button (see the highlighted button in the following figure). The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024xR1']** dialog opens.
3. Select existing and / or create elements.
4. Click **OK**when you are done.

Make sure the Multiple Selection mode is turned on.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675880 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01009: Selecting port and part types

- page_id: `169675880`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675880/Selecting+port+and+part+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T10:32:07.831+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1822737895

1822737899

1822737898

INLINE

The **Type Selection Mode** turns on the automatic port and part type selection. When the function is turned on:

- On a part creation, the Select Type dialog appears. The same is valid when creating ports. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_type_on_part_creation.png']
- If a part does not have a specified type, then a new type for the part will be created on a nested port creation. A type for the new port will be created, too.
- If a part does not have a name, type the name on the part shape on the diagram and the type will be created automatically. The typed name will be the name of the part type (NOTE: that part will be without a name).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To turn on the **Type Selection Mode**

- On the Composite Structure, Class, Component, or Package diagrams [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR1'], click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The**Type Selection Mode**works separately for each project.

You can save the time spent looking for elements in the type selection list by using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package import' space='MD2024xR1'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f4c76bc-54ba-47fe-8e05-1a3e1a2d5da9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1822737895</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3807e4c0-665f-4d84-918e-b61acd664b5e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1822737899</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa0ea0d8-213b-42ae-a707-43fce7b57308"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1822737898</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ed3c4c4-4771-4351-8493-7076df38404c"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Type Selection Mode</strong> turns on the automatic port and part type selection. When the function is turned on:</p><ul><li>On a part creation, the <strong>Select Type </strong>dialog appears. The same is valid when creating ports.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_type_on_part_creation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting port and part types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>If a part does not have a specified type, then a new type for the part will be created on a nested port creation. A type for the new port will be created, too.</p></li><li><p>If a part does not have a name, type the name on the part shape on the diagram and the type will be created automatically. The typed name will be the name of the part type (NOTE: that part will be without a name).</p></li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="typing_part_name_with_type_selection_mode_on.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting port and part types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To turn on the <strong>Type Selection Mode</strong></p><hr /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the Composite Structure, Class, Component, or Package diagrams <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:title="Type Selection Mode" ac:alt="Type Selection Mode"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specify_type.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting port and part types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> .</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a792f8e-361c-4f23-8d0f-6accbb0243b6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span><span class="confluence-link">The<strong> Type Selection Mode </strong>works separately for each project.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62b3dd84-8460-4e39-88bf-a7f534c7c7cf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can save the time spent looking for elements in the type selection list by using <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Package import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[package imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675844 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01010: Selecting referenced element in Containment tree

- page_id: `169675844`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675844/Selecting+referenced+element+in+Containment+tree
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:18.855+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can select a referenced element in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='SYSMLP2024xR1'] directly from the hyperlinked text or element.

To select a referenced element in the Containment tree

1. Select the hyperlinked element on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR1'] or in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'] , or hower your mouse pointer at the hyperlinked text.
2. Do one of the following: - Press Alt and double click the hyperlinked text or element. - Press Ctrl+Alt and double click the hyperlinked text or element. The referenced element is selected in the Containment tree.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>You can select a referenced element in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> directly from the hyperlinked text or element.</p><p>To select a referenced element in the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">Select the hyperlinked element on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>, or hower your mouse pointer at the hyperlinked text.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target">Do one of the following:<br /> - Press Alt and double click the hyperlinked text or element.<br /> - Press Ctrl+Alt and double click the hyperlinked text or element.<br />The referenced element is selected in the Containment tree.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675664 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01011: Selecting single property value from editable list

- page_id: `169675664`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675664/Selecting+single+property+value+from+editable+list
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-10-18T19:00:01.048+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Selecting values
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This is the case of assigning a value to a property in one of the following ways:

- Selecting a value either from a predefined value (auto-completion) list or from the whole model in the **Select Element** dialog.
- Creating an element and selecting it as property value.

To select a property value from a predefined (auto-completion) list

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the arrow (see the highlighted button in the following figure).
3. In the drop-down list, select a value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To assign a property value in the **Select Element** dialog

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Edit button (see the highlighted button in the figure below). The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024xR1']******dialog opens.
3. Select an existing or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new elements' space='MD2024xR1'] an element.
4. Click **OK**when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To remove a value selected for a single value type property

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. In the drop-down list, select *<UNSPECIFIED>*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675661 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01012: Selecting single property value from non-editable list

- page_id: `169675661`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675661/Selecting+single+property+value+from+non-editable+list
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-10-19T19:30:17.148+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Selecting values
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this case you can choose one of the predefined values. 
To select a property value from the non-editable predefined list

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. In the drop-down list, select the value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To remove a property value selected from the non-editable predefined list

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. In the drop-down list, select *<UNSPECIFIED>*.

The property value can be removed only for those properties, that can have an empty value.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676126 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01013: Selecting source model

- page_id: `169676126`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676126/Selecting+source+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-01-20T11:18:08.029+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Working with Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the second step of the wizard, the Transformation Source tree displays all project data, i.e., the packages and their inner elements, that can be selected as a transformation source. Transformation will take the selected elements as input data.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the second step of the wizard, the Transformation Source tree displays all project data, i.e., the packages and their inner elements, that can be selected as a transformation source. Transformation will take the selected elements as input data.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_transformation_wizard_2nd_step.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting source model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676123 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01014: Selecting transformation type

- page_id: `169676123`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676123/Selecting+transformation+type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-01-26T13:19:39.198+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Working with Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the first step of the Model Transformation Wizard, a list of the available transformation types is displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Transformation types are displayed in the list window.

The following operations are available in the Select transformation type window:

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Next | Proceed to the next step (in this case, Select source). |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the first step of the Model Transformation Wizard, a list of the available transformation types is displayed.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_transformation_wizard_1st_step.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting transformation type" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Transformation types are displayed in the list window.</p><p>The following operations are available in the  Select transformation type window:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Next</strong></td><td> Proceed to the next step (in this case, Select source).</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676131 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01015: Selecting type mappings

- page_id: `169676131`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676131/Selecting+type+mappings
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:47:40.231+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Working with Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The fourth step in the wizard allows for selecting a type map that will be applied during the transformation. Usually transformation has and brings in some predefined type map, but if you want, you can specify another type map.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

A type map can be regarded as a collection of rules of the form “Replace the usage of type X in the used project with the usage of type Y”.

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transformation Type Mapping' space='MD2024xR1'] is a model object, i.e. a package with a collection of dependencies, hence all model manipulation operations can be performed on it. In particular, it can be refactored into a used project and mounted into any project, which needs it. It can be a simple package in your project as well, if you need a custom, one-off type map. A predefined type map can be taken from the modeling tool module and edited.

To see a list of the type maps available in your model, click the down arrow in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transformation Type Mapping' space='MD2024xR1']** combo box. These type maps specify the mapping rules that will be applied to the model during the transformation.

When you select a particular map, its contents are displayed in a table below. Each row in the table is a rule to remap one particular type to another. The **From type** and **To type** columns in the table show the source and target types.

The **Run type mapping in reverse order** check box creates the opposite type mapping. Type maps can be bidirectional, e.g., the same type map is reused both in the UML to XML schema and XML schema to UML transformations. This checkbox governs the direction in which the type map should be used.

The following operations are available in the **Select type mapping** window:

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Back | Return to the previous dialog box. |
| Next | Proceed to the next step (in this case, Specify transformation details). NOTE: This button is disabled during the Any to Any transformations. |
| Finish | Finish the configuration of the transformation. The Model Transformations Wizard exits and the transformation results appear in the project. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The fourth step in the wizard allows for selecting a type map that will be applied during the transformation. Usually transformation has and brings in some predefined type map, but if you want, you can specify another type map.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_transformation_wizard_4th_step.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting type mappings" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>A type map can be regarded as a collection of rules of the form “Replace the usage of type X in the used project with the usage of type Y”.</p><p>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Transformation Type Mapping" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[type map]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is a model object, i.e. a package with a collection of dependencies, hence all model manipulation operations can be performed on it. In particular, it can be refactored into a used project and mounted into any project, which needs it. It can be a simple package in your project as well, if you need a custom, one-off type map. A predefined type map can be taken from the modeling tool module and edited.</p><p>To see a list of the type maps available in your model, click the down arrow in the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Transformation Type Mapping" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Transformation type map]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> combo box. These type maps specify the mapping rules that will be applied to the model during the transformation.</p><p>When you select a particular map, its contents are displayed in a table below. Each row in the table is a rule to remap one particular type to another. The <strong>From type</strong> and <strong>To type</strong> columns in the table show the source and target types.</p><p>The <strong>Run type mapping in reverse order</strong> check box creates the opposite type mapping. Type maps can be bidirectional, e.g., the same type map is reused both in the UML to XML schema and XML schema to UML transformations. This checkbox governs the direction in which the type map should be used.</p><p>The following operations are available in the <strong>Select type mapping</strong> window:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Back</strong></td><td>Return to the previous dialog box.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Next</strong></td><td><p>Proceed to the next step (in this case, Specify transformation details). <br /><strong>NOTE:</strong> This button is disabled during the <strong>Any to Any</strong> transformations.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Finish</strong></td><td><p>Finish the configuration of the transformation. The <strong>Model Transformations Wizard</strong> exits and the transformation results appear in the project.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675660 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01016: Selecting values

- page_id: `169675660`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675660/Selecting+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-03-22T08:56:05.307+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Selectable value type property values are selected from lists. There can be two types of lists:

- **Non-editable**- for properties with value ranges restricted by UML (for example, Visibility, Message Sort, Event Type).
- **Editable**- for properties with value ranges not restricted by UML and can be defined by the user (for example, Type Modifier, Multiplicity).

Learn about selecting property values from lists in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting single property value from non-editable list' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting single property value from editable list' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting multiple property values' space='MD2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true1[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674773 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01017: Selection and multiple selections

- page_id: `169674773`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674773/Selection+and+multiple+selections
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-23T05:35:52.978+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

570038830

570038832

570038831

To select a shape

- Click the desired shape on the Diagram pane.

To select all shapes of the same type

- Press and hold the Alt key, and click one shape.

To select all shapes on the diagram, do one of the following

- From the Edit menu, choose Select All .
- Press Ctrl+A.

To make multiple selections, do one of the following

- Select a shape on the diagram pane and, holding down Shift, select as many shapes as you need.
- Drag the cursor diagonally across the area you wish to select.

##### Select a group of shapes

To select a group of shapes

- Drag the cursor diagonally across the area you wish to select.

This is a simple and fast way to select a group of shapes on the diagram.

The following rectangular selection modes are available:

1. Partial coverage. Symbols that are only partly covered with the rectangular selector are selected.
2. Complete coverage. Only those symbols that are fully covered by the rectangular selector are selected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The default selection mode is Partial coverage.

To change the group selection mode from Partial coverage to Complete coverage mode and vice versa

- Press Ctrl and drag the cursor diagonally across the area you want to select.

To change the group selection mode for the whole project, do one of the following

- In the main diagram toolbar, click the Complete coverage mode for group selection button.
- In the Environment Options dialog, Diagram > Symbols Manipulation group, change the property of the Group selection mode option.

570038829

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1dba59bd-3e5f-4356-b967-f3d32a38a3f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">570038830</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75e38a43-539c-4d61-916e-3c85e672e8a5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">570038832</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c1d0772-d0d9-478f-bc82-a0807b88998a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">570038831</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To select a shape</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the desired shape on the Diagram pane.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To select all shapes of the same type</p><hr /><ul><li>Press and hold the Alt key, and click one shape.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To select all shapes on the diagram, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>From the <strong>Edit</strong> menu, choose <strong>Select All</strong>.</li><li>Press Ctrl+A.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To make multiple selections, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a shape on the diagram pane and, holding down Shift, select as many shapes as you need.</li><li>Drag the cursor diagonally across the area you wish to select.</li></ul><h3>Select a group of shapes</h3><p>To select a group of shapes</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag the cursor diagonally across the area you wish to select. </li></ul><p><br class="_mce_tagged_br" />This is a simple and fast way to select a group of shapes on the diagram.</p><p>The following rectangular selection modes are available:</p><ol><li>Partial coverage. Symbols that are only partly covered with the rectangular selector are selected.</li><li>Complete coverage. Only those symbols that are fully covered by the rectangular selector are selected.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="complete_coverage_mode_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selection and multiple selections" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The default selection mode is Partial coverage.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the group selection mode from Partial coverage to Complete coverage mode and vice versa</p><hr /><ul><li>Press Ctrl and drag the cursor diagonally across the area you want to select. </li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the group selection mode for the whole project, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the main diagram toolbar, click the <em>Complete coverage mode for group selection</em> button.</li><li>In the Environment Options dialog, <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Symbols Manipulation</strong> group, change the property of the Group selection mode option.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c45236a8-b9d5-44a3-a89f-0e47ecfc276d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">570038829</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674941 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01018: Send Signal Action

- page_id: `169674941`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674941/Send+Signal+Action
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:56.812+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Action
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

846228484

846228486

846228485

The Send Signal Action creates a signal instance from its inputs and transmits it to the target object, where it can cause the start of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024xR1'] transition or the execution of an Activity. The argument values are available to the execution of associated [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024xR1']. The requester continues the execution immediately. Any reply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] is ignored and is not transmitted to the requester. If the input is already a Signal instance, use the Send Signal Action.

| Notation | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Send Signal Action | This example describes an order process.First, an order is created (the Create order ).Next, a Signal to fill the order request is sent to the warehouse (the Fill order request Send Signal Action).Finally, an invoice is created (the Create invoice Call Behavior Action). The relationships are represented with Control Flow paths. |

##### Assigning signals

To assign a Signal to a Send Signal Action, you can use any of the following:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Send Signal Action' space='MD2024xR1'] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Send Signal Action' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Send Signal Action' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Send Signal Action' space='MD2024xR1']

specificationTo assign a Signal via the Send Signal Action’s Specification window

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] for the Send Signal Action.
2. In the Signal property value cell, do any of the following

- 
  - Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] button. The Select Signal dialog opens. Select the Signal from the list, or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new elements' space='MD2024xR1'] .
  - Click the button [ATTACHMENT filename='expand.png'] to open the list of Signals available in the project. Select the Signal from the list. [ATTACHMENT filename='send_signal_action_signal_list.png']
  - Click Close once the Signal is selected.

darg_n_dropTo assign a signal using a drag-and-drop operation

1. Select the Signal in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024xR1'] .
2. Drag the Signal to the Send Signal Action as shown in the following picture. [ATTACHMENT filename='send_signal_action_drag_signal.png']

shortcutTo assign a signal using the Send Signal Action shortcut menu

1. Right-click the Send Signal Action to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DH Links panel shortcut menu and Sync Status icons' space='CDH2024xR1'] .
2. Click Signal . The list of Signals available in the project opens.
3. Select the Signal from the list, or click **New Signal** and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new elements' space='MD2024xR1'].

typeTo assign a signal by typing the Send Signal Action name

1. Select the Send Signal Action shape, click on it to activate the name edit mode.
2. Do one of the following: - if you want to use an existing Signal from the model, start typing an appropriate Signal name, and select it from the opened list. - if you want to create a new Signal in the model, type its name and press Enter.

846286589

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675556 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01019: Separator

- page_id: `169675556`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675556/Separator
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-27T17:12:16.064+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Auxiliary diagram symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

490991590

490991592

490991591

You can use either a horizontal or vertical separator to rule off different parts of a diagram. The **Rotate** button allows changing the separator from the horizontal to vertical position, and vice versa.

See some examples of the types of separators in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the vertical, horizontal, and rectangular separators

You can format the separator symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR1']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

You can display text next to the separator as plain text or HTML text. You can edit the HTML text using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space='MD2024xR1'].

To change separator properties

1. From the separator's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties or select the separator and press Alt+Enter.
2. The Symbol Properties dialog opens. In that dialog, set particular property values, such as Text Position , Line Style , Line Width and other, as you need.
3. Click Close , when you are done.

To change the separator's position

1. Select the separator on the diagram pane.
2. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-28 at 5.32.52 PM.png'] Rotate button at the corner of the selection.

To select the type of separator from the diagram palette

1. Find the Separator under the Common pallet.
2. Click the arrow on the right side of the button to reveal a drop-down menu with the following options: Vertical Separator , Horizontal Separator , and Rectangular Shape .
3. Select the separator you would like to use for your model.

To set the text position on a separator

1. From the separator's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, change the Text Horizontal Position property to one of the following: Left (Default), Center , or Right .

To set a separator line style

1. From the separator's shortcut menu, select Line style .
2. Select the style you need: Dashed (Default), Solid , or Dotted .

490991589

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Auxiliary diagram symbols' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024xR1']

need.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To change the separator's position</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the separator on the diagram pane.</li><li>Click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-28 at 5.32.52 PM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Separator" Separator under the <strong>Common</strong> pallet. </li><li>Click the arrow on the right side of the button to reveal a drop-down menu with the following options: <strong>Vertical Separator</strong>, separator</p><hr /><ol><li>From the separator's shortcut menu, select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, change the <strong>Text Horizontal Position</strong> property to one of the following: <strong>Left</strong> (Default), <strong>Center</strong>, or <strong>Right</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To set a separator line style</p><hr /><ol><li>From the separator's shortcut menu, select <strong>Line style</strong>.</li><li>Select the style you need: <strong>Dashed</strong> (Default), <strong>Solid</strong>, or <strong>Dotted</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc00cf92-7f66-4238-bb99-3a15e39b6f12"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">490991589</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Auxiliary diagram symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Auxiliary Diagram Symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673872 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01020: Sequence diagram

- page_id: `169673872`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673872/Sequence+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:20:27.515+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

307649973

307649975

307649974

INLINE

**Overview**

The Sequence diagram is a type of Interaction diagram (which falls under the behavioral diagramming family) that focuses on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1']interchange between various [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='MD2024xR1'].

A Sequence diagram shows the interaction information with an emphasis on the time sequence. The diagram has two dimensions: the vertical axis representing time and the horizontal axis representing the participating objects. The time axis could be an actual reference point by placing the time labels as text boxes. The horizontal ordering of the objects is not significant to the operation, and you can rearrange them as necessary.

There are two Sequence Diagram modeling approaches:

- Activation bars as the lifelines.
- Activation bars as the Execution Specifications (new modeling approach).

**Purpose**

The purpose of the Sequence diagram is to represent some aspect of the communication between objects to perform a task. The main benefit of the diagram is that it helps to identify the messages exchanged between objects.

**Usage**

A Sequence diagram can be used to:

- represent the details of a UML Use Case
- model the logic of a sophisticated procedure, function, or operation
- analyze the interaction of objects and components to complete a process
- plan and understand the detailed functionality of an existing or future scenario.

**Summary**

Sequence diagrams are valuable because they:

- allow seeing the specific questions, commands, and data being communicated during the execution of a specific task
- identify the communication required to fulfill an interaction
- identify the objects that participate in an interaction
- identify the data that is passed as part of the interactions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Sequence diagram

307649971

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dragging objects' space='MD2024xR1']
