<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673468 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01021: Sequence diagram elements

- page_id: `169673468`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673468/Sequence+diagram+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-12-27T14:58:34.953+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import > Special Transformation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

491052790

491052793

491052789

**On this page**

3

491052792

##### Lifelines

All of the EA Lifelines will be imported, but a part or port within a Lifeline will be transformed into a new separate [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='MD2024xR1'].

###### Gaps between Lifelines

The position and width of any Lifeline created in EA will not be imported. Every Lifeline will be given a fixed value and position in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM will place the first Lifeline on the left-hand side of the diagram and the second Lifeline on the right-hand side next to the first one. The length of the gap between the Lifelines will be fixed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Gaps between Lifelines.

###### Lifelines Arrangement

A Lifeline can be nested within another component, such as a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MD2024xR1']or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024xR1']. If this is the case, every component nested within the Lifeline and the Lifeline itself will be drawn separately. They will be arranged in order depending on the position of their Lifeline lines.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Lifelines arrangement.

###### Class, Part, and Port

When represented as Lifelines, Classes, Parts, and Ports have different characteristics from the others. They will be bundled according to their relationships. A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024xR1'] provides one convenient way to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR1'], Part, or Port.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Class, Part, and Port.

##### Gate

A Sequence Message whose tail is connected to a Gate and head connected to a Lifeline in EA will be transformed into a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MD2024xR1'] with its tail connected to one of the boundary lines of the diagram in which it is contained. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Gate' space='MD2024xR1']itself will be removed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Gate.

Once the Gate has been removed and the transformation process has been completed, the following transformation message will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: uml:Gate.

A Sequence Message whose head is connected to a Gate and tail connected to a Lifeline line in EA will be transformed into a Reply Message with its tail connected to one of the boundary lines of the diagram in which it is contained. The Gate itself will be removed

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Gate (EA) and Reply Message (MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM).

The Diagonal Message and Reply Message will be connected tothe nearest diagram boundary.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Gate (EA) and Direction of Diagonal Message (MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM).

Anything else connected to a Gate will also be removed, except the tail of a Sequence Message. For example, if the head of a Sequence Message is connected to a Gate, the Message will be removed. If the tail of a Message is connected to a Gate, but the head is connected to anything other than a Lifeline line, the Message will be removed.

##### Endpoints

A Sequence Message whose head connected to an Endpoint and tail connected to a Lifeline line in EA will be transformed into a Lost Message. The Endpoint element itself will be removed. Once the process has been completed, the following transformation message will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: EndPoint.

A Message whose head connected to a Lifeline line and tail connected to an Endpoint will be transformed into a Found Message.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Endpoint.

Anything else connected to an endpoint will also be removed, except the head of a Sequence Message. For example, if the tail of a Sequence Message is connected to an endpoint, the Message will be removed.

If the head of a Message is connected to an endpoint, but the tail is connected to anything other than a Lifeline line, the Message will be removed. Once the process has been completed, the following transformation message will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Message. Source and Target of the Mes- sage are not connected to any Lifeline.

##### Delete Messages

A Sequence Message whose property *Lifecycle* is set to *Delete* (that causes the Lifeline targeted by the Message to end at some range after the point of contact), will be transformed into a Delete Message. The Lifeline connected to its head will end at the point of contact, and all Messages will be removed after that point of contact of the Lifeline. MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM will report the following transformation message once each process has been completed:

Removed element <xmi:id>: Message is under Delete Message.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Delete message.

Under certain circumstances, a Lifeline connected tothe head ofa Delete Message does notendatthepointofcontact.Instead,anActivationwillbecreatedandstartedfromthepointofcontact. TheLifelinewill thenendattheendoftheActivation.However,thatActivationshouldnot interact with any Message.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Special case of delete message.

Under certain circumstances, an **X** sign (normally drawn after a Delete Message) in EA will be drawn on a Lifeline line whose Lifeline is being pointed by a Create Message. If this is the case, the **X** sign has no significant meaning and can be ignored.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The X sign.

##### Branch Messages

If the **Branch with previous Message**option of a Sequence Message is enabled, the tail of the message connect to the tail of its previous Message on the same Lifeline line. The message will be called *Branch Message* from that time on.

Every Branch Message in EAwill be transformed into a normal Message in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Branch Message.

After the transformation process has been completed, the following transformation message will open:

Updated element <xmi:id>: Branch Message updated to regular Message.

##### Boundaries, Controls, and Entities

The Boundary, Control, and Entity elements in a Sequence diagram context in EA are Lifeline elements whose stereotype property types are set to *Boundary*, *Control*, or *Entity*. They will be imported like any other normal Lifelines. However, an XMI exported from EA has an «entity» stereotype problem. This means the Lifeline with the «entity» stereotype applied is unable to show the stereotype icon. You can solve this problem by placing an «entity» stereotype in *EA_Profile.xml.*The converted model can then use it instead of the one from EA.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Boundary, Control, and Entity.

##### Actors

An Actor element in the Sequence diagram context in EA is not a regular Lifeline element. It is a special Lifeline element whose property type is *uml:Actor*. It will be transformed into a regular Actor and a Lifeline element will be created to represent it. The figure below shows an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MD2024xR1']element in the MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Actor.

##### General Ordering

Not every General Ordering element in EA will be imported, as MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM does not support it in the current release.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### General ordering.

After completing the transformation process, the following transformation message will open:

Updated element <xmi:id>: General Ordering will not be imported.

##### State Invariant

A State Invariant in EA will be transformed as is. MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM does not support State Invariants in the current release.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### State Invariant.

##### Continuation

Continuations from EA can be imported to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. They are viewable in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MD2024xR1'], but without a picture displayed in the diagram. MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM does not support Continuations in the current release.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Continuation.

After completing the transformation process, the following transformation message will open:

Updated element <xmi:id>: Continuation will not be displayed.

##### Diagonal Message

A Diagonal Message is a Message whose destination's height is adjustable. A diagonal Message may change Activations.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Tocreate a Diagonal Message in EA,you mustspecify the*Timing Details* property ofthe Message and add a numeric value tothe Duration Constraint input field.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a Diagonal Message.

##### Synchronous Message behavior

The placement of Synchronous Messages in EA affects the way Activations are created.

###### Order

A Message in EA has a *Sequence Number* that indicates the order of the message in the diagram. This information can be found in the exported XMI file. Knowing it helps predict how Activations will be created. The order starts from the top and goes downward, so the first Message in the diagram is the one drawn at the top of the diagram. Its Sequence Number will be set to '1'.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Message sequence number.

###### Process

Generally, in EA, any two Synchronous Messages will be in the same process if they meet the following conditions:

- Both of their tails are placed on the same Lifeline.
- The head of the upper Message and the tail of the lower Message are on the same Lifeline.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Process.

###### Activation level

The Activation Level starts from level 0. It will increase in increments of 1 as an ongoing process that receives a Message that is not a Return Message (Reply Message in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Activation level.

##### Asynchronous Message behavior

If a Message is an Asynchronous Message in EA, its source Activation will end if there is no other Message in the same group with a higher Sequence Number. Its source will be attached to the same Lifeline.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Asynchronous Message.

##### Return Message behavior

A Return Message in EA is called a Reply Message in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. When it is pointed to an Activation, it will not create an Activation at the top of the existing Activation, unlike other normal Synchronous Messages.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Return message.

##### Activation options

You can control how Activations behave at some level through the Message options. You can access Message options by right-clicking any Message, and then selecting the **Activations**option. However, MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM does not support Activation options in the current release.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Activation options.

491058390

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="608c4a9e-892c-41a4-ba39-58e208b6320c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">491052790</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5baad7ef-87c0-4147-84d0-a257aaa97dcd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">491052793</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f84e2b35-859d-4da5-a848-35e183338097"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">491052789</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e166d40-2bb1-4d95-8ecb-c1c6717364a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da78371f-8786-4e84-9f47-bbff8bfd1656"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">491052792</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Lifelines</h3><p>All of the EA Lifelines will be imported, but a part or port within a Lifeline will be transformed into a new separate <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Lifeline" /></ac:link>.</p><h4>Gaps between Lifelines</h4><p>The position and width of any Lifeline created in EA will not be imported. Every Lifeline will be given a fixed value and position in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM will place the first Lifeline on the left-hand side of the diagram and the second Lifeline on the right-hand side next to the first one. The length of the gap between the Lifelines will be fixed.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="gaps_lifelines.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Gaps between Lifelines.</h6><h4>Lifelines Arrangement</h4><p>A Lifeline can be nested within another component, such as a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Port" /></ac:link>. If this is the case, every component nested within the Lifeline and the Lifeline itself will be drawn separately. They will be arranged in order depending on the position of their Lifeline lines.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="lifelines_arrangement.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Lifelines arrangement.</h6><h4>Class, Part, and Port</h4><p>When represented as Lifelines, Classes, Parts, and Ports have different characteristics from the others. They will be bundled according to their relationships. A <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> provides one convenient way to create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link>, Part, or Port.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="class_part_port.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Class, Part, and Port.</h6><h3>Gate</h3><p>A Sequence Message whose tail is connected to a Gate and head connected to a Lifeline in EA will be transformed into a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sequence Message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with its tail connected to one of the boundary lines of the diagram in which it is contained. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Gate" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Gate ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>itself will be removed.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="gate.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Gate.</h6><p>Once the Gate has been removed and the transformation process has been completed, the following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: uml:Gate.</pre></blockquote><p>A Sequence Message whose head is connected to a Gate and tail connected to a Lifeline line in EA will be transformed into a Reply Message with its tail connected to one of the boundary lines of the diagram in which it is contained. The Gate itself will be removed</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="gateEA_replyMD.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Gate (EA) and Reply Message (MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM).</h6><p><span>The Diagonal Message and Reply Message will be connected to<span> </span>the nearest diagram boundary.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="gateEA_diagonalMD.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Gate (EA) and Direction of Diagonal Message (MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM).</h6><p>Anything else connected to a Gate will also be removed, except the tail of a Sequence Message. For example, if the head of a Sequence Message is connected to a Gate, the Message will be removed. If the tail of a Message is connected to a Gate, but the head is connected to anything other than a Lifeline line, the Message will be removed.</p><h3>Endpoints</h3><p>A Sequence Message whose head connected to an Endpoint and tail connected to a Lifeline line in EA will be transformed into a Lost Message. The Endpoint element itself will be removed. Once the process has been completed, the following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: EndPoint.</pre></blockquote><p>A Message whose head connected to a Lifeline line and tail connected to an Endpoint will be transformed into a Found Message.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="endpoint.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Endpoint.</h6><p>Anything else connected to an endpoint will also be removed, except the head of a Sequence Message. For example, if the tail of a Sequence Message is connected to an endpoint, the Message will be removed.</p><p>If the head of a Message is connected to an endpoint, but the tail is connected to anything other than a Lifeline line, the Message will be removed. Once the process has been completed, the following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Message. Source and Target of the Mes- sage are not connected to any Lifeline.</pre></blockquote><h3>Delete Messages</h3><p>A Sequence Message whose property <em>Lifecycle</em> is set to <em>Delete</em> (that causes the Lifeline targeted by the Message to end at some range after the point of contact), will be transformed into a Delete Message. The Lifeline connected to its head will end at the point of contact, and all Messages will be removed after that point of contact of the Lifeline. MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM will report the following transformation message once each process has been completed:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Message is under Delete Message.</pre></blockquote><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deleteMessage.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Delete message.</h6><p><span>Under certain circumstances, a Lifeline connected to<span> </span>the head of<span> </span>a Delete Message does not<span> </span>end<span> </span>at<span> </span>the<span> </span>point<span> </span>of<span> </span>contact.<span> </span>Instead,<span> </span>an<span> </span>Activation<span> </span>will<span> </span>be<span> </span>created<span> </span>and<span> </span>started<span> </span>from<span> </span>the<span> </span>point<span> </span>of<span> </span>contact. The<span> </span>Lifeline<span> </span>will then<span> </span>end<span> </span>at<span> </span>the<span> </span>end<span> </span>of<span> </span>the<span> </span>Activation.<span> </span>Howeve<span>r</span>,<span> </span>that<span> </span>Activation<span> </span>should<span> </span>not interact with any Message.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deleteMessage_special.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Special case of delete message.</h6><p>Under certain circumstances, an <strong>X</strong> sign (normally drawn after a Delete Message) in EA will be drawn on a Lifeline line whose Lifeline is being pointed by a Create Message. If this is the case, the <strong>X</strong> sign has no significant meaning and can be ignored. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="xsign.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The X sign.</h6><h3>Branch Messages</h3><p>If the <strong>Branch with previous Message </strong>option of a Sequence Message is enabled, the tail of the message connect to the tail of its previous Message on the same Lifeline line. The message will be called <em>Branch Message</em> from that time on.</p><p><span>Every Branch Message in EA<span> </span>will be transformed into a normal Message in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="branchMessage.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Branch Message.</h6><p>After the transformation process has been completed, the following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Branch Message updated to regular Message.</pre></blockquote><h3>Boundaries, Controls, and Entities</h3><p>The Boundary, Control, and Entity elements in a Sequence diagram context in EA are Lifeline elements whose stereotype property types are set to <em>Boundary</em>, <em>Control</em>, or <em>Entity</em>. They will be imported like any other normal Lifelines. However, an XMI exported from EA has an «entity» stereotype problem. This means the Lifeline with the «entity» stereotype applied is unable to show the stereotype icon. You can solve this problem by placing an «entity» stereotype in <em>EA_Profile.xml. </em>The converted model can then use it instead of the one from EA.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="boundary_control_entity.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Boundary, Control, and Entity.</h6><h3>Actors</h3><p>An Actor element in the Sequence diagram context in EA is not a regular Lifeline element. It is a special Lifeline element whose property type is <em>uml:Actor</em>. It will be transformed into a regular Actor and a Lifeline element will be created to represent it. The figure below shows an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Actor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Actor ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element in the MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM Containment tree.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actor.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Actor.</h6><h3>General Ordering</h3><p>Not every General Ordering element in EA will be imported, as MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM does not support it in the current release.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalOrdering.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">General ordering.</h6><p>After completing the transformation process, the following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: General Ordering will not be imported.</pre></blockquote><h3>State Invariant</h3><p>A State Invariant in EA will be transformed as is. MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM does not support State Invariants in the current release.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="stateInvariant.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">State Invariant.</h6><h3>Continuation</h3><p>Continuations from EA can be imported to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. They are viewable in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /></ac:link>, but without a picture displayed in the diagram. MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM does not support Continuations in the current release.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="continuation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Continuation.</h6><p>After completing the transformation process, the following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Continuation will not be displayed.</pre></blockquote><h3>Diagonal Message</h3><p>A Diagonal Message is a Message whose destination's height is adjustable. A diagonal Message may change Activations.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagonalMessage.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span>T</span><span>o<span> </span>create a Diagonal Message in EA,<span> </span>you must<span> </span>specify the<span> </span><em>Timing Details</em> property of<span> </span>the Message and add a numeric value to<span> </span>the Duration Constraint input field.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_diagonalMessage.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating a Diagonal Message.</h6><h3>Synchronous Message behavior</h3><p>The placement of Synchronous Messages in EA affects the way Activations are created.</p><h4>Order</h4><p>A Message in EA has a <em>Sequence Number</em> that indicates the order of the message in the diagram. This information can be found in the exported XMI file. Knowing it helps predict how Activations will be created. The order starts from the top and goes downward, so the first Message in the diagram is the one drawn at the top of the diagram. Its Sequence Number will be set to '1'.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Message_sequenceNo.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Message sequence number.</h6><h4>Process</h4><p>Generally, in EA, any two Synchronous Messages will be in the same process if they meet the following conditions:</p><ul><li>Both of their tails are placed on the same Lifeline.</li><li>The head of the upper Message and the tail of the lower Message are on the same Lifeline.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="process.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Process.</h6><h4>Activation level</h4><p>The Activation Level starts from level 0. It will increase in increments of 1 as an ongoing process that receives a Message that is not a Return Message (Reply Message in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="activationLevel.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Activation level.</h6><h3>Asynchronous Message behavior</h3><p>If a Message is an Asynchronous Message in EA, its source Activation will end if there is no other Message in the same group with a higher Sequence Number. Its source will be attached to the same Lifeline.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="asynchronous_message.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Asynchronous Message.</h6><h3>Return Message behavior</h3><p>A Return Message in EA is called a Reply Message in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. When it is pointed to an Activation, it will not create an Activation at the top of the existing Activation, unlike other normal Synchronous Messages.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="returnMessage.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Return message.</h6><h3>Activation options</h3><p>You can control how Activations behave at some level through the Message options. You can access Message options by right-clicking any Message, and then selecting the <strong>Activations </strong>option. However, MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM does not support Activation options in the current release.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="activationOptions.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Activation options.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6b2bbd2-9a61-4725-96a6-a344a774aef7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">491058390</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677100 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01022: Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard

- page_id: `169677100`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677100/Sequence+Diagram+from+Java+Source+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:16:58.881+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Model Visualizer
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

430666716

430666718

430666717

This feature is available only in the Enterprise edition.

The **Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard** allows for visualizing Java method implementation within UML [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD190']. At this time, the UML [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD190'] cannot show Java code with 100% accuracy; however, the modeling tool provides a mechanism for generating a diagram that reflects the essence of Java method content. 
If you want to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD190'] from the Java source, you must first [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reverse Options' space='MD2024xR1']the Java source code to a model.

You can also create a model corresponding to your Java code structure manually.

Ensure the model you use to create a sequence diagram fully corresponds the Java source code you want to represent in the diagram.

One [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD190'] can represent one method in a Java code. Classes are represented as lifelines, and method calls are represented as messages in the diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD190'] can also be used to visualize dependencies for all classes used in this method.

To create a sequence diagram using the **Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard**

1. Open the Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the Diagrams menu, select Diagram Wizards > Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard .
  - From the Analyze menu, select Model Visualizer . In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard and click **Start** .
  - In the Code Engineering Set, select an operation of the class and from the shortcut menu, select Reverse Implementation .
2. Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.

What you should know when selecting classes for the diagram:

- Analyze and split long expressions in the diagram : Select to display every call as a separate call message with a temporary variable initialization, if the expression containing calls cannot be displayed as a call message. In the final expression message, these calls are replaced with appropriate temporary variable names.
- Create reply message : Select to display the return message for every call message.
- Wrap message text : Select to wrap message text in the diagram. In the Maximum wrapped messages name length (in pixels) box, specify the maximum message text length in pixels.

430666715

**Related Pages**:

- Method Implementation Reverse
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6a872b12-27e6-4271-ac24-5fa4d91cd8da"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">430666716</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="393a65ab-6d59-44da-bb75-02bc491fdd33"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">430666718</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e90a7bf4-3458-4711-b619-b60326798e56"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">430666717</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4130b8d-3062-43a3-bcbd-3b30a60ce4d6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature is available only in the Enterprise edition.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The <strong>Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard</strong> allows for visualizing Java method implementation within UML <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sequence diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. At this time, the UML <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link> cannot show Java code with 100% accuracy; however, the modeling tool provides a mechanism for generating a diagram that reflects the essence of Java method content.<br />If you want to create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[sequence diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the Java source, you must first <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Reverse Options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reverse ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>the Java source code to a model.</p><p>You can also create a model corresponding to your Java code structure manually.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cba5dacd-027a-4118-9e0f-677ab0d04000"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Ensure the model you use to create a sequence diagram fully corresponds the Java source code you want to represent in the diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>One <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[sequence diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can represent one method in a Java code. Classes are represented as lifelines, and method calls are represented as messages in the diagram. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[sequence diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can also be used to visualize dependencies for all classes used in this method.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a sequence diagram using the <strong>Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard</strong> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Diagram Wizards</strong> &gt; <strong>Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard</strong>.</li><li>From the <strong>Analyze</strong> menu, select <strong>Model Visualizer</strong>. In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the <strong>Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard </strong>and click <strong><strong>Start</strong></strong>.</li><li>In the Code Engineering Set, select an operation of the class and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Reverse Implementation</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>What you should know when selecting classes for the diagram:</p><ul><li><strong>Analyze and split long expressions in the diagram</strong>: Select to display every call as a separate call message with a temporary variable initialization, if the expression containing calls cannot be displayed as a call message. In the final expression message, these calls are replaced with appropriate temporary variable names.</li><li><strong>Create reply message</strong>: Select to display the return message for every call message.</li><li><strong>Wrap message text</strong>: Select to wrap message text in the diagram. In the <strong>Maximum wrapped messages name length (in pixels)</strong> box, specify the maximum message text length in pixels.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0e91ef9-cd84-473a-9653-58d754e0449a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">430666715</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong>:</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4722dcef-e09b-4400-92f3-f78276a6d976" /></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2022x/Method+Implementation+Reverse">Method Implementation Reverse</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Code Engineering" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676244 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01023: Server Certificates

- page_id: `169676244`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676244/Server+Certificates
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-01-22T14:18:20.932+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

**5**

Server Certificates is a consolidated server certificate management solution. Itallows you to handle secure connections supported by the client.

#### Managing server certificates

You can manage server certificates using Server Certificates option group in Environment Options.

To access Server Certificates option group

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, select Options > Environment .
2. In the options group list on the left:
  - Select Server Certificates or
  - Click the plus icon [ATTACHMENT filename='plus-icon.png'] next to Server Certificates to extend the option list.
3. Then, choose one of these options:
  - Trusted by All Users: manage server certificates trusted by all users in this application on this machine. Add, remove, or view details of the selected trusted server certificate. You may need system administrator rights to manage these certificates.
  - Trusted by Current User: manage server certificates trusted by the current user in this application on this machine. Add, remove, or view details of the selected trusted server certificate. [ATTACHMENT filename='Server_Certificates_tool.jpg'] Managing secure connections.
4. Click Add , locate the certificate file, then click Open to add the certificate.

#### Managing self-signed certificates when using SSO

When using SSO, you can also acceptor reject a self-signed certificate when a secure connection is requested. Once a self-signed certificate is detected, the certificate details are presented for review so you can decide whether to accept or reject the certificate

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Managing self-signed certificate.

If accepted, the certificate is added to the **T****rusted by Current User** list of certificates.

For more information about the Server Certificates in Environment Options, please see the video below:

700349

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39c87a23-eedb-48ad-8299-9b163a0810c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Server Certificates is a <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">consolidated server certificate management solution. It </span>allows you to handle secure connections supported by the client.</p><h2><span>Managing server certificates</span></h2><p>You can manage server certificates using Server Certificates option group in Environment Options. </p><p>To access Server Certificates option group</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of your modeling tool, select <strong>Options &gt; Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the options group list on the left:<ul><li>Select <strong>Server Certificates </strong>or</li><li>Click the plus icon <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="plus-icon.png" /></ac:image> next to <strong>Server Certificates </strong>to extend the option list.</li></ul></li><li>Then, choose one of these options:<ul><li><strong>Trusted by All Users:</strong> manage server certificates trusted by all users in this application on this machine. Add, remove, or view details of the selected trusted server certificate. You may need system administrator rights to manage these certificates.</li><li><strong>Trusted by Current User: </strong>manage server certificates trusted by the current user in this application on this machine. Add, remove, or view details of the selected trusted server certificate.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Server_Certificates_tool.jpg" /></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">Managing secure connections.</span></h6><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Add</strong>, locate the certificate file, then click <strong>Open </strong>to add the certificate.</li></ol><h2><span>Managing self-signed certificates when using SSO</span></h2><p><span style="color: rgb(3,3,3);">When using SSO, you can also accept </span><span>or reject a self-signed certificate when a secure connection is requested. Once a self-signed certificate is detected, the certificate details are presented for review so you can decide whether to accept or reject the certificate</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="self_signed_certificate_management.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Managing self-signed certificate.</h6><p>If accepted, the certificate is added to the <strong>T</strong><strong>rusted by Current User</strong> list of certificates.</p><p>For more information about the Server Certificates in Environment Options, please see the video below:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="871d379d-e66f-4246-8b34-a5c909e21c4f"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="http://youtube.com/watch?v=x4l7r9tClLQ" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">349</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676135 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01024: Setting custom transformation properties

- page_id: `169676135`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676135/Setting+custom+transformation+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-01-21T16:34:17.236+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Working with Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Transformation Details**panel displays the various properties of a specific transformation, selected in the first step of the **Model Transformation Wizard**. Each transformation type has its own set of options, which govern functionality of that transformation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change the transformation properties to the default values, click **Reset to Defaults**.

The following operations are available in the **Specify transformation details** window:

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Back | Return to the previous dialog box. |
| Finish | Finish the transformation configuration. The Model Transformations Wizard exits and the transformation results appear in the project. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Transformation Details </strong>panel displays the various properties of a specific transformation, selected in the first step of the <strong>Model Transformation Wizard</strong>. Each transformation type has its own set of options, which govern functionality of that transformation.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_transformation_wizard_5th_step.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting custom transformation properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To change the transformation properties to the default values, click <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong>.</p><p>The following operations are available in the <strong>Specify transformation details</strong> window:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Back</strong></td><td>Return to the previous dialog box.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Finish</strong></td><td><p>Finish the transformation configuration. The <strong>Model Transformations Wizard</strong> exits and the transformation results appear in the project.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675955 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01025: Setting date and time

- page_id: `169675955`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675955/Setting+date+and+time
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-08-02T16:42:46.872+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Date and time are set in the **Date and Time Settings** dialog. This dialog is always used when specifying the properties that have a date type value. It allows you to select a date, time, and time zone using a visual calendar and/or the **Date**, **Time**, and **Time zone** boxes.

#### NOTE: Date/time format information

Date/time format information

The **Date and Time Settings** dialog uses your computer's locale to set the date and time format. You can still enter date and time values of another format manually in the **Set date and time** box, but the dialog will not recognize them; i.e., you won't be able to use a calendar or the **Date** and **Time** boxes. Using them would automatically change the date and time format to the one specified in your computer's locale.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The figure displays the Date and Time Settings dialog with specified date, time and time zone values.

The **Date and Time Settings** dialog can be opened from:

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] of an element that has a property with a date type value
- A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space='MD2024xR1'] that has a column of date type values.

##### The Date and Time Settings dialog elements

| Dialog element | Element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Set the date and time | Text box | Allows you to enter date and time values in the following order:DateTime (optional)Time zone (optional)Values should be separated by spaces. |
| Date | Visual calendar | To select a date, click a day on the calendar. To switch between months, use the arrows on the left and right sides of the title section of the calendar. To switch between years, use the double-arrows on the left and right sides of the title section of the calendar.The selected date is automatically entered in the Set the date and time box using the format specified in your computer's locale. |
| Date | Spin box | Allows you to select a date value in the format specified in your computer's locale. The default value is the current date.To change a date value, use the up or down arrow to increase or decrease the current value. The selected value is automatically entered in the Set the date and time box. |
| Time | Spin box | Allows you to select a time value in the format specified in your computer's locale. The default value is the current time.To change a time value, use the up or down arrow to increase or decrease the current value. The selected value is automatically entered in the Set the date and time box. |
| Time zone | Drop-down list | Click the arrow on the right side of the box to select the desired time zone. |
| Today | Button | Click the button to set the Date box value to the current date of your computer. |
| Now | Button | Click the button to set the Time box value to the current time of your computer. |
| Default | Button | Click the button to set the Time zone box value to the value set in your computer. |

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

4

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='MD2024xR1']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default property values' space='MD2024xR1']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024xR1']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Refactoring' space='MD2024xR1']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extracting' space='MD2024xR1']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR1']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing lists of properties and commands' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It allows you to select a date, time, and time zone using a visual calendar and/or the <strong>Date</strong>, <strong>Time</strong>, and <strong>Time zone</strong> boxes.<br /> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d911e83-2fca-4f6d-bbcf-05b26aeb2fec"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Date/time format information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Date and Time Settings</strong> dialog uses your computer's locale to set the date and time format. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674826 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01026: Setting diagram as layout template

- page_id: `169674826`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674826/Setting+diagram+as+layout+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:22:15.144+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Layout templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

59422504

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

59422507

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

59422505

After the layout template (appearance of the required Class and its Ports in the Class diagram) is defined, you can now use it as the layout template, which you can reuse in other diagrams.The procedure below describes how to set the diagram as the layout template. Note that the layout template name automatically becomes the same as diagram name. This will be important when applying the layout template, and choosing which template you want to apply.

To set the Class Diagram as a layout template

1. Open the Class Diagram that you want to set as layout template.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right click the diagram and select Display > Layout Template > Use as Layout Template .
  - Ensure that nothing is selected on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR1'] , then in the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_icon.png'] and select Layout Template > Use as Layout Template .

The «layoutTemplate» stereotype appears on the diagram header (see the figure below).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Definition BDD is set as the layout template named Definition. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

#### INFO: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

- From the version 19.0 SP1, you can define the appearance and layout of a Part Property with its Ports in the Composite Structure Diagram and set this diagram as layout template.
- If a Composite Structure Diagram displays several Part Properties, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating layout template' space='MD2024xR1'] directly from a particular Part Property. It's appearance and layout will be copied to the template automatically.

59422503

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figure of this page is **Modem Cable**which you can download here: *[Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1481817717837&api=v2).*

It's appearance and layout will be copied to the template automatically.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dac1cbd0-e47d-441a-90fd-8dec40ac322b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">59422503</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The sample model used in the figure of this page is <strong>Modem Cable </strong>which you can download here: <span class="confluence-link"><em><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1481817717837&amp;api=v2">Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip</a>.</em></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678503 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01027: Setting image DPI

- page_id: `169678503`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678503/Setting+image+DPI
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:20:20.265+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Report provides three levels to set DPI for images in generated reports as follows:

1. Global level By adding the template.output.image.dpi variable in the Report Environment options, this DPI setting takes effect in images in any reports generated from any templates.
2. **Template** level 
The**ImageDPI** variable supported in Report Data of a template can be added so that the defined DPI value takes effect in images in reports generated from this template. NoteThe template level setting has a higher priority than the global level setting.
3. **Object** level 
Supporting**$report.getImageFor($element, $dpi)**, this DPI setting takes effect in some images in a template whereas: NoteThe object level setting has the highest priority. The DPI from this level setting overrides the global and template level settings and takes effect in specified images.
  - $element is a modeling tool element. The image of $element is returned with specified DPI.
  - $dpi is an Integer.
    - The normal value boundary of the integer is 1 to 4800.
      - If less than 1, the value is not taken, and the DPI setting in the template and global levels will be used respectively.
      - If more than 4800, the value will be rounded down to 4800.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Magic Report provides three levels to set DPI for images in generated reports as follows:</p><ol><li><strong>Global</strong> level<br />By adding the<strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> template.output.image.dpi</strong> variable in the Report Environment options, this DPI setting takes effect in images in any reports generated from any templates.<br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Template</strong> level<br />The<strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> ImageDPI</strong> variable supported in Report Data of a template can be added so that the defined DPI value takes effect in images in reports generated from this template.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb0523d6-b7f8-49cb-98cc-5dc23d7a6327"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The template level setting has a higher priority than the global level setting.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Object</strong> level<br />Supporting<strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> $report.getImageFor($element, $dpi)</strong>, this DPI setting takes effect in some images in a template whereas:</p><ul class="alternate"><li><strong>$element</strong> is a modeling tool element. The image of $element is returned with specified DPI.</li><li><strong>$dpi</strong> is an Integer.<ul class="alternate"><li>The normal value boundary of the integer is 1 to 4800.<ul class="alternate"><li>If less than 1, the value is not taken, and the DPI setting in the template and global levels will be used respectively.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If more than 4800, the value will be rounded down to 4800.</p></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b351b559-0d05-4791-af7a-4b0d9bd621f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The object level setting has the highest priority. The DPI from this level setting overrides the global and template level settings and takes effect in specified images.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674842 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01028: Setting layout template as default

- page_id: `169674842`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674842/Setting+layout+template+as+default
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T14:59:28.679+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Layout templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

59632409

59632412

59632411

If you have a commonly used layout template, you can set it as the default. The default layout template will always be the first and separated by a line when trying to apply or open the layout templates. Only one layout template can be set as the default. The procedures below explain how to:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting layout template as default' space='MD2024xR1'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting layout template as default' space='MD2024xR1'] .

###### setSetting the layout template as default template

To set the layout template as default template

1. From the diagram shortcut menu, select Specification .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] , select the Tags property group.
3. Find the <<layoutTemplate>> tag, and select its default property. [ATTACHMENT filename='layout_template_tag_specification_window.png']
4. Click the Create Value button to create a new value for the <<layoutTemplate>> tag, if it is not already created.
5. Ensure that the Value check box is selected and set to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='layout_template_tag_value.png']
6. Click Close . The layout template diagram is now set as the default layout.

###### changeChanging the default layout template

To change the default layout template

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] of the default layout template diagram.
2. Select the Tags property group.
3. Clear the Value check box to set it to false . [ATTACHMENT filename='layout_template_tag_value_false.png']
4. Click Close .
5. Open the layout diagram template that you want to be the default template.
6. Repeat all steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting layout template as default' space='MD2024xR1'] .

59632408

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figure of this page is **Modem Cable** which you can download here:*[Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1481817717837&api=v2).*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac019dc0-3c8b-4da5-b8f8-6f67b4120e18"><ac:parameter ac:name="">set</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span class="confluence-link">Setting the layout template as default template</span></h4><p>To set the layout template as default template</p><hr /><ol><li>From the diagram shortcut menu, select <strong>Specification</strong>.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, select the <strong>Tags</strong> property group.</li><li>Find the <em>&lt;&lt;layoutTemplate&gt;&gt;</em> tag, and select its <em>default</em> property.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="layout_template_tag_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting layout template as default" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button to create a new value for the <em>&lt;&lt;layoutTemplate&gt;&gt;</em> tag, if it is not already created.</li><li>Ensure that the <strong>Value</strong> check box is selected and set <em>to true</em>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="layout_template_tag_value.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting layout template as default" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The layout template diagram is now set as the default layout.</li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f99cf95-0872-46fd-9774-615fd604b326"><ac:parameter ac:name="">change</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Changing the default layout template</h4><p>To change the default layout template</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of the default layout template diagram.</li><li>Select the <strong>Tags</strong> property group.</li><li>Clear the <strong>Value</strong> check box to set it to<em> false</em>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="layout_template_tag_value_false.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting layout template as default" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li><li>Open the layout diagram template that you want to be the default template.</li><li>Repeat all steps of the <ac:link ac:anchor="set"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[previous procedure]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Setting layout template as default" /></ac:link>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d38c196-0fda-4f9f-8592-95fea7298e53"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">59632408</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The sample model used in the figure of this page is <strong>Modem Cable</strong> which you can download here:<span class="confluence-link"><em> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1481817717837&amp;api=v2">Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip</a>.</em></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677280 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01029: Setting package permissions

- page_id: `169677280`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677280/Setting+package+permissions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-04-28T06:52:32.848+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Package permissions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282481725

1282481727

1282481724

**On this page**

**3**

1282481726

##### Modify package permissions

To modify the permissions of the package

1. Right-click the package or its shape and select **Permissions**. The **Permissions of Package** dialog opens. The full name of the dialog depends on the selected package name. Thus, if you select to modify the permissions of the package *domain*, the **Permissions of Package domain** dialog opens.
2. Add new package permission:
  1. Click the Add button under the Package permissions table. The Select User/Group dialog opens.
  2. Use the search bar to filter users or groups, then click to select the check box near the user or group name. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  3. Click OK and see the new row appeared in the Package permissions table.
  4. Click the Permission cell and select:
    - Read-Only to restrict editing of the package for the selected user/group.
    - Read-Write to allow editing of the package for the selected user/group.
  5. If you don't want to apply the same permission on the subpackages of this package, click the Apply To cell and select This package only .
3. Add as many permissions as you need by repeating step 3.
4. Close the Permissions of Package dialog.

###### Inheritable permissions

The **Permissions of Package** dialog includes a few options for handling inheritable permissions. The following table provides their descriptions.

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Include inheritable permissions from the parent | Inheritable permissions of the package are those that apply to its subpackages as well.By default, these permissions are displayed together with individual permissions of every subpackage, but cannot be modified or deleted there. If you want to hide them, click to clear the check box.Let's say you have a package Model and a subpackage Requirements.If you set on the package Model the Read-Only permission for a certain user and leave the default setting to apply this permission on subpackages as well, the Permissions of Package dialog also displays the permission, if the relevant check box is selected. The Inherited From cell value of the inherited permission is Model. |
| Replace all subpackages permissions with inheritable permissions from this package | Inheritable permissions of the package are those that apply to its subpackages as well.If the package, whose permissions you modify, has subpackages, you can choose to replace their individual permissions with those of the parent package. Make sure the appropriate permissions are set as inheritable and click to select the check box. |

##### Modify global permissions and review package permissions

You can use the**Package permissions**dialog to review the permissions set on all packages in a project. This dialog allows you to change the global permissions affecting all users for a project.You can also change or remove the user permission for a selected package.

To review package permissions

- From the Options main menu, select Package permissions . Package permissions dialog opens.

To modify package permissions

1. Open the Package permissions dialog as described above.
2. Click the value in the Permission cell of the Package permissions table set for the user/group and select:
  - Read-Only to restrict editing of the package for the selected user/group.
  - Read-Write to allow editing of the package for the selected user/group.
3. If you don't want to apply the same permission on the subpackages of this package, click the Apply To cell and select This package only . [ATTACHMENT filename='modify_package_permissions_2.png']

To modify global permissions

1. Open the Package permissions dialog as described above.
2. In the upper-right corner of the dialog, select the appropriate Global permission:

- 
  - To allow editing of the entire model, select **Read-Write**.
  - To restrict editing of the entire model, select **Read-Only**. Global permissions can be overridden by package permissions, e.g. Read-Only global permission can be overridden by a package with Read-Write permission. This allows the user/group to edit the package, whereas editing the rest of the model is restricted. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

To copy permissions

1. Open the Package permissions dialog as described above.
2. Select the permission you want to copy.
  - To select multiple permissions, hold down the Ctrl key and select the permissions.
  - To select all permissions, click Ctrl+A .
3. Copy the selected permissions using Ctrl+C .
4. Paste the permissions (e.g., to an Excel document.)
