personal property, meaning its value is unique for each user)</p><p>Show a dialog before reusing the name and text when creating a new Derive relation.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Reuse Requirement Name and Text</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>(This is personal property, meaning its value is unique for each user)</p><p>Reuse name and text when creating a new Derive relation.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%"><p title="">Styling</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ask to update outdated 'Default' symbol styles</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set to true to ask to update outdated 'Default' symbol styles.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ask to update outdated 'Defined Elsewhere' symbol styles</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set to true to ask to update outdated 'Defined Elsewhere' symbol styles.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Suspect Links</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Enable Tracking</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to start tracking suspect links.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Track Deprecated Elements</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to track element deletions.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Track Links in main Project Only</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to track suspect links in the main project only.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Tracking Scope</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select elements whose suspicions should be tracked.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%">SysML</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Propagate SysML Values</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If True, the propagation mechanism is turned on which allows value reconfiguration from the nested structures of the context. Propagated values will be displayed in the initialValue compartment. Set to False to turn off the Value Propagation mechanism. Then the values will be collected only from the specified instances.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Change allocated Behavior context</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, Behavior::context will be the Block to which behavior is allocated.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Rename Property on Type Change</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If set to true, the name of the property or port (except Association End) that is default tool-generated is automatically changed to a type name after specifying the type for them. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Rename Association End on Type Change</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If set to true, the name of the Association End that is default tool-generated is automatically named as a type specified at the role end after connecting or reconnecting the Association. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Create Association on Part Creation</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, an association between a part property type and part owner is created automatically.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Include Directed Features into Combined Direction of Proxy Port</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, includes all owned and inherited Directed Features of the property type into derived combined direction of the Proxy Port. Tread Provided as Out, Required as In direction. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Units</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, the units are shown next to values and can be specified or changed directly on a Value Specification.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Type Names in Property Path</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Use type name in property path &quot;dot notation&quot; when property name is empty.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Allocation Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Ask and Remember </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Definition (Activity allocated to Block)</p><p>Usage (Activity allocated to Part)</p><p>Ask and Remember </p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select the Usage mode to create allocation relationships between an Action and Part Property representing a swimlane in which the action is contained. Select the Definition mode to create an allocation relationship between a behavior of action (Activity) and Block representing a swimlane in which the action is contained.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Item Flow Creation Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Direct</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Between Part Types</p><p>Direct</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Select the mode for managing Item Flow in your project:</p><p>Select &quot;Direct&quot; to create the Item Flow between ports or parts in SysML Internal Block Diagram.</p><p>Select &quot;Between Part Types&quot; to create the Item Flow between Part property types. The &quot;Between Part Types&quot; option also allows you to quickly realize and synchronize Flows on Activity Edges, Connectors, and Messages. </p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Remove Not Realized Item Flows</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to automatically remove Item Flows after the last element that realizes them is deleted. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Creation of Contextual Relationships</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Disabled</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Fully enabled</p><p>Partially Enabled</p><p>Disabled</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Select &quot;Fully Enabled&quot; to establish relationships contextualization throughout the project.</p><p>Select &quot;Partially Enabled&quot; to ignore the contextualization while creating relationships on the diagram pane.</p><p>Select &quot;Disabled&quot; to bypass contextualization.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Item Flows realized on Connectors, Activity Edges, Messages, or Associations whenever a new Item Flow is created or an existing one is modified.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Check Item Flow Realization Compatibility</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set to true to check the Item Flow compatibility with the behavior model while running the Behavior to Structure Synchronization. <br />Learn more about <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR1" ri:content-title="UAF Behavior to structure synchronization" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Learn more about <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Behavior to structure synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Behavior to structure synchronization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="grey" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background colour :">UAF</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Architecture Framework</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0, UAF<br />UAF Enterprise Architecture</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The architecture framework option identifies the environment and a subset of aliases to use within the context of the project (i.e., UAF, DoDAF, MODAF, etc.). To change the framework, go to File &gt; Convert To.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">ISO Date Time format</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">YYYY-MM-DD</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Various, according to your locale</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select ISO Date Time format for setting dates for elements.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">None</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Available library versions</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies which MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library Version to use for the project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">BPMN Compliance Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the compliant BPMN stereotypes should be applied to UAF Elements. The options must be true to create UAF diagrams dependent on the Business Process Diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Map UAF Information Elements on ERD Entities</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the Entity stereotype should be applied to the UAF Entity Item element. The options must be true to create a UAF Entity Relationship Diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Auto Check Model Completeness</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If true, the user will be automatically notified about model incompleteness (recommended for novice UAF users). Note, that the auto-check may decrease the performance of the modeling tool. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Respect UAF Information Flows on Generalization</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the realization of inherited Operational Exchanges/Resource Exchanges is allowed or not. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Respect UAF Information Flows on Aggregation</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the realization of aggregated Operational Exchanges/Resource Exchanges is allowed or not. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Property Sets owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Property Sets, that are created using Property Set Models such as SV-7, NSV-7, or SvcV-7.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Inform about user read-only projects after conversion</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to inform about user read-only projects that haven't been converted during project conversion.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show default documentation for UAF Packages</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the default Package documentation should be shown in the UAF Packages Documentation property:<br />Set to true to show the default Package documentation for the newly created UAF Packages.<br />Set to false to create the new UAF Packages without the default documentation. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR1" ri:content-title="Show default documentation for UAF packages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Operational Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Operational Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Operational constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Operational Constraints, Business Rules, OV-6a, NOV-6a, or L8. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Resource Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Resource Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Resource constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Resource Constraints, SV-10a, NSV-10a, or P8. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Service Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Service Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Service Policies and Service Contracts constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner for the Service Policies constraints is Service Constraints, SOV-4a, SvcV-10a, or S8 and for the Service Contracts, the default owner is Service Constraints. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Strategic Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Strategic Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Strategic constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Strategic Constraints or C8. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Security Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;"> Security Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Security constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Security Constraints. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Validation</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Validation Scope</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation scope. The validation scope can be the entire model or some part of it. When the validation is run, each validation rule is evaluated for each suitable element in the validation scope.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Validate only Visible Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to validate only elements in one or more visible diagrams.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Exclude Elements from Used Read-Only Project</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to exclude projects used in read-only mode and the elements that exist in these projects. Excluded projects will not be validated.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Mark in Tree and Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to highlight the invalid model elements in the browser with a small x symbol. The element symbol will be colored according to the severity level of the problem in the diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Suites</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation suites which should not be run during the model validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Rules</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation rules that should not be run during the automatic model validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Minimal Severity</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">warning</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>debug</p><p>info</p><p>warning</p><p>error</p><p>fatal</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the minimal severity. When the validation is running, only the validation rules of the specified and the higher severity level will be run.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Composition Inspection</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Standard</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Standard</p><p>Advanced</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select the level of composition inspection in your projects. The Standard inspection searches for issues only in the project, while the Advanced inspection, in addition to the Standard inspection, searches for issues in all the used projects. The inspection runs validation rules which validate the correctness of model integrity and project composition.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to check for illegal model references in projects. Illegal references are one of the main reasons why recovered elements appear in shared projects.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Suites</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation suites which should not be run during the passive diagram validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Rules</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation rules which should not be run during the passive diagram validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Minimal Severity</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">warning</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>debug</p><p>info</p><p>warning</p><p>error</p><p>fatal</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the minimal severity. When passive diagram validation is running, only the validation rules of the specified and the higher severity level will be run.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light blue 35%"><strong>Diagram Info</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4" style="width: 100.0%;">Diagram information shows summarized data in each diagram, e.g. the author, creation and modification dates, etc. Choose the diagram tags in standard mode, their values should be shown in a predefined diagram information table, or choose how diagram information should e represented using the HTML code.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light blue 35%"><strong>Symbol styles</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4" style="width: 100.0%;">Specify the shape, path, diagram, and stereotype symbol properties. Create, edit, clone, import/export, or remove element display styles. Also, set default styles or apply new styles to existing diagrams.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Default</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Fill Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RGB [255, 255, 204]</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Change the fill color of the shape.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Fill Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Shapes are colored on the diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Pen Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RGB [66, 66, 66]</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Change the color of the path or lines around shapes.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Text Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RGB [0, 0, 0]</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Change the color of the text.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Line Width</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">1</td><td 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The option allows you to turn on or turn off the element number tag name on the symbol.</td></tr><tr><td name</p><p>Do not display on symbol</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The element number display mode option specifies the position on the symbol where the element number should be 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The option allows you to turn on or turn off the element number tag name on the symbol.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Element Number Display Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Before the element name</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Above the element name</p><p>Before the element name</p><p>Do not display on symbol</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The element number display mode option specifies the position on the symbol where the element number should be displayed.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Autosize</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Adjusts the size of a symbol to the contained information so that it uses minimum space.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Fixed Connection Points</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">When drawing a path between shapes, the end of the path is connected to the fixed point of the shape.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shape</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Same as Diagram</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Same as Diagram</p><p>true</p><p>false</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to keep the original size of the selected shape while editing the model by cutting text to fit the shape. You can use the Resize to Fit Content command (a small black square on the bottom line of the selected shape) to adjust the size of the shape to fit the content completely. By default, the appropriate property defined for the diagram is used.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Suspend Auto-Displaying of Label</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Same as Diagram</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Same as Diagram</p><p>true</p><p>false</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true if you do not want to automatically show labels on the diagram pane while editing the model. You can use the Show Suspended labels command (a small black square on the selected path) to show suspended labels on the diagram pane. By default, the appropriate property defined for the diagram is used.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Label Orientation</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Horizontal</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Horizontal</p><p>Vertical</p><p>Automatic</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the orientation of the labels. Automatic label orientation will rotate labels of symbols according to the symbol orientation or edge orientation on which the symbol is placed.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Header in You can use the Resize to Fit Content command (a small black square on the bottom line of the selected shape) to adjust the size of the shape to fit the content completely. By default, the appropriate property defined for the diagram is used.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Suspend Auto-Displaying of Label</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Same as Diagram</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Same as Diagram</p><p>true</p><p>false</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true if you do not want to automatically show labels on the diagram pane while editing the model. You can use the Show Suspended labels command (a small black square on the selected path) to show suspended labels on the diagram pane. By default, the appropriate property defined for the diagram is used.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Diagram Frame</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Displays diagram frame on the diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Abbreviated Type</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Shows full/abbreviated diagram keyword type on the diagram frame header.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Diagram Name</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The diagram name is displayed or hidden in the diagram frame header.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Diagram Type</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The diagram kind is displayed or hidden in the diagram frame header.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Parameters</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The diagram context element parameters (if there are any) are displayed in the diagram frame header.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Context Name</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The diagram context element name is displayed or hidden in the diagram frame header.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Context Type</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The diagram context element type is displayed or hidden in the diagram frame header.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Context Kind</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The context kind is a keyword predefined in UML (e.g., package, class, activity).</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Autosize</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Adjusts the size of a symbol to the contained information so that it uses minimum space.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Aspect Ratio</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Unspecified</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Unspecified</p><p>1:1</p><p>4:3</p><p>3:4</p><p>16:9</p><p>9:16</p><p>A4 Landscape</p><p>A4 Portrait</p><p>US Letter Landscape</p><p>US Letter Portrait</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Choose the diagram frame aspect ratio from the list or define a new one. the format of your defined aspect ratio should be 'W:H'.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Rounded Corners</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Use rounded corners on the diagram frame. Diagram frames in Activity and State Machine diagrams can be represented as rounded rectangles.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Same as Diagram</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Same as Diagram</p><p>true</p><p>false</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to keep the original size of shapes while editing the model by cutting text to fit inside the shape. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676215 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01031: Setting the spell checking options

- page_id: `169676215`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676215/Setting+the+spell+checking+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:30:15.509+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Spelling Checker
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Dictionary Link

266217422

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Dictionary Link

266217424

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Dictionary Link

266217423

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024xR1'] dialog, you can change general spelling options and the default language to be checked in the modeling tool, as well as manage dictionaries to be used in the modeling tool.

To change spell checker options

1. On the main menu, click Options > Environment . The **Environment Options** dialog box opens.
2. In the options group list, select Spelling . Spelling options appear to specify in the options list. [ATTACHMENT filename='environment_optins_spelling.png']

Spell checking options are described in the following table:

| Property Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Check Spelling as you type | Underlines incorrectly spelled words and provides a list of suggested corrections in a shortcut menu. For more information about spell check while typing, see . |
| Check Tagged Values | Checks all tagged values of a string type. |
| Dictionary | Select language for spelling. All "Open Office" supportive spelling languages can be added to the existing ones. For more information about spelling dictionaries, see . |
| Case Sensitive | If true, words will differ in meaning based on the differing use of uppercase and lowercase letters. |
| Use Camel Case Words | If true, compound words or phrases in which the words are joined without spaces and are capitalized within the compound, e.g. BackColor will be spelled as separate words. |
| Ignore Upper Case Words | If true, words with uppercase words only are not to be spell checked. |
| Skip Numbers | If true, numbers are not spell checked. |

##### Spell checking dictionaries

All Apache OpenOffice supportive spelling languages are available.

To import spelling dictionaries

1. Click the Add button in the Environment Options dialog box > the Spelling branch > the Add Spelling Dictionaries group. The Dictionary dialog box opens.
2. Type the name of a new spelling dictionary in the Name text box.
3. Click the ... button and select the OpenOffice zip file location.
4. Type the description of the new spelling dictionary in the Description text box. [ATTACHMENT filename='dictionary_dialog.png']

#### INFO: Dictionary Link

Dictionary Link

http://wiki.services.openoffice.org/wiki/Dictionaries

266217421

**Related Pages:**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spelling Checker' space='MD2024xR1']**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675422 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01032: Shallow History

- page_id: `169675422`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675422/Shallow+History
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-09T21:55:36.889+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Shallow History represents the most recent active substate of its containing state (but not the substates of that substate). A composite state can have at most one shallow history vertex. A transition coming to the shallow history vertex is equivalent to a transition coming to the most recent active substate of a state.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Shallow History represents the most recent active substate of its containing state (but not the substates of that substate). A composite state can have at most one shallow history vertex. A transition coming to the shallow history vertex is equivalent to a transition coming to the most recent active substate of a state.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7e19d119-2f65-4f2a-b944-60c34f7c4ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673670 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01033: Sharing project data

- page_id: `169673670`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673670/Sharing+project+data
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-17T07:24:24.750+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Decomposing model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The shared packages are available only for local projects. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='MD2024xR1'] is an alternative for shared packages in the Teamwork Cloud environment.

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

Not all contents of a used project are visible in the main project. The used project has a shared part and the part that is not shared. Only contents of the shared part are visible in the main project. The concept “shared” is similar to the public/private attributes of a class in programming languages (e.g., Java).

To designate packages of the project as shared

1. Select a package.
2. Do one of the following: The **Shared Packages** dialog opens. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - From the **File** menu, select **Share Packages**.
  - From the package shortcut menu, select **Project Usages** > **Share Packages**.
3. If not all desired packages are showing in the Selected packages list, select them in the Tree/List tabs and click the Add button.
4. Click OK .

The selected package including its owned elements is shared.

When a project is used in another project, all shared packages of the used project appear in that project. Moreover, each shared package can have a different location in the Containment tree. Any available package in a project can be used as a container for the shared package of the used project.

Additionally, each shared package can provide a “Preferred Path” that can be used as a hint where to mount a package when it is used in other project. No “Preferred Path” means that the package will be mounted directly under the root package Data. Profiles are typically mounted directly under the root package Data, however this can be changed.

Shared package “util” from the used project can be mounted on the “com::company” path in the main project in order to form the “com::company::util” path. The Preferred Path of the Shared Package can be used in this case to serve as a hint for the suggested location where to mount the package.

Used projects form a recursive data structure — the main project uses one or more other projects; these projects in turn can use other projects; those other projects can use another set of projects etc. All model pieces from these projects are gathered and connected into the integral model, which is shown in the Model Browser when the main project is opened.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0b6f6a2-cb26-418c-a484-30deafa22eab"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The shared packages are available only for local projects. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing project usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Used Project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is an alternative for shared packages in the Teamwork Cloud environment.</p><p>This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Not all contents of a used project are visible in the main project. The used project has a shared part and the part that is not shared. Only contents of the shared part are visible in the main project. The concept “shared” is similar to the public/private attributes of a class in programming languages (e.g., Java).</p><p><br /></p><p>To designate packages of the project as shared</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a package. </li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li><span>From the <strong>File</strong> menu, select <strong>Share Packages</strong>.</span></li><li><p><span>From the package shortcut menu, select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Share Packages</strong>.</span></p></li></ul><p>The <strong>Shared Packages</strong> dialog opens.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="shared_packages_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sharing project data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>If not all desired packages are showing in the <strong>Selected packages</strong> list, select them in the <strong>Tree/List </strong>tabs and click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>The selected package including its owned elements is shared.</p><p>When a project is used in another project, all shared packages of the used project appear in that project. Moreover, each shared package can have a different location in the Containment tree. Any available package in a project can be used as a container for the shared package of the used project.</p><p>Additionally, each shared package can provide a “Preferred Path” that can be used as a hint where to mount a package when it is used in other project. No “Preferred Path” means that the package will be mounted directly under the root package Data. Profiles are typically mounted directly under the root package Data, however this can be changed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f9d3aa3-d253-436f-8334-7a9f02534a7c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Shared package “util” from the used project can be mounted on the “com::company” path in the main project in order to form the “com::company::util” path. The Preferred Path of the Shared Package can be used in this case to serve as a hint for the suggested location where to mount the package.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Used projects form a recursive data structure — the main project uses one or more other projects; these projects in turn can use other projects; those other projects can use another set of projects etc. All model pieces from these projects are gathered and connected into the integral model, which is shown in the Model Browser when the main project is opened.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674054 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01034: Showing single node per element

- page_id: `169674054`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674054/Showing+single+node+per+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-21T09:26:20.332+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-single-node']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To represent element node in relation map

- Turn the Show Single Node Per Element option off in the [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] , if you need to see separate nodes of the same element representing every occurrence of the element in different branches of the graph. [ATTACHMENT filename='relation_map_show_single_node1.png']
- Turn the **Show Single Node Per Element** option on in the [IMAGE alt='' src=''], if you need to see only one node of the element representing all occurrence of the element in the whole graph. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p> To represent element node in relation map</p><hr /><ul><li>Turn the <strong>Show Single Node Per Element</strong> option off in the <ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, if you need to see separate nodes of the same element representing every occurrence of the element in different branches of the graph.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Separate nodes of element" ac:alt="Separate nodes of element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="relation_map_show_single_node1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing single node per element" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Turn the <strong>Show Single Node Per Element</strong> option on in the <ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, if you need to see only one node of the element representing all occurrence of the element in the whole graph.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Single node of element" ac:alt="Single node of element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="relation_map_show_single_node2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing single node per element" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ul><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675117 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01035: Signal Event

- page_id: `169675117`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675117/Signal+Event
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-02T14:52:18.102+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Event
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A signal may be sent as the action of a state in a state machine or as a message in an interaction.

A signal is a named object sent asynchronously by one object and received by another.

In the Signal [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'], you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(68,68,68);"><span style="color: rgb(68,68,68);">A signal may be sent as the action of a state in a state machine or as a message in an interaction.</span><br /></span></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(68,68,68);">A signal is a named object sent asynchronously by one object and received by another.</span></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the Signal <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">, you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it. </span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677136 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01036: Single Sign-On Authentication

- page_id: `169677136`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677136/Single+Sign-On+Authentication
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:25.155+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Starting a collaboration session
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

854241299

854241301

854241300

The Single Sign-On (SSO) property is available to log on to a Teamwork Cloud server. For now, you can log using SAML authentication (e.g., ForgeRock, Ping, WSO2). And for you it means:

- No need to remember and type credentials each time you logging to a server.
- No issues about weak passwords and updating it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Logging to Teamwork Cloud using SSO.

SSO implementation is based on the [JxBrowser platform](https://www.teamdev.com/jxbrowser), andis supported by following OS:

- Windows (32-bit & 64-bit)
  - Windows 10
  - Windows 8 & 8.1
  - Windows 7
  - Windows Server 2008 R2
  - Windows Server 2012
  - Windows Server 2016
- Linux (32-bit & 64-bit)
  - Ubuntu 12.04+
  - Debian 7.7+
  - Fedora 20
  - openSUSE 13.1
  - RedHat Enterprise Linux 7

- macOS (64-bit)
  - macOS 10.9.x - 10.12.x

For more information about supported OS, please refer to [JxBrowser](https://www.teamdev.com/jxbrowser)[system requirements](https://jxbrowser-support.teamdev.com/docs/guides/introduction/requirements.html).

854241298

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication server' space='TWCloud2024xR1']

## PAGE 01037: Slot

- page_id: `169675374`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675374/Slot
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-03T12:21:07.181+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Slot specifies that an entity modeled by an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MD2024xR1'] specification has a value or values for a specific structural feature.Values of each [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attribute' space='MD2024xR1'] have specific type and are allocated in the slots of the instance or associated with that instance.

You can modify the current element and add various specifications to it in the element dialog box: click onthe property group Tags, select the tag with the assigned value and clickEditValue to open theSlot[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A Slot specifies that an entity modeled by an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /></ac:link> specification has a value or values for a specific structural feature. </span>Values of each <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Attribute" /></ac:link> have specific type and are allocated in the slots of the instance or associated with that instance. </p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">You can modify the current element and add various specifications to it <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">in the element dialog box:</span> c</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">lick on </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">the property group <span>Tags</span>, select the tag with the assigned value and click </span><span class="hcp1" style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Edit</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span class="hcp1" style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Value <span>to open the </span><span class="hcp1">Slot</span><span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">.  </span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673106 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01038: Slot Multiplicity

- page_id: `169673106`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673106/Slot+Multiplicity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-11T08:24:00.392+02:00`
- ancestors: UML correctness rules (in progress)
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

SlotMult1

**Description**

This validation rule checks if the number of specified Slot Values does not exceed the multiplicity defined for the Defining Feature.

**Severity**

error

**Constrained Element**

Slot

**Solvers**

- Remove last value(s) - removes the Slot Value(s) starting from the last one, leaving only the number of values corresponding to the specified multiplicity.
- Increase multiplicity - increases the Defining Feature's multiplicity by one integer.

**Example**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The Enumeration element *Car Models* has the *Doors*property with the multiplicity set to 1 and Enumeration Literals that inherit that property as a Slot Value. The Enumeration Literal *Coupe* has the Slot *Doors* specified with 2 values (4 and 2), which causes an error since the property *Doors* has the multiplicity of 1, allowing it to have only one value for the Slot. 
The error is resolved via the **Remove last value(s)**solver.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>SlotMult1</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if the number of specified Slot Values does not exceed the multiplicity defined for the Defining Feature.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>error</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Slot</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Remove last value(s) </strong>- removes the Slot Value(s) starting from the last one, leaving only the number of values corresponding to the specified multiplicity.  </li><li><strong>Increase multiplicity </strong>- increases the Defining Feature's multiplicity by one integer.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Slot Multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Slot Multiplicity" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Enumeration element <em>Car Models</em> has the <em>Doors </em>property with the multiplicity set to 1 and Enumeration Literals that inherit that property as a Slot Value. The Enumeration Literal <em>Coupe</em> has the Slot <em>Doors</em> specified with 2 values (4 and 2), which causes an error since the property <em>Doors</em> has the multiplicity of 1, allowing it to have only one value for the Slot. <br />The error is resolved via the <strong>Remove last value(s) </strong>solver. </h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676983 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01039: Slot multiplicity 2

- page_id: `169676983`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676983/Slot+multiplicity+2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-18T09:34:18.853+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

SlotMult2

**Description**

This validation rule checks if the number of Slot Values is in the multiplicity range. The validation rule is triggered if there are fewer values than the set multiplicity range.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

Slot

**Solvers**

- To fix this, make sure the number of Slot Values is not lower than the specified multiplicity range.

**Example**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The Instance Specification *car Models* is typed by the Class *Car Models*, which as an attribute *Sedan*typed by an Enumeration *Airbag Types*with the multiplicity set to four. The Instance Specification's Slot *Sedan*is specified with one value instead of four causing the error. 
The error is resolved by specifying the Slot with four values.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>SlotMult2</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if the number of Slot Values is in the multiplicity range. The validation rule is triggered if there are fewer values than the set multiplicity range.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Slot</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li>To fix this, make sure the number of Slot Values is not lower than the specified multiplicity range.</li></ul><p><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="de566b2c-e1e7-4c85-9936-881513af3bb7">Example</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Slot multiplicity 2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Slot multiplicity 2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Instance Specification <em>car Models</em> is typed by the Class <em>Car Models</em>, which as an attribute <em>Sedan </em>typed by an Enumeration <em>Airbag Types </em>with the multiplicity set to four. The Instance Specification's Slot <em>Sedan </em>is specified with one value instead of four causing the error.<br />The error is resolved by specifying the Slot with four values.</h6>
````

Smart manipulation is a feature designed to make modeling with diagrams easier. Use smart manipulation to display or suppress compartments, create elements, set an auto-size option, reset a label position on a path, and draw relationships with the most commonly used elements. The modeling tool offers varying smart mechanisms depending on the shapes involved.

There are the following types of smart manipulation:

## PAGE 01041: Smart manipulator toolbar

- page_id: `169674761`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674761/Smart+manipulator+toolbar
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-22T14:39:00.795+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Smart manipulation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Smart manipulator toolbar appears when symbols are selected on the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the smart manipulator toolbar, buttons are divided into standard and extra modes. You can toggle between these two modes by clicking the Expand button - the arrow symbol - located at the bottom of every smart manipulator toolbar. Your choice will be remembered for all elements.

Use the smart manipulator toolbar to perform simple actions and create new elements quickly.

To create a new element connected to a particular element

1. From the selected element's smart manipulator toolbar, select the relationship that you want to draw. Create a class symbol. In the class smart manipulator toolbar, select the Directed Association relationship for drawing. The drawing of this Directed Association relationship is initiated, and the mouse cursor displays a class icon. Click the left mouse button. The element displayed on the mouse cursor is created together with the relationship.
2. Right-click the mouse button and select the element from the list to be created at the end of the relationship you are drawing.

- To create a path breakpoint, use the following keyboard combination: Ctrl key + Mouse click.
- To cancel the drawing of an element, press Esc.

To solve the detected symbol ownership problem

1. Select the element with incorrect ownership on the diagram pane (it is highlighted in red). The smart manipulator toolbar appears.
2. Click the red button at the top of the smart manipulator toolbar. The menu with the possible problem-solving solutions appears.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide smart manipulators and the smart manipulator toolbar

1. From the Options menu, select Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. In the Diagram options group, Smart Manipulators category, click to cancel the selection of the Show Smart Manipulators check box.
3. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675376 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01043: Smart Package

- page_id: `169675376`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675376/Smart+Package
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:22:52.606+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1342419680

1342419683

1342419682

This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

Smart package is a special collection of model elements.

There are two ways an element can be included in the smart package:

- *Manually -*the user selects to include the particular element. You can create a smart package named *My Bookmarks*and add frequently used elements to it.

- *Automatically -*the element meets the set of criteria defined by the user. You can create a smart package named *Requirements v5*with the criteria “all elements of type *Requirement*under the package *Business requirements*, having tag *version=5*”.

The membership in the smart package is not the UML ownership; one element can belong to several smart packages.

Smart packages aggregate relevant elements, so that you can:

- Easily browse, navigate, list, and discover these elements in the Containment tree.
- Narrow the scope in both the Find dialog and the element Selection dialog.
- Define dynamic row and column scopes in dependency matrices.

##### Concepts

To better understand this material, please read through the following concepts.

##### Static contents

A collection of manually included elements.

##### Dynamic contents

A collection of elements automatically calculated according to the set of criteria specified by the user.

1342419679

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Profiles' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b3290ec-229c-4ed2-ad56-c93a363fff17"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1342419680</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3dad257-c7a9-4267-901b-5efcddcc4610"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1342419683</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40c1b770-5752-4e66-b44b-b8a1801a8a7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1342419682</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd111539-459a-46d9-8b6c-b00ad9f274a7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Smart package is a special collection of model elements.</p><p>There are two ways an element can be included in the smart package:</p><ul><li><p> <em>Manually - </em>the user selects to include the particular element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4684ce33-c49c-483c-99b2-a93d3a113a41"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create a smart package named <em>My Bookmarks </em>and add frequently used elements to it.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><ul><li><p><em>Automatically - </em>the element meets the set of criteria defined by the user.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf0ee22e-e48d-43c1-817e-7447a6424412"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create a smart package named <em>Requirements v5 </em>with the criteria “all elements of type <em>Requirement </em>under the package <em>Business requirements</em>, having tag <em>version=5</em>”.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p>The membership in the smart package is not the UML ownership; one element can belong to several smart packages.</p><p>Smart packages aggregate relevant elements, so that you can:</p><ul><li>Easily browse, navigate, list, and discover these elements in the Containment tree.</li><li>Narrow the scope in both the <strong>Find </strong>dialog and the element Selection dialog.</li><li>Define dynamic row and column scopes in dependency matrices.</li></ul><h3>Concepts</h3><p>To better understand this material, please read through the following concepts.</p><h3>Static contents</h3><p>A collection of manually included elements.</p><h3>Dynamic contents</h3><p>A collection of elements automatically calculated according to the set of criteria specified by the user.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e59eac7c-23df-47d4-85ac-b782416d0b78"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1342419679</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="76d9c65e-a6bf-482b-903b-63419514e323" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Package" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Profiles" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675390 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01044: Smart package properties

- page_id: `169675390`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675390/Smart+package+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-16T14:10:17.042+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Smart Package
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every smart package has several properties that allow you to specify the contents of the smart package. These properties are described in the following table.

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Query | Stores a set of criteria for automatic inclusion of elements into the contents of the smart package.For more information, see . |
| Additional Elements | Stores the list of the elements manually included into the contents of the smart package.For more information, see . |
| Excluded Elements | Stores the list of the elements excluded from the contents of the smart package.For more information, see |

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Profiles' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675411 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01045: Smart package-related performance issues

- page_id: `169675411`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675411/Smart+package-related+performance+issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-04-07T12:10:59.126+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Smart Package
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

1342803013

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

1342803015

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

1342803014

When working with large projects, smart packages can sometimes cause performance issues. They can influence the time of a search, commit, and derived properties-related operations. However, there is a way to identify which smart packages or properties cause the problem by getting notifications about their evaluation time.

To get notifications about the evaluation time of smart package content and derived properties

1. Go to <modeling tool installation directory>/bin and open the properties file of your modeling tool.
2. Enter the *md.expression.evaluation.duration.notification.threshold* Java argument and define the threshold time in seconds for getting notifications, e.g.: -Dmd.expression.evaluation.duration.notification.threshold=30 NoteThe default value of the *md.expression.evaluation.duration.notification.threshold* argument is 60 seconds. If you add the argument to the properties file of your modeling tool but do not define its value, the default value is used.
3. Save and close the file
4. Restart your modeling tool if it's open.

Your modeling tool is now configured to show notifications about the evaluation time of smart package content and derived properties. The notifications are shown in the **Notification Window** area at the bottom of a modeling tool window.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Smart package content and property evaluation notifications - the time threshold applied is 1 second.

To disable notifications

- Click Do not show this message again when a new notification appears on the right side of a modeling tool window.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Disabling notifications from a modeling tool.

This action disables notifications about the evaluation time of both smart package content and derived properties.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

You can increase the performance of your modeling tool by following these recommendations:

- Avoid using smart packages with Find queries, especially if they are used to find common elements (e.g., Class, Package, Block, etc.).
- Avoid using smart packages with Find queries that have a wide scope.
- Avoid nesting smart packages, i.e., using one smart package as part of another smart package's content.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

You can prevent a number of performance issues stemming from the use of smart packages by using tables and matrices with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying scope' space='MD2024xR1'] instead.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart Package' space='MD2024xR1']true

1342803012

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Profiles' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674771 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01046: Smart shape sizing

- page_id: `169674771`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674771/Smart+shape+sizing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-23T05:37:43.512+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

569974092

569974094

569974093

You can use smart shape sizing when drawing new elements from other elements via paths. The size of the newly created shape is kept the same as the size of the shape from which the path is drawn:

- Drawing a shape from another shape using a path from a smart manipulator. The size of the new shape is the same as the size of the shape from which you draw a path. Note that this is valid if the source shape is not large, for example, if it is not more than 3 times larger than the new shape. [ATTACHMENT filename='shape_size.png']
- Inserting a new shape into the flow in the activity or state diagram. The size of the newly inserted shape is the same as the size of the existing shape.

569974091

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024xR1']

The size of the new shape is the same as the size of the shape from which you draw a path. Note that this is valid if the source shape is not large, for example, if it is not more than 3 times larger than the new shape.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="shape_size.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Smart shape sizing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </li><li><p>Inserting a new shape into the flow in the activity or state diagram.
