## PAGE 01047: Sorter tool

- page_id: `169678202`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678202/Sorter+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-10-12T05:52:00.669+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Information

2050374827

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Information

2050374833

2050374822

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Information

2050374832

$sorter is used to sort a collection of report templates.

#### INFO: Information

Information

The following functions perform case-insensitive sorting by default:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sorter tool' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sorter tool' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sorter tool' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sorter tool' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sorter tool' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sorter tool' space='MD2024xR1']

###### **$sorter.sort(collection, fieldName)**

The sort function for report templates. The context name of this class is "sorter". Use $sorter to access public functions of this class through templates.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted |
| fieldName | java.lang.String | A fieldName to be sorted and the sort direction. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

For example:

```text

```

- $package is the collection to be sorted.
- "name:desc" is separated by ":" in two parts:
  - The first part is to identify fieldName to be sorted.
  - The second part is the option to identify the sorting direction. Sometimes, the direction is not identified. It is ascending by default.

****

###### ******$sorter**.sort(collection, fieldName, isCaseInsensitive)****

The sort function for report templates. This function allows case-sensitive sorting.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
|  | fieldName | java.lang.String | A fieldName to be sorted and the sort direction. |
|  | isCaseInsensitive | boolean | True if case-insensitive sorting. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

****

###### **$sorter.sort(collection)**

This is the sort function for report templates. The context name of this class is "sorter". Use $sorter to access public functions of this class through the templates.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

For example:

```text

```

- $package is a collection to be sorted.

The collection will be sorted by name, in ascending order, by default.

****

###### **$sorter.sort(collection, fieldNameCollection, direction)**

This sort function allows specifying more than one field name to be sorted. The priority of fields is ranked by the sequence in the fieldNameCollection parameter.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
| fieldNameCollection | java.util.Collection | A collection of field names, sorted by the priority of field name order in the collection. |  |
| direction | java.lang.String | Sorting direction. It is either "asc" or "desc". If this field is not entered, "asc" is used by default. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

For example:

```text

```

****

###### ****$sorter.**sort(collection, fieldName, direction, isCaseInsensitive)**

This sort function allows specifying more than one field name to be sorted. The priority of fields is ranked by the sequence in the fieldNameCollection parameter. This function allows case-sensitive sorting.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
|  | fieldName | java.lang.String | A fieldName to be sorted and the sort direction. |
|  | direction | java.lang.String | Sorting direction. It is either "asc" or "desc". If this field is not entered, "asc" is used by default. |
|  | isCaseInsensitive | boolean | True if case-insensitive sorting. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

****

###### **$sorter.sortByFirstNumber(collection, fieldName)**

The sortByFirstNumber function is for report templates. The context name of this class is "sorter". Use $sorter to access public functions of this class through templates.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
| fieldName | java.lang.String | A fieldName to be sorted and the sorting direction. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

For example:

```text

```

- $package is a collection to be sorted by FirstNumber .
- "name:desc" is separated by ":" in two parts:
  - The first part is to identify fieldName to be sorted.
  - The second part is the option to identify the sorting direction. Sometimes, the direction is not identified. It is ascending by default.

****

###### **$sorter.sortByFirstNumber(collection)**

The sortByFirstNumber function for report templates. The context name of this class is "sorter". Use $sorter to access public functions of this class through templates.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

For example:

```text

```

- $package is a collection to be sorted by FirstNumber.

****

###### **$sorter.sortByLocale(collection, String)**

This is a function to sort for report templates. The context name of this class is "sorter". Use $sorter to access public functions of this class through templates. To sort a given collection by a particular language, identify the country code to specify the language.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
| countryCode | java.lang.String | The country code to specify a language to perform sorting. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

For example:

```text

```

- $package is a collection to be sorted by country code.
- "DE" is the country code for GERMANY (ISO country code).

This method sorts a collection by “name” attribute of each element by default.

****

###### **$sorter.sortByLocale(collection, String, String)**

This is a function to sort for report templates. The context name of this class is "sorter". Use $sorter to access public functions of this class through templates. To sort a given collection by a particular language, specify the language by identifying the country code and field name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
| fieldName | java.lang.String | A fieldName to b0e sorted. |  |
| countryCode | java.lang.String | The country code to specify a language to perform sorting. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

For example:

```text

```

- $package is the collection to be sorted.
- “name” is the field name to be sorted.
- "DE" is the country code for GERMANY (ISO country code).

****

###### **$sorter.humanSort(collection, fieldName)**

This is a special function to sort text in a human-like order. It splits text into numeric and non-numeric chunks and sorts them in numerical order. For example, "foo10" is ordered after "foo2".

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
| fieldName | java.lang.String | A fieldName to be sorted and the sorting direction. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

For example:

```text

```

- $package is the collection to be sorted.
- "name:desc" is separated by ":" in two parts:
  - The first part is to identify fieldName to be sorted.
  - The second part is the option to identify the sorting scheme. Sometimes, the order is not identified. It is ascending by default.

****

###### **$sorter.humanSort(collection, fieldName, isCaseInsensitive)**

This is a special function to sort text in a human-like order. It splits text into numeric and non-numeric chunks and sorts them in numerical order. For example, "foo10" is ordered after "foo2". This function allows case-sensitive sorting.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
|  | fieldName | java.lang.String | A fieldName to be sorted and the sorting direction. |
|  | isCaseInsensitive | boolean | True if case-insensitive sorting. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

****

###### **$sorter.humanSort(collection)**

This is a special function to sort text in a human-like order. It splits text into numeric and non-numeric chunks and sorts them in numerical order. For example, "foo10" is ordered after "foo2".

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

For example:

```text

```

- $package is the collection to be sorted.

###### **$sorter.humanSort(collection, fieldNameCollection, direction)**

This humanSort function allows specifying more than one field name to be sorted. The priority of fields is ranked by the sequence in the fieldNameCollection parameter.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
| fieldNameCollection | java.util.Collection | A collection of field names, sorted by the priority of field name order in the collection. |  |
| direction | java.lang.String | Sorting direction. It is either "asc" or "desc". If this field is not entered, "asc" is used by default. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

For example:

```text

```

###### ****$sorter.**humanSort(collection, fieldName, direction, isCaseInsensitive)**

This humanSort function allows specifying more than one field name to be sorted. The priority of fields is ranked by the sequence in the fieldNameCollection parameter. This function allows case-sensitive sorting.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
|  | fieldName | java.lang.String | A fieldName to be sorted and the sorting direction. |
|  | direction | java.lang.String | Sorting direction. It is either "asc" or "desc". If this field is not entered, "asc" is used by default. |
|  | isCaseInsensitive | boolean | True if case-insensitive sorting. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

###### **$sorter.containmentTreeSort(collection)**

This function is for sorting elements that match the Containment tree order.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

###### **$sorter.tagSort(collection, stereotypeName, tagName)**

This function is for sorting a collection item by specifying an applied stereotype name and tag name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
|  | stereotypeName | java.lang.String | An applied stereotype name. |
|  | tagName | java.lang.String | A tag name of the applied stereotype and the sorting direction. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

For example:

```text

```

- $package is the collection to be sorted.
- "name:desc" is separated by ":" in two parts:
  - The first part is to identify tagName to be sorted.
  - The second part is the option to identify the sorting scheme. Sometimes, the order is not identified. It is ascending by default.

###### **$sorter.tagSort($collection, $stereotypeName, $tagName, $forceNumber)**

This function is for sorting a collection item by specifying an applied stereotype name and tag name and forcing a tag value to convert to a numeric before sorting.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
|  | stereotypeName | java.lang.String | An applied stereotype name. |
|  | tagName | java.lang.String | A tag name of the applied stereotype and the sorting direction. |
|  | forceNumber | boolean | True if a tag value is converted to a numeric before sorting. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

For example:

```text

```

- $package is the collection to be sorted.
- "name:desc" is separated by ":" in two parts:
  - The first part is to identify tagName to be sorted.
  - The second part is the option to identify the sorting scheme. Sometimes, the order is not identified. It is ascending by default.
- If this parameter is not defined in the function, the forceNumber default value of false will be applied.

###### **$sorter.tagSort($collection, $stereotype, $tagName)**

This function is for sorting a collection item by specifying an applied stereotype object and tag name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection to be sorted. |
|  | stereotype | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles.Stereotype | An applied stereotype object. |
|  | tagName | java.lang.String | A tag name of the applied stereotype and the sorting direction. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A sorted collection. |

For example:

```text

```

- $package is the collection to be sorted.
- "name:desc" is separated by ":" in two parts:
  - The first part is to identify tagName to be sorted.
  - The second part is the option to identify the sorting scheme. Sometimes, the order is not identified. It is ascending by default.

###### ****$sorter**.tagSort($collection, $stereotype, $tagName, $forceNumber)**

This function is for sorting a collection item by specifying an applied stereotype object and tag name and forcing a tag value to convert to a numeric before sorting.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

|  | forceNumber | boolean | True if a tag value is converted to a numeric before sorting. |

For example:

- "name:desc" is separated by ":" in two parts:

  - The second part is the option to identify the sorting scheme. Sometimes, the order is not identified. It is ascending by default.

For example, &quot;foo10&quot; is ordered after &quot;foo2&quot;.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong> Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>collection </td><td>java.util.Collection </td><td>A collection <br />to be sorted.</td></tr><tr><td>fieldName</td><td> java.lang.String</td><td>A fieldName to <br />be sorted and <br />the sorting direction.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td> java.util.Collection</td><td>A sorted collection.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="954492aa-bc74-4ac1-85af-bc4e8cf4adce"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($rel in $sorter.humanSort($package, "name:desc"))
````html
$rel.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li> $package is the collection to be sorted.</li><li>&quot;name:desc&quot; is separated by &quot;:&quot; in two parts:<ul><li>The first part is to identify fieldName to be sorted.</li><li>The second part is the option to identify the sorting scheme. Sometimes, the order is not identified. It is ascending by default.</li></ul></li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$sorter.humanSort(collection, fieldName, isCaseInsensitive)</strong></h4><p>This is a special function to sort text in a human-like order. It splits text into numeric and non-numeric chunks and sorts them in numerical order. For example, &quot;foo10&quot; is ordered after &quot;foo2&quot;. This function allows case-sensitive sorting.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>collection</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection to be sorted.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>fieldName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A fieldName to be sorted and the sorting direction.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>isCaseInsensitive</td><td>boolean</td><td>True if case-insensitive sorting.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Return</strong></td><td colspan="1">-</td><td colspan="1"> java.util.Collection</td><td colspan="1">A sorted collection.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$sorter.humanSort(collection)</strong></h4><p>This is a special function to sort text in a human-like order. It splits text into numeric and non-numeric chunks and sorts them in numerical order. For example, &quot;foo10&quot; is ordered after &quot;foo2&quot;.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>collection </td><td>  java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection <br />to be sorted.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td> java.util.Collection </td><td>A sorted collection. </td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c270b5b1-2746-467f-9635-52f007987cb8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($rel in $sorter.humanSort($package))
$rel.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>$package is the collection to be sorted.</li></ul><p><br /></p><h4><strong>$sorter.humanSort(collection, fieldNameCollection, direction)</strong></h4><p>This humanSort function allows specifying more than one field name to be sorted. The priority of fields is ranked by the sequence in the fieldNameCollection parameter.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s)</strong>  </td><td>collection</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection to be sorted.</td></tr><tr><td>fieldNameCollection</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of field names, sorted by the priority of field name order in the collection.</td></tr><tr><td>direction</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Sorting direction. It is either &quot;asc&quot; or &quot;desc&quot;. If this field is not entered, &quot;asc&quot; is used by default.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A sorted collection.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="463da748-ee5e-499c-b2de-375ed5c79df8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($p in $sorter.humanSort($Package, ["version", "name"], "desc"))
$p.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h4><strong><strong>$sorter.</strong>humanSort(collection, fieldName, direction, isCaseInsensitive)</strong></h4><p>This humanSort function allows specifying more than one field name to be sorted. The priority of fields is ranked by the sequence in the fieldNameCollection parameter. This function allows case-sensitive sorting.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>collection</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection to be sorted.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>fieldName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A fieldName to be sorted and the sorting direction.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>direction</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Sorting direction. If this field is not entered, &quot;asc&quot; is used by default.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>isCaseInsensitive</td><td>boolean</td><td>True if case-insensitive sorting.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Return</strong></td><td colspan="1">-</td><td colspan="1">java.util.Collection</td><td colspan="1">A sorted collection.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><h4><strong>$sorter.containmentTreeSort(collection)</strong></h4><p>This function is for sorting elements that match the Containment tree order.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>collection </td><td>  java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection <br />to be sorted.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td> java.util.Collection </td><td>A sorted collection. </td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><h4><strong>$sorter.tagSort(collection, stereotypeName, tagName)</strong></h4><p>This function is for sorting a collection item by specifying an applied stereotype name and tag name.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>collection</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection to be sorted.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>stereotypeName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An applied stereotype name.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>tagName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A tag name of the applied stereotype and the sorting direction.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A sorted collection.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5102064f-b22a-4cb7-ad55-c6267257df2f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($p in $sorter.tagSort($Package, "MyStereotype", "tagA:desc"))
$report.getStereotypePropertyString($p, "Mystereotype", "tagA") :: $p.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul class="alternate"><li>$package is the collection to be sorted.</li><li>&quot;name:desc&quot; is separated by &quot;:&quot; in two parts:<ul class="alternate"><li>The first part is to identify tagName to be sorted.</li><li>The second part is the option to identify the sorting scheme. Sometimes, the order is not identified. It is ascending by default.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><h4><strong>$sorter.tagSort($collection, $stereotypeName, $tagName, $forceNumber)</strong></h4><p>This function is for sorting a collection item by specifying an applied stereotype name and tag name and forcing a tag value to convert to a numeric before sorting.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>collection</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection to be sorted.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>stereotypeName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An applied stereotype name.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>tagName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A tag name of the applied stereotype and the sorting direction.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>forceNumber</td><td>boolean</td><td>True if a tag value is converted to a numeric before sorting.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A sorted collection.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb9809f1-5495-413a-add8-0c3d34eaf710"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($p in $sorter.tagSort($Package, "MyStereotype", "tagA:desc", true))
$report.getStereotypePropertyString($p, "Mystereotype", "tagA") :: $p.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul class="alternate"><li>$package is the collection to be sorted.</li><li>&quot;name:desc&quot; is separated by &quot;:&quot; in two parts:<ul class="alternate"><li>The first part is to identify tagName to be sorted.</li><li>The second part is the option to identify the sorting scheme. Sometimes, the order is not identified. It is ascending by default.</li></ul></li><li>If this parameter is not defined in the function, the forceNumber default value of <strong>false</strong> will be applied.</li></ul><p><br /></p><h4><strong>$sorter.tagSort($collection, $stereotype, $tagName)</strong></h4><p>This function is for sorting a collection item by specifying an applied stereotype object and tag name.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>collection</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection to be sorted.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>stereotype</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles.Stereotype</td><td>An applied stereotype object.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>tagName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A tag name of the applied stereotype and the sorting direction.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A sorted collection.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92bbb967-b44a-4308-9d89-8fa741f92357"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($p in $sorter.tagSort($Package, $stereotype, "tagA:desc"))
$report.getStereotypePropertyString($p, "Mystereotype", "tagA") :: $p.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul class="alternate"><li>$package is the collection to be sorted.</li><li>&quot;name:desc&quot; is separated by &quot;:&quot; in two parts:<ul class="alternate"><li>The first part is to identify tagName to be sorted.</li><li>The second part is the option to identify the sorting scheme. Sometimes, the order is not identified. It is ascending by default.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><h4><strong><strong>$sorter</strong>.tagSort($collection, $stereotype, $tagName, $forceNumber)</strong></h4><p>This function is for sorting a collection item by specifying an applied stereotype object and tag name and forcing a tag value to convert to a numeric before sorting.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s</strong>)</td><td>collection</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection to be sorted.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>stereotype</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles.Stereotype</td><td>An applied stereotype object.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>tagName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A tag name of the applied stereotype and the sorting direction.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>forceNumber</td><td>boolean</td><td>True if a tag value is converted to a numeric before sorting.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A sorted collection.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b42d7306-d29f-4744-8d7a-d9833f9f4033"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($p in $sorter.tagSort($Package, $stereotype, "tagA:desc", true))
$report.getStereotypePropertyString($p, "Mystereotype", "tagA") :: $p.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul class="alternate"><li>$package is the collection to be sorted.</li><li>&quot;name:desc&quot; is separated by &quot;:&quot; in two parts:<ul class="alternate"><li>The first part is to identify tagName to be sorted.</li><li>The second part is the option to identify the sorting scheme. Sometimes, the order is not identified. It is ascending by default.</li></ul></li><li>If this parameter is not defined in the function, the forceNumber default value of <strong>false</strong> will be applied.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675682 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01048: Sorting elements

- page_id: `169675682`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675682/Sorting+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-02-09T07:27:53.111+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

1344172913

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

1344172916

1344172912

**On this page**

**4**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

1344172915

##### Sorting elements manually

When creating a model, most of the elements are sorted alphabetically in the model browser. However, if you want to customize the structure of your model, you can sort the inner elements of any model element manually. For example, you can change the order of the Packages owned by the parent *Model* element.

#### NOTE: Sorting elements in server projects

Sorting elements in server projects

Model elements can only be reordered if they or elements with the same owner are not locked by another user.

To sort model elements manually

1. In the model browser, double click the model element that owns the elements you want to sort. Its Specification window opens.
2. In the property group list on the left side of the window, select the Inner Elements node.
3. Make sure the sorting view is selected in the Specification window toolbar, as displayed in the figure below.
4. On the right side of the window, select the element that you want to move up or down in the model browser. Sorting multiple elementsIf you want to reorder a group of elements, select multiple elements by clicking the first or last element of the group. Then press Shift+▼ to select elements below it or Shift+▲ to select elements above it.
5. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Up button on the bottom of the window, to move the element up.
  - Click the Down button on the bottom of the window, to move the element down.
6. Repeat step 5 until the element is in the desired position.
7. Close the Specification window.

Once you sort elements in the Specification window of the owning element, the elements are instantly reordered in the model browser as well. The newly created elements of the sorted element type are automatically placed at the bottom.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Sorting inner Packages of the *System Structure* Package.

##### Sorting elements alphabetically

If you have sorted the elements of your model manually, as described above, but want to restore alphabetical element sorting, you can do it in the Specification window of the owning element.

#### NOTE: Sorting elements in server projects

Sorting elements in server projects

Model elements can only be reordered if they or elements with the same owner are not locked by another user.

To sort model elements alphabetically

1. In the model browser, double click the model element owning the elements you want to sort. Its Specification window opens.
2. In the property group list on the left side of the window, select the Inner Elements node.
3. Make sure the sorting view is selected in the Specification window toolbar, as displayed in the figure above.
4. On the right side of the window, select any element in the group of elements that you want to sort.
5. Click the **Sort A to Z** button. TipIf the **Sort A to Z** button is disabled (grayed out), the elements of the selected element group are already sorted alphabetically.
6. Close the Specification window.

After completing the steps above, all the elements in the selected element group are sorted alphabetically.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Its Specification window opens.</li><li>In the property group list on the left side of the window, select the <strong>Inner Elements</strong> node.</li><li>Make sure the sorting view is selected in the Specification window toolbar, as displayed in the figure above.</li><li>On the right side of the window, select any element in the group of elements that you want to sort.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Sort A to Z</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7fa18bdc-2e30-46b2-99dc-adc4bfbb6687"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Sort A to Z</strong> button is disabled (grayed out), the elements of the selected element group are already sorted alphabetically.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Close the Specification window.</li></ol><p><br />After completing the steps above, all the elements in the selected element group are sorted alphabetically. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673404 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01049: Special characters

- page_id: `169673404`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673404/Special+characters
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:49.131+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tool can use either XML version 1.0 or XML version 1.1. Because XML version 1.0 does not support some special characters, our modeling tool contains a mechanism that replaces certain characters in element names with the underscore symbol ( _ ). However, only a small part of them are replaced, including profile, stereotype and stereotype property names. Please try to avoid special characters and use ASCII letters or numbers. In comparison,XML 1.1 supports all characters,except code point 3000 from “Japanese-style punctuation,"which means the tool will replace it withan underscore symbol ( _ ).

The default XML version in the modeling tool is 1.0, to use XML 1.1, you need to manually change it.

To change the XML version

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select**Options**>**Environment**. The**Environment Options**dialog opens.
2. In the Environment Options dialog, select General > Save/Load from the options group list.
3. Click the XML Version drop-down list in the option specification area on the right side of the dialog, and select the desired XML version.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Our modeling tool can use either XML version 1.0 or XML version 1.1. <span>Because XML version 1.0 does not support some special characters, our modeling tool contains a mechanism that replaces certain characters in element names with the underscore symbol ( _ ). However, only a small part of them are replaced, including profile, stereotype and stereotype property names. Please try to avoid special characters and use ASCII letters or numbers. In comparison,</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> XML 1.1 supports all characters, </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">except code point 3000 from “Japanese-style punctuation,&quot;</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> which means the tool will replace it with </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">an underscore symbol ( _ )</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The default XML version in the modeling tool is 1.0, to use XML 1.1, you need to manually change it. </span></p><p><span><br /></span></p><p>To change the XML version</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the main menu of a modeling tool, select </span><strong>Options</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Environment</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. The </span><strong>Environment Options</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> dialog opens.</span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Environment+Options">Environment Options</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> dialog, select </span><strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>Save/Load</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">from the options group list.</span><br /></span></li><li>Click the <strong>XML Version</strong> drop-down list in the option specification area on the right side of the dialog, and select the desired XML version.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="left"><ri:attachment ri:filename="XML_version.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Special characters" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673442 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01050: Special Transformation

- page_id: `169673442`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673442/Special+Transformation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-02T14:15:30.412+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An EA-exported XMI contains both non-standard UML elements and elements that can break the XMI schema. To retain standard UML elements and keep the XMI schema intact, the Enterprise Architect Import applies specific transformation rules. The following sections describe how each model element is transformed, enabling you to import a complete XMI model conforming to UML standards.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An EA-exported XMI contains both non-standard UML elements and elements that can break the XMI schema. To retain standard UML elements and keep the XMI schema intact, the Enterprise Architect Import applies specific transformation rules. The following sections describe how each model element is transformed, enabling you to import a complete XMI model conforming to UML standards.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f34cc2ac-1b86-4e92-a521-57071f59087c" /></p>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1905557019

1905557021

1905557017

**On this page**

4

1905557020

##### How to build a metric definition for date and time tracking?

If your metric suite isn't created using the *BaseMetricSuite*, you must build the metric definition for date and time tracking on your own.

To build the metric definition for date and time tracking

1. Add a new metric definition with the following characteristics to your metric suite: [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_metric_definition_for_date_and_time_tracking.png']
  - date and time as name
  - String as the result type
  - StructuredExpression as the language of the metric definition body
2. In the Body area of the Default Value dialog, click Create operation , and then select Operation from Model on the right side of the dialog.
3. In the open dialog, select the **FormatDate** built-in operation which is stored in the *UML Standard Profile* and click **OK**. Make sure you clear the**Apply Filter**check box in the **Select Opaque Behavior** dialog.
4. In the **Body** area of the **Default Value** dialog, select the **Date** parameter of the **FormatDate** operation and click The **Reset** button.
5. Select Operation from Model on the right side of the dialog.
6. In the open dialog, select the Timestamp built-in operation which is stored in the UML Standard Profile and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_date_parameter_value.png']
7. In the Body area of the Default Value dialog, select the Format parameter of the FormatDate operation and in the Value box type "dd.MM.yyyy hh:mm aaa".
8. Click OK , and then close the metric definition Specification window.

Now you can track the date and time of each instance of the metric suite.

##### How to access parameter values in a metric definition?

If you need to use the values of a parameter for specifying calculations in a metric definition, you can easily access them with the help of the functionality provided by the StructuredExpression language. Let’s overview the case when you need to use the value of the *scope* parameter inherited from the *BaseMetricSuite* to calculate your metrics.

To access the value of the *scope* parameter inherited from the *BaseMetricSuite*

1. Add a new metric definition to your metric suite.
2. Select the StructuredExpression language for specifying the calculations.
3. In the structured expression, create a script operation with single parameter named scopes .
4. Select the InstanceNavigation built-in operation as the scopes parameter value.
5. Specify the following parameter values of that operation: [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-5-30 20:51:42.png']
  - Contextual Variable as the Instance parameter value.
  - The scope parameter of the BaseMetricSuite as the Navigate Property parameter value.
6. In the script operation, refer to the *scopes* parameter simply by its name as the following Groovy script sample illustrates: import static com.nomagic.magicdraw.modelmetrics.ScriptHelper.*; 
import static com.nomagic.requirements.util.SysMLProfile.*; getElementsRecursively(scopes) 
findAll {el -> isRequirement(el)} 
size()

An alternative way to access parameters is calling the *getValue()* method from MagicDraw Open API on the *valueContext* globally defined value, as the following Groovy script sample illustrates:

```text
def scopes = valueContext.getValue(“scope”)
```

##### How to build a metric definition that takes the result of another metric definition?

Let’s say, you need to calculate the percentage of requirements that are covered by blocks. To do this, you must have the following values:

- Overall requirements count
- Requirements covered by blocks count

Let’s say, these values are the results of two already existing metric definitions: *requirementsCount* and *requirementsCoveredByBlocksCount*. You need another metric definition, which takes the results of the latter metric definitions and calculates the percentage. To access these values and make sure they are already calculated, we must call the *getOneValue()* method from MagicDraw Open API on the *valueContext* globally defined value for both metric definitions, as the following Groovy script sample illustrates:

```text

```

```text
int all = valueContext.getOneValue(“requirementsCount”);int covered = valueContext.getOneValue(“requirementsCoveredByBlocksCount”);com.nomagic.magicdraw.modelmetrics.ScriptHelper.calcPercentage(all, covered)
```

1905832998

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675631 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01052: Specification window

- page_id: `169675631`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675631/Specification+window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T16:19:32.934+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: ['specification-window']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

496564810

496564812

496564811

You can define all model elements in the Specification window.

Shortcut menus, toolbars, and browser help ease the task of editing model elements.

You can also edit model elements and symbol properties directly in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties tab' space='MD2024xR1']**Properties**tab, which is located at the bottom of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Specification window is used to define UML model elements such as class, package, activity. The Specification window is a non-modal window in which you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024xR1'] and work with a model simultaneously.

To open the Specification window, do one of the following

- From the selected symbol shortcut menu, select **Specification**.
- Double-click a symbol on the Diagram pane or in the Model Browser.
- Select a symbol on the Diagram pane and press Enter.
- The element Specification window opens when you add a model element to an owning model element in its Specification window. The second Specification window opens on top of the first. Use the **Back to**or **Forward to**arrow buttons for switching between windows.

496564809

**Related pages**

## PAGE 01053: Specification window toolbar

- page_id: `169675637`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675637/Specification+window+toolbar

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1655005484

1655005486

1655005485

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Icon | Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Open Specification | Opens specification of the selected referenced element in the same window. |
|  | Select in Containment Tree | Selects the chosen element in the Containment tree of the Model Browser. |
|  | Specification Opening Mode | If selected, the referenced Specification windows are opened in the same window. To navigate between specifications, use Back and Forward buttons. You can cancel the selection, if you need the Specification window to be open for each element in a separate window, for example, to compare specifications of several elements at the same time. |
|  | Refresh | Refresh data of the Specification window. Refresh the Specification window of an element to see if any changes that are not directly related to that element were made. Also you may need to refresh data if the Specification window is open for a long time. |

opened in the </ac:inline-comment-marker><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d8b4c0ea-c714-4f7b-8593-fecedd9c67de">same window. To navigate between specifications, use <strong>Back</strong> and <strong>Forward</strong> buttons. Y<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ou can cancel the selection, i</span>f you need the Specification window to be o</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">pen for each element in a separate window, for example, to compare specifications of several elements at the same time.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Refresh button" ac:alt="Refresh button"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Refresh.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window toolbar" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Refresh<br /></span></td><td colspan="1"><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f4ceef48-8094-42c7-a69c-7d02a9b2e1b2">Refresh data of the Specification window. Refresh the Specification window of an element to see if any changes that are not directly related to that element were made. Also <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">you may need to refresh data </span>if the Specification window is open for a long time.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676098 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01054: Specify the target for the Metric Suite

- page_id: `169676098`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676098/Specify+the+target+for+the+Metric+Suite
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-12-01T06:40:44.371+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Metric Suites
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Important

1891057916

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Important

1891057919

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Important

1891057917

A Metric Suite must have a target. It specifies model elements, for which the metrics of a particular Metric Suite can be calculated. The target value can be any type of elements or even a particular element of your model. For example, if the target of a Metric Suite is the Package type, you can calculate metrics for any package in your model by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metric table' space='MD2024xR1'].

Although the target of a Metric Suite can be any type of element or even a particular element of your model, we recommend selecting the required Package or the Package type.

A target needs to be specified as the property of a Metric Suite. The element, or at least the type of the element, must be a target value of that Metric Suite.

To specify the target of a Metric Suite

1. Double-click the shape of the newly created Metric Suite to open its Specification window.
2. Click the cell of the Target property value and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] . The Select Elements dialog opens.
3. In the tree or list on the left side of the dialog, select the element that you want to use as a target. If you want to select the type of elements (e.g., Package), which is stored in a standard/system profile used in your project, make sure you clear the **Apply Filter** check box. Besides, you can select more than one element. For more information about the manipulations in the element selection dialog, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Elements multiple selection' space='MD2024xR1'].
4. Add the element to the **Selected elements** list by clicking [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or [IMAGE alt='' src=''] (if you want to add elements recursively).
5. Click **OK.**
6. In the Specification window, click**Close**. ImportantIf you cannot see a particular Metric Suite in the **Create Metric Table** dialog, check [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specify the target for the Metric Suite' space='MD2024xR1']. It is possible that the element selected in step 1 is not the target of this Metric Suite.

The target is specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Metric Suite with the Package type target.

If you need to calculate metrics for the whole model, select the *Model* Package as the target of your Metric Suite.

1891057915

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metric Suites' space='MD2024xR1']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding a new Metric Suite' space='MD2024xR1']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying parameter definitions' space='MD2024xR1']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building metric definitions' space='MD2024xR1']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building validation-based metric definitions' space='MD2024xR1']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specific use cases' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675116 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01055: Specifying a time for a time event

- page_id: `169675116`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675116/Specifying+a+time+for+a+time+event
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-02-27T13:25:53.272+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Event
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A time event specifies an instance in time by an expression. The expression might be absolute or it might be relative to some other point in time. Relative time events must always be used in the context of a trigger and the starting point is the time at which the trigger becomes active.

A relative time trigger is specified with the keyword “after” followed by an expression that evaluates to a time value, such as “after (5 seconds).” An absolute time trigger is specified with the keyword “at” followed by an expression that evaluates to a time value, such as “Jan. 1, 2012, Noon.”

You can change the time expression by changing the **Is Relative** property value.

To specify a time for a time event

1. Open the selected [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MD2024xR1'] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accept Event Action' space='MD2024xR1'] Specification window having the TimeEvent type assigned.
2. In the **Trigger** category, do one of the following: If you do not see the **Event Type** property, click the button **+** near the **Trigger** category to expand its content.

- 
  - To specify the event occurrence at the absolute time, set **Is Relative** property value to *false*.The time event is specified as absolute, and the command syntax changes from *after()* to *at ()*.
  - To specify the event occurrence at the relative time, set **Is Relative** property value to *true*.The time event is specified as relative, and the command syntax changes from *at ()* to *after ()*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A time event specifies an instance in time by an expression. The expression might be absolute or it might be relative to some other point in time. Relative time events must always be used in the context of a trigger and the starting point is the time at which the trigger becomes active.</p><p>A relative time trigger is specified with the keyword “after” followed by an expression that evaluates to a time value, such as “after (5 seconds).” An absolute time trigger is specified with the keyword “at” followed by an expression that evaluates to a time value, such as “Jan. 1, 2012, Noon.”</p><p>You can change the time expression by changing the <strong>Is Relative</strong> property value.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify a time for a time event</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the selected <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Transition" /></ac:link><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Accept Event Action" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Accept Event Action ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>Specification window having the <strong>TimeEvent</strong> type assigned.</li><li><p>In the <strong>Trigger</strong> category, do one of the following:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1dca04a-933f-4fa0-9df0-4c92c0d1668c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not see the <strong>Event Type</strong> property, click the button <strong>+</strong> near the <strong>Trigger</strong> category to expand its content.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><p>To specify the event occurrence at the absolute time, set <strong>Is Relative</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.The time event is specified as absolute, and the command syntax changes from <em>after()</em> to <em>at ()</em>.</p></li><li><p>To specify the event occurrence at the relative time, set <strong>Is Relative</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.The time event is specified as relative, and the command syntax changes from <em>at ()</em> to <em>after ()</em>.</p></li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675498 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01056: Specifying a Use Case subject

- page_id: `169675498`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675498/Specifying+a+Use+Case+subject
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:22:06.777+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Use Case
- labels: ['use-case-subject', 'specifying-use-case-subject']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

21126566

21126568

21126567

INLINE

The subject of a Use Case represents a system under consideration to which that Use Case applies. The subject can be any element that may have a behavior, such as a Component or Class (SysML Block).

To specify the Use Case subject

1. Select the Use Case or its shape.
2. Drag it onto a Class, SysML Block, or Component shape. The Use Case becomes nested in that shape, and the appropriate model element is set as the Use Case subject.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Having a Use Case with the subject specified jumpstarts the creation of Swimlanes in the Activity diagram owned by that Use Case. In this case, you can select the subject properties you wish to be represented by the Swimlanes on that diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

21126565

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674059 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01057: Specifying colors and legend

- page_id: `169674059`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674059/Specifying+colors+and+legend
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-21T09:27:00.249+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-colors-legend', 'relation-map-colors', 'relation-map-legend']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

As of version 18.3, you can specify the colors and line styles to be used for representing the relationships in your relation maps. Your selection is displayed in the legend in the top right corner of the diagram pane.

To specify colors and line styles

1. Open the Relation Criteria dialog by
  - double-clicking the legend header ( Legend ).
  - clicking the [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] button next to Relation Criteria in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MD2024xR1']**Criteria** area .
2. Open the Style dialog by clicking the [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] button in the Style column of your selected relation criterion. [ATTACHMENT filename='relation_criteria_dialog.png']
3. Change the line color and style to what you like and click OK when you're done. [ATTACHMENT filename='style_dialog.png']
4. When you've changed line colors and styles for all the relation criteria you needed to, click OK to close the Relation Criteria dialog.

The customized legend helps you identify relation criteria. When working with relation maps, the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button is clicked to select by default. If you don't want to see the legend all the time, simply click the button to cancel the selection.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>As of version 18.3, you can specify the colors and line styles to be used for representing the relationships in your relation maps. Your selection is displayed in the legend in the top right corner of the diagram pane.</p><p> </p><p>To specify colors and line styles</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Relation Criteria</strong> dialog by<br /> <br /><ul><li>double-clicking the legend header (<strong>Legend</strong>).</li><li>clicking the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button next to <strong>Relation Criteria</strong> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Criteria</strong> area</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></li><li>Open the <strong>Style</strong> dialog by clicking the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button in the <strong>Style</strong> column of your selected relation criterion.<br /> <br /> <ac:image ac:title="Relation Criteria Dialog" ac:alt="Relation Criteria Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="relation_criteria_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying colors and legend" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Change the line color and style to what you like and click <strong>OK</strong> when you're done. <br /> <br /> <ac:image ac:title="Style Dialog" ac:alt="Style Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="style_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying colors and legend" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>When you've changed line colors and styles for all the relation criteria you needed to, click <strong>OK</strong> to close the <strong>Relation Criteria</strong> dialog.</li></ol><p> </p><p>The customized legend helps you identify relation criteria. When working with relation maps, the <ac:image ac:title="Show Legend" ac:alt="Show Legend"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_legend.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button is clicked to select by default. If you don't want to see the legend all the time, simply click the button to cancel the selection.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Relation Map Legend and Color Scheme" ac:alt="Relation Map Legend and Color Scheme"><ri:attachment ri:filename="relation_map_legend.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying colors and legend" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677038 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01058: Specifying Constraint Element property

- page_id: `169677038`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677038/Specifying+Constraint+Element+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-11T14:23:18.091+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

You must ensure that the constrained element is specified and points to the necessary classifier. Each validation rule modeled as a Constraint has a**Constrained Element** property. This property determines the type of element this rule applies to. If you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new Constraint element' space='MD2024xR1']create the Constraint for a classifier or stereotype, this property is filled automatically. Otherwise, you need to specify it manually, shown in the following procedure:

To specify a constrained element manually

1. Open the Constraint Specification window. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
2. Find the Constrained Element property and select it's value box.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select an appropriate element from the **Select Elements** dialog. For constraints on metaclasses, select an appropriate element from the **UML Standard Profile** > **UML2 Metamodel**. Only the single **Constrained Element** property value is supported for validation rules.
4. Click **OK**. 
The **Constrained Element** property value is specified.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6f77b84-96bc-4ac2-9880-5164476e1c85"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You must ensure that the constrained element is specified and points to the necessary classifier. Each validation rule modeled as a Constraint has a<span> <strong>Constrained Element</strong> property</span>. This property determines the type of element this rule applies to. If you <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating new Constraint element" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">create the Constraint for a classifier or stereotype</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>, this property is filled automatically. Otherwise, you need to specify it manually, shown in the following procedure:</p><p>To specify a constrained element manually</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Constraint Specification window. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to open the Specification window >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Find the <strong>Constrained Element</strong> property and select it's value box.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Edit" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button_in_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying Constraint Element property" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select an appropriate element from the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e15d554-92b2-46f5-be9b-61477a090f51"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For constraints on metaclasses, select an appropriate element from the <strong>UML Standard Profile</strong> &gt; <strong>UML2 Metamodel</strong>. Only the single <strong>Constrained Element</strong> property value is supported for validation rules.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The <strong>Constrained Element</strong> property value is specified.</p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676417 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01059: Specifying criteria for querying model

- page_id: `169676417`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676417/Specifying+criteria+for+querying+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-04-01T07:20:15.542+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

265829020

INLINE

265829022

INLINE

265829021

INLINE

Specifying criteria is necessary to

- Associate row and column elements in a dependency matrix.
- Display relations between elements in a relation map.
- Gather the contents of a smart package.
- Calculate/gather the value of a derived property.

A structured expression can also be the body of the executable opaque behavior, metric definition or validation rule, when the StructuredExpression language is selected for specifying that body.

Use one of the following operation types to specify the criterion:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Simple Navigation' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Metachain Navigation' space='MD2024xR1'] (indirect relations through chains of properties)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Find operation' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new operations' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new operations' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new operations' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new operations' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Property Test operation' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Calling operations from the model' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating script operations' space='MD2024xR1']

You can also use custom operation types. In addition, a criterion can be an expression combining several operations by passing the results of one operation call to another operation.

If multiple criteria (operations) are specified, the result will be calculated according to all these criteria (operations).

You can copy and paste the selected structured expressions in the**Expression**dialog. Select all or part of the structured expression in one dialog and paste it into the same or another dialog.

You can also copy the structured expression:

- from one smart package and paste it into another;
- from the matrices and paste them into the relation maps and vice versa;
- and use it in the smart package.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Copying from the Expression dialog

##### Concepts

Read the following description to better understand the material.

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Contextual ElementContextual element | The element that begins the calculation of the result according to the specified criteria. In a dependency matrix, the context is a row or column element. In a relation map, this is every node of the structure. In a table, the context is the table scope value. A smart package is the context itself. The context of a derived property is the target of the customization element that owns this derived property. |

265841031

INLINE

**Related pages**

true

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674041 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01060: Specifying criteria, layout, and depth

- page_id: `169674041`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674041/Specifying+criteria+layout+and+depth
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-06-08T05:59:33.634+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-criteria-layout-depth', 'relation-map-criteria', 'relation-map-layout', 'relation-map-depth']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After creating a relation map, you need to specify the criteria, layout, and the depth for it.

To change the relation map properties

- Right-click the diagram pane and from shortcut menu, select Specification , or click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] on the Options toolbar and then select Specification . Change the property values in the Specification window of the relation map.
- Specify the most popular property values by using the relation map environment capabilities, such as toolbars and the Criteria area.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can choose whether you want to show or hide Context, Relation Criteria, Element Type, and/or Scope. In the Relation Map **Specification** window, set the needed check-box to true or false.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To select the specified value in the Model Browser or to open its Specification window, you can right-click on the value boxes (except for the Relation Criteria) in the **Criteria** area.

| Element name | Element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Context | Text box with | Click to select the main element from which the structure is started, that is, the context of the structure. You can . |
| Relation Criteria | Text box with | Click to select relation criteria that will be displayed on the relation map. |
| Element Type | Text box with | Click to that will be displayed on the relation map.Standard and custom subtypes of the selected element types can be optionally included when displaying them on the relation map.Select the Include Subtypes check box to includes the subtypes of the selected elements, e.g., models and profiles of Packages in the relation map.Select the Include Custom Types check box to show the custom types extending the selected element types, e.g., a Smart Package extending a Package on the relation map. |
| Scope | Text box with | Click to select packages from which the relation map structure will be shown. |
|  | Command on the Options button menu | Select the relation map layout:Tree.Radial. |
| Depth | Spin box | Type or select the level of the relation map branches that will be automatically expanded. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After creating a relation map, you need to specify the criteria, layout, and the depth for it.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the relation map properties</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the diagram pane and from shortcut menu, select <strong>Specification</strong>, or click <ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the Options toolbar and then select <strong>Specification</strong>. 568086116
````

568086118

568086117

You can specify your diagram's properties by accessing the diagram's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'].

To open the diagram's specification window

- Right-click the diagram's window and click on Specification in the shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Diagram Specification window.

568086115
