<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674589 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01062: Specifying diagram style properties

- page_id: `169674589`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674589/Specifying+diagram+style+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-23T15:27:15.534+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the **Diagram Properties** dialog, you can customize the diagram's style: color, grid, diagram info, font, etc. This allows for your diagram to stand out from other diagrams in your project, or simply just for aesthetic purposes.

To open the **Diagram Properties** dialog

There are multiple ways to bring up the dialog, so do one of the following:

- Select Diagram Properties from the diagram shortcut menu.
- Press Shift+Enter.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Diagram properties dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog, you can customize the diagram's style: color, grid, diagram info, font, etc. This allows for your diagram to stand out from other diagrams in your project, or simply just for aesthetic purposes.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog</p><hr /><p>There are multiple ways to bring up the dialog, so do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Select <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> from the diagram shortcut menu.</li><li>Press Shift+Enter.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_properties.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying diagram style properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Diagram properties dialog.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674470 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01063: Specifying instances as slot values

- page_id: `169674470`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674470/Specifying+instances+as+slot+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-10-13T09:31:48.571+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Instance table
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If the slot type is a classifier, you can specify an instance of this classifier as the slot value. Do this in one of the following ways:

- By creating a new instance.
- By selecting an already existing instance.

To select an instance from the model, simply click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the slot value cell and choose the instance from the tree or list in the open dialog.

To create an instance as a slot value in the Instance Table

1. Select the slot value cell.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='create_instance_specification.png'] . If the slot type has subtypes, choose one of them. [ATTACHMENT filename='instance_table_selecting_subtype_for_new_instance.png'] The new instance is simultaneously created in the model and set as the slot value. You can see this instance in the Model Browser, where it is owned by the classifier, which is a type or a subtype of the selected slot.

To remove an instance from the cell

1. Select the cell.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_instance_specification.png'] . The instance is removed from the Instance Table, but not from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If the slot type is a classifier, you can specify an instance of this classifier as the slot value. Do this in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link">By creating a new instance.</span></li><li>By selecting an already existing instance.</li></ul><p>To select an instance from the model, simply click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying instances as slot values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the slot value cell and choose the instance from the tree or list in the open dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an instance as a slot value in the Instance Table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the slot value cell.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Create Instance Specification" ac:alt="Create Instance Specification"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_instance_specification.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying instances as slot values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. If the slot type has subtypes, choose one of them.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Choosing subtype for new instance" ac:alt="Choosing subtype for new instance"><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_selecting_subtype_for_new_instance.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying instances as slot values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /><p>The new instance is simultaneously created in the model and set as the slot value. You can see this instance in the Model Browser, where it is owned by the classifier, which is a type or a subtype of the selected slot.</p></li></ol><p>To remove an instance from the cell</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the cell.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Remove" ac:alt="Remove"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_instance_specification.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying instances as slot values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br />The instance is removed from the Instance Table, but not from the model.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679017 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01064: Specifying Invisible Stereotypes, Tags, and Constraints

- page_id: `169679017`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679017/Specifying+Invisible+Stereotypes+Tags+and+Constraints
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:28.610+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Working with Profiles > Creating Profiles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example:

174389746

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example:

174389757

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example:

174389747

Normally, tags are used to hold additional specific information. This information helps interpret basic elements differentiated by some external tools, such as transformations, code engineering, etc.

In these cases, you only want to mark the model, not make an impact on existing diagrams.

Tag definitions are properties of stereotype in UML2. UML does not allow using tagged values without assigning stereotype. Your diagrams will be changed when some tags are added into the model.

A modeling tool enables you to make chosen stereotypes or tags in diagrams invisible.

To make a stereotype invisible

The stereotype you want to make invisible must be inherited from «InvisibleStereotype» found in the modeling tool profile.

1. Create a custom stereotype.
2. Create a Generalization between your stereotype and InvisibleStereotype. Your custom stereotype will be not displayed on symbols where it is applied. Note that all tags will also be invisible on symbols.

#### INFO: Example

Example

It is not desirable to see an author tag in diagrams. To accomplish this, follow these steps:

1. Create a stereotype «copyrighted» that extends the Element metaclass.
2. Create a property “author: String” for a «copyrighted» stereotype.
3. Inherit this stereotype from «InvisibleStereotype» (create generalization in the model or diagram).

Now you may enter the tagged value “author” for any element in your model.

The stereotype «copyrighted» will be automatically applied but will be invisible in diagrams.

To create an invisible tag

1. Apply «InvisibleStereotype» on your tag definition (a property of a stereotype).

#### NOTE: Hidden Tags

Hidden Tags

All hidden tags will be invisible on the symbols of stereotyped elements.

#### INFO: Example:

Example:

The stereotype «identification» has two tags: ID and Description. The user would like to see a Description value on elements in diagrams, but ID should be hidden as it is used in some external model transformation tools or similar activities.

Apply «InvisibleStereotype» on the ID property of the «identification» stereotype and it will be not visible on stereotyped elements on diagrams.

To make a constraint invisible

1. Apply the «InvisibleStereotype» stereotype to the constraint.
2. Invisible constraints may be used while creating validation constraints, in DSL customization, or in other modeling cases.

174389745

**Related pages**

- Stereotype
- Tag
- Generalization

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674645 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01065: Specifying Legend Item adornment

- page_id: `169674645`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674645/Specifying+Legend+Item+adornment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-07-10T13:03:41.387+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Legends > Creating Legends > Specifying Legend Items
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

582405493

582405495

582405494

Each Legend Item of a Legend should have a unique style. This style is defined by specifying Legend Item adornment properties. After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='MD2024xR1'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legends' space='MD2024xR1'] a Legend in a diagram or table, Legend Item adornment (style) is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legend Items' space='MD2024xR1'] to the symbols or table rows of corresponding elements and represented as the Legend Item icon inside the Legend. There is a wide range of Legend Item adornment properties, including:

- Fill color
- Pen color
- Text color
- Line width
- Shape or path line color, style, and width
- Icon and icon position on shape or path
- Representation as a square shape, rectangular shape, line, or extended line

You can specify the adornment properties of a Legend Item in the **Adornment Properties** dialog.

To open the adornment properties of a Legend Item

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend Item.
2. Click the specification cell of the Adornment property, and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
3. In the Adornment Properties dialog, specify the desired adornment properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adornment Properties dialog.

582461300

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space='MD2024xR1']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legend Items' space='MD2024xR1']

This style is defined by specifying Legend Item adornment properties. After <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Legends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[creating]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Applying Legends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[displaying]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a Legend in a diagram or table, Legend Item adornment (style) is <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Applying Legend Items" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[applied]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the symbols or table rows of corresponding elements and represented as the Legend Item icon inside the Legend. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674867 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01067: Specifying or removing ignored aspects

- page_id: `169674867`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674867/Specifying+or+removing+ignored+aspects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:55.826+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All of the available aspects are visible when trying:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating an aspect diagram' space='MD2024xR1']: in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024xR1']**Create Diagram** dialog under the Aspect Diagrams group.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams' space='MD2024xR1'] : in the diagram shortcut menu under the Aspect command group.

If you have many diagram aspects defined, but you need to use only a few of them in your project, you can restrict the visibility of any unnecessary aspects in your project. The procedure below describes how to specify/remove ignored aspects.

To specify/remove ignored aspects in your project

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, expand the General property group, and select the Diagram Aspects .
3. On the right side of the Project Options dialog, select the Ignored Aspects option value.
4. Click the Edit button.
5. In the **Select Stereotype** dialog, select aspects that you do not want to use in your project. If you want to use them in your project again, remove them from this list by double-clicking on the aspect in the Selected elements area. Click **OK**. InformationIf you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024xR1'] installed, you can see predefined aspects in the **List** tab by clearing the **Apply Filter** check box in the **Select Stereotype** dialog.
6. Click OK .

All aspects added into **Ignored Aspect**option value are no longer visible when trying to create a new aspect diagram or to apply it to a pre-existing diagram. To see them again in the model, you must remove them from the **Ignored Aspect**option value.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>All of the available aspects are visible when trying:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating an aspect diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To create a new aspect diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:</span> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /><ac:link-body><strong>Create Diagram</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> under the <strong>Aspect Diagrams</strong> group.</li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To apply aspect for a diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>: in the diagram shortcut menu under the <strong>Aspect</strong> command group.</li></ul><p>If you have many diagram aspects defined, but you need to use only a few of them in your project, you can restrict the visibility of any unnecessary aspects in your project. The procedure below describes how to specify/remove ignored aspects.</p><p>To specify/remove ignored aspects in your project</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, expand the <strong>General</strong> property group, and select the <strong>Diagram Aspects</strong>.</li><li>On the right side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Ignored Aspects </strong>option value.</li><li>Click the <strong>Edit</strong> button.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Stereotype</strong> dialog, select aspects that you do not want to use in your project. If you want to use them in your project again, remove them from this list by double-clicking on the aspect in the Selected elements area. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="533336c9-f263-4f4d-860b-3a0ad35545be"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR1" ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> installed, you can see predefined aspects in the <strong>List</strong> tab by clearing the <strong>Apply Filter</strong> check box in the <strong>Select Stereotype</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>All aspects added into <strong>Ignored Aspect </strong>option value are no longer visible when trying to create a new aspect diagram or to apply it to a pre-existing diagram. To see them again in the model, you must remove them from the <strong>Ignored Aspect </strong>option value.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676101 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01068: Specifying parameter definitions

- page_id: `169676101`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676101/Specifying+parameter+definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-02-19T08:52:59.378+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Metric Suites
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1891316524

1891316527

1891316522

**On this page**

4

1891316526

The parameter definition of a Metric Suite can be specified:

- As an attribute with the «ParameterDefinition» stereotype.
- In the body of a metric definition.

If you need to track parameter values, the parameter definition should be specified as the attribute of a Metric Suite. In this case, each value of the parameter becomes the slot of an instance created when running the Metric Suite. If a Metric Suite is created using the *BaseMetricSuite*, it already has a few parameter definitions inherited through the Generalization relationship, such as *scope* and *coveringScope*. If you have created a Metric Suite from scratch or the inherited parameters don't meet your needs, you should specify your own parameter definitions.

To specify a parameter definition as the attribute of a Metric Suite

1. Select the shape of a Metric Suite.
2. Click the Create Element button on the right side of the shape and select Parameter definition . The attribute with the «ParamterDefinition» stereotype is created. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_element_button.png']
3. Type its name directly on the shape, and then click a free space on the diagram.
4. Select the attribute on the shape and click [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_type_button.png'] .
5. Select the type of the parameter. This step is mandatory. A parameter must have a type.

The parameter definition is specified.

Instead of defining the parameters from scratch, you can copy them from the *BaseMetricSuite* and then modify according to your needs.

##### Declaring the context

One of the parameter definitions must be declared as the context of a Metric Suite. If a Metric Suite has the context declared, then any element, for which you select to calculate appropriate metrics, becomes the value of the context parameter definition of this Metric Suite and is used for calculating metrics. Otherwise, calculating metrics for the selected element makes no sense (unless the context is declared in the body of a metric definition).

Make sure the type of the parameter you are going to declare as the context is the same as the type of the target of a Metric Suite.

To declare the context for a Metric Suite

1. On the shape of a Metric Suite, double-click the parameter you need to define as the context for the Metric Suite. The Specification window of the parameter opens.
2. Set the Set As Context property value as true .
3. Click the Close button.

The parameter is declared as the context of the Metric Suite.

If your Metric Suite is created using the *BaseMetricSuite*, it already has the context parameter definition - the *scope*.

1891322135

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676547 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01069: Specifying parameters

- page_id: `169676547`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676547/Specifying+parameters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-04-19T08:39:02.130+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Creating new operations > Creating script operations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The number of parameters a script operation can have depends on the script operation language:

- An OCL expression can have a single parameter, which by default specifies the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MD2024xR1'] . Though the parameter cannot be removed, you can easily reset the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MD2024xR1'] , if necessary.
- A binary expression inherits parameters from the Java class to which the expression body references. You cannot create additional parameters.
- Other script operations, such as JavaScript or Groovy, can have as many parameters as you need (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The number of parameters a script operation can have depends on the script operation language:</p><ul><li>An OCL expression can have a single parameter, which by default specifies the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Contextual element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Though the parameter cannot be removed, you can easily reset the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Contextual element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, if necessary.</li><li>A binary expression inherits parameters from the Java class to which the expression body references. You cannot create additional parameters.</li><li>Other script operations, such as JavaScript or Groovy, can have as many parameters as you need (see the following figure).</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_parameters.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying parameters" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673965 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01070: Specifying relationship line styles

- page_id: `169673965`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673965/Specifying+relationship+line+styles
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-04-02T14:28:48.713+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Using Dependency Matrix > Selecting criteria
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can specify the colors, line styles, or, alternatively, set an icon for the representation of your relationships in **Dependency Matrices**. To be able to do this, you need to access the **Style** dialog in the first place.

To open the **Style** dialog

1. Open the Dependency Criteria dialog by
  - double-clicking the Legend .
  - clicking the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button next to Dependency Criteria in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix Criteria area' space='MD2024xR1']**Criteria** area .
2. Open the Style dialog by clicking the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the Style column of your selected relation criterion. [ATTACHMENT filename='dependency_criteria_dependency_matrix.png']
3. In the Style dialog, customize Color , Style , Start Arrow , End Arrow or set Representation Icon (see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying relationship line styles' space='MD2024xR1'] below for more information). [ATTACHMENT filename='style_dialog.png']
4. When you are done, click OK to close the dialog.

##### TableStyle Options

| Color | Specify the line color. |
| --- | --- |
| Style | Specify the line style. |
| Start Arrow | Specify the arrowhead style. |
| End Arrow | Specify the arrow end style. |
| Representation Icon | Represent relationships using an icon.File - upload the icon image from your file system.URL - add a path to the icon available on a shared location.Remove - remove the uploaded icon image or the specified URL. |

Your selection is shown in the customized legend in the top-left corner or above the matrix. The legend is displayed by default in all new projects, but if you don't want to see it all the time, simply click the [IMAGE alt='' src='']button in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix toolbar' space='MD2024xR1'] and then click **Legend Location** > **Do Not Display**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The legend shown in the top-left corner of the Dependency matrix.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The legend shown above column header in the Dependency matrix.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673956 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01071: Specifying scope

- page_id: `169673956`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673956/Specifying+scope
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-12-20T12:13:15.880+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Using Dependency Matrix > Selecting criteria
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you define a scope for the row or a column, you can:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying scope' space='MD2024xR1'] , dragging selected packages on the scope box
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying scope' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying scope' space='MD2024xR1'] , building a list of elements related through the defined criteria

##### Specify the row/column scopeSpecify the row/column scope

To specify the row/column scope

Do one of the following:

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'] , select one or more packages and drag them to the Row / Column Scope box in the Criteria area. [ATTACHMENT filename='multiple_drag_scope.png']

- For the multiple selection Press and hold Shift or Ctrl.
- If you want to add additional elements, press and hold Ctrl while dragging the elements to the Row / Column Scope box.

- Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button next to the **Row**/**Column Scope** box and in the open dialog select the needed packages. Click **OK**.
- Open the matrix [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] , click the Row / Column Scope property value cell, then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] button, and in the open dialog select the needed packages. Click Close .

##### Define expressions for building personalized hierarchy treesDefine expressions for building personalized hierarchy trees

You can specify the criterion for building a personalized Row/Column hierarchy tree in matrices by choosing from the following tree-view options:

- Select Containment to display the decomposition of elements related through the Containment relationship.
- Select Structure to display the decomposition of Part Properties related through the Composition relationship.
- Select Custom to display the decomposition of elements related through any criteria of your choice.

By selecting Structure, you can build a custom tree-like view for the rows and columns of the matrix. Currently it is possible to build a tree hierarchy by specifying your own criteria. You can select the predefined Structure criteria which builds the same hierarchy as in the Structure Tree in Model Browser.

Select Custom if you need to collect elements that are not necessarily owned by the same package. You can specify the query in the **Row/Column Query** dialog and see the results added to it immediately.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specifying criteria for building a personalized tree-view in a matrix

##### Specify the row/column scope querySpecify the row/column scope query

To specify the row/column scope query

1. Click the Specify Row/Column Query icon in the Row/Column Scope field. [ATTACHMENT filename='row_query_open.png']
2. In the Row/Column Query dialog, select one of the following:
  - Containment . Select if you want that the row/column elements would be displayed as in the Containment tree. O wned elements will be collected recursively starting from the elements specified as scope values.
  - Structure . Select if you want that the row/column elements would be displayed as in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Structure tab' space='SYSMLP190SP2'] . This option is available only when the SysML Plugin is installed.
  - **Custom**. Select if you want to make your own adjustments. Select the Build Hierarchy check box to recursively execute the specified expression for building multilevel hierarchy starting from the scope as the root.******** If you have selected **Custom**, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MD2024xR1']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

/></ac:link>, dragging selected packages on the scope box</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Define expressions for building personalized hierarchy trees"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="851f1af8-ced8-4532-8dc3-e2ad2cd88ae2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>For the multiple selection Press and hold Shift or Ctrl.</li><li>If you want to add additional elements, press and hold Ctrl while dragging the elements to the <strong>Row</strong>/<strong>Column Scope</strong> box.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><p>Click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying scope" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button next to the <strong>Row</strong>/<strong>Column Scope</strong> box and in the open dialog select the needed packages. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li><li>Open the matrix <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, click the <strong>Row</strong>/<strong>Column Scope</strong> property value cell, then click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying scope" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button, and in the open dialog select the needed packages. Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ul><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d3dead4-bc85-4c96-a989-c3c13439f138"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Define expressions for building personalized hierarchy trees</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Define expressions for building personalized hierarchy trees</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can specify the criterion for building a personalized Row/Column hierarchy tree in matrices by choosing from the following tree-view options:</span></p><ul><li>Select <strong>Containment</strong> to display the decomposition of elements related through the Containment relationship.</li><li>Select <strong>Structure </strong>to display the decomposition of Part Properties related through the Composition relationship.</li><li>Select <strong>Custom</strong> to display the decomposition of elements related through any criteria of your choice. </li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">By selecting Structure, you can build a custom tree-like view for the rows and columns of the matrix. Currently it is possible to build a tree hierarchy by specifying your own criteria. You can select the predefined Structure criteria which builds the same hierarchy as in the Structure Tree in Model Browser.</span></p><p>Select Custom if you need to collect elements that are not necessarily owned by the same package. You can specify the query in the <strong>Row/Column Query</strong> dialog and see the results added to it immediately.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="customization_of_matrices.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying scope" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">Specifying criteria for building a personalized tree-view in a matrix</span></h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a0c1cbce-527d-4cc5-b01b-f2d732b6ba02"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Specify the row/column scope query</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Specify the row/column scope query</h3><p>To specify the row/column scope query</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Specify Row/Column Query</strong> icon in the <strong>Row/Column Scope</strong> field.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="row_query_open.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying scope" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the Row/Column Query dialog, select one of the following:<ul><li><strong>Containment</strong>. Select if you want that the row/column elements would be displayed as in the Containment tree. O<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">wned elements will be collected recursively starting from the elements specified as scope values.</span></li><li><strong>Structure</strong>. Select if you want that the row/column elements would be displayed as in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP190SP2" ri:content-title="Structure tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Structure tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This option is available only when the SysML Plugin is installed.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Custom</strong>. Select if you want to make your own adjustments. Select the Build Hierarchy check box to recursively execute the specified expression for building multilevel hierarchy starting from the scope as the root.<strong><strong><br /></strong></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e34aa9ca-e310-490a-bc04-cb51f8324902"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have selected <strong>Custom</strong>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specify the expression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="row_query_dependency_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying scope" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674459 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01072: Specifying the classifier

- page_id: `169674459`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674459/Specifying+the+classifier
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-10-13T09:30:36.483+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Instance table
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Specifying the classifier criteria is necessary. You are not able to add a single instance to the table until the classifier is known.

To specify the classifier for the Instance Table

1. In the Criteria area of the Instance Table, click the ... button next to the Classifier box. The Select Classifier dialog opens.
2. Select one or more elements and add them to the Selected elements list. These are the elements that can be selected:
  - Classifier.
  - Classifier that owns other classifiers. In this case, the classifier and all the classifiers owned by it are specified as criteria.
  - Package that contains classifiers. In this case, the contents of the package, but not the package itself, are specified as criteria.
3. Click OK when you are done.

- To specify the classifier, you can also drag a selected element to the Classifier box directly from the Model Browser.
- To select adjacent elements, select a single element and then hold down the Shift key while you click other elements.
- To select nonadjacent elements, select a single element and then hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements.
- Keep in mind that the new value of the Classifier box replaces the old value. [ATTACHMENT filename='instance_table_dnd_to_specify_classifier.png']

Once the classifier is specified, the following columns are displayed in the Instance Table (on the right of the row number # column):

- Name which is up to display the names of the instances of the specified classifier.
- Columns corresponding to the Data type attributes of the specified classifier and named after these attributes using sentence style capitalization. The columns are up to display the slot values of the instances of the specified classifier.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Specifying the classifier criteria is necessary. You are not able to add a single instance to the table until the classifier is known.</p><p>To specify the classifier for the Instance Table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Criteria area of the Instance Table, click the<strong> ...</strong> button next to the Classifier box. The <strong>Select Classifier</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select one or more elements and add them to the Selected elements list. These are the elements that can be selected:<br /><ul><li>Classifier.</li><li>Classifier that owns other classifiers. In this case, the classifier and all the classifiers owned by it are specified as criteria.</li><li>Package that contains classifiers. In this case, the contents of the package, but not the package itself, are specified as criteria.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e142bbc-9627-4f9b-b361-4be03d8c69fd"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>To specify the classifier, you can also drag a selected element to the Classifier box directly from the Model Browser.</li><li>To select adjacent elements, select a single element and then hold down the Shift key while you click other elements.</li><li>To select nonadjacent elements, select a single element and then hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements.</li><li>Keep in mind that the new value of the Classifier box replaces the old value.<br /> <ac:image ac:title="Using drag-and-drop operation to specify classifier for Instance Table" ac:alt="Using drag-and-drop operation to specify classifier for Instance Table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_dnd_to_specify_classifier.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying the classifier" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Once the classifier is specified, the following columns are displayed in the Instance Table (on the right of the row number # column):</p><ul><li>Name which is up to display the names of the instances of the specified classifier.</li><li>Columns corresponding to the Data type attributes of the specified classifier and named after these attributes using sentence style capitalization. The columns are up to display the slot values of the instances of the specified classifier.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674286 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01073: Specifying the scope for the table

- page_id: `169674286`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674286/Specifying+the+scope+for+the+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-07-26T12:45:29.897+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can specify the scope in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space='MD2024xR1'] for every table.

To specify the scope

1. In the Criteria area of the table, click the ... button next to the Scope (optional) box.
2. In the **Select Scope** dialog, select one or more containers, such as packages or smart packages, and add them to the**Selected Elements** list. The **Select Scope** dialog is a modification of the element[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Elements multiple selection' space='MD2024xR1']**Selection** dialog.
3. Click **OK**. 
The scope is defined, and the contents of the table are updated with the elements from the selected scope. In the future, all elements created in that scope will automatically be added to the table.

Alternatively, you can select one or more elements directly in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'] and drag them to the **Scope (optional)**box, as shown in the image below:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If the contents of the table have not been updated, try the following:

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh_button.png'] on the View toolbar, in case a smart package is specified as the scope criteria.
- Make sure the specified scope contains elements specified in the Element Type box, Metric Suite box, or Classifier box.
- Make sure at least one element is specified for the table.

In addition to specifying the scope for the table yourself, you can set the expression to collect table elements automatically.

To specify the expression to collect row elements

1. In the **Criteria** area, next to the **Scope** box, click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] icon . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Once the**Query**dialog opens (see an image below), select **Custom**from the list of available query criteria.
3. Specify the desired expression to collect elements for your table automatically.
4. Click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']Specifying the expression in the Query dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677040 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01074: Specifying the validation rule properties

- page_id: `169677040`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677040/Specifying+the+validation+rule+properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:24.305+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

If you applied the «validationRule» stereotype to the Constraint element, you must specify the following validation rule properties in its Specification window:**Abbreviation**, **Error Message**, and **Severity**.

To specify the validation rule properties

1. Open the Constraint Specification window. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
2. On the right side of the window, find the Validation Rule group.
3. Specify the following properties:
  - **Abbreviation**. Select the **Abbreviation** property value box, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and type a short word, phase, acronym of the validation rule. It allows you to distinguish the validation rules among other rules when sorting or filtering.
  - **Error Message**. Select the **Error Message** property value box, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and type the description of the invalid situation when this validation rule fails. If you need to define the variable error message, you can embed expressions directly in the **Error Message** value box by using curly brackets - {}. Everything between them is treated as an expression and evaluated for each validation result. Expressions are treated as OCL2.0 expressions by default, but you can also use binary expressions: {bin: <binary expression>}. For example, in the following error message the budget is calculated according to variables. The result of the expression is provided as number in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='MD2024xR1']**Validation Results** panel, e.g. Budget not balanced - overbudget by $500.sum() - 
Sponsor::allInstances().contribution->sum()}$]]>You can insert a hyperlink. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='MD2024xR1']Learn more about defining hyperlinks >>
  - Severity . Select the Severity property value and from the drop-down list select one of the following severity levels:
  - 
    - debug - for missing information of a constrained element that is dedicated for the developer.
    - info - for missing information of a constrained element that is dedicated mostly for the end-user.
    - warning - for incorrect situations that can cause errors. It is used for less severe situations than the error.
    - error - for incorrect situations that must be solved.
    - **fatal** - for critical errors such as model corruption or model is invalid according to the UML metamodel. This severity level is mostly reserved for future use. The icons on invalid elements in the model depend on the selected severity level described above. [Learn more about invalid elements representation >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Validation#Validation-Invalidelementsrepresentation)You can customize severity levels. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing severity levels' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
4. Click Close . The validation rule properties are specified.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1443801d-146f-4840-9bbe-738a899e05a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you applied the «validationRule» stereotype to the Constraint element, you must specify the following validation rule properties in its Specification window:<strong> Abbreviation</strong>, <strong>Error Message</strong>, and <strong>Severity</strong>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify the validation rule properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Constraint Specification window. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to open the Specification window >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>On the right side of the window, find the <strong>Validation Rule</strong> group.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the following properties:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Abbreviation</strong>. Select the <strong>Abbreviation</strong> property value box, click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Edit" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button_in_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying the validation rule properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and type a short word, phase, acronym of the validation rule. It allows you to distinguish the validation rules among other rules when sorting or filtering.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Error Message</strong>. Select the <strong>Error Message</strong> property value box, click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Edit" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button_in_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying the validation rule properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and type the description of the invalid situation when this validation rule fails.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2997c06-a86d-48c7-b726-15df7b242706"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to define the variable error message, you can embed expressions directly in the <strong>Error Message</strong> value box by using curly brackets - {}. Everything between them is treated as an expression and evaluated for each validation result. Expressions are treated as OCL2.0 expressions by default, but you can also use binary expressions: {bin: &lt;binary expression&gt;}. For example, in the following error message the budget is calculated according to variables. The result of the expression is provided as number in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validation Results panel" /><ac:link-body><strong>Validation Results</strong> panel</ac:link-body></ac:link>, e.g. Budget not  balanced - overbudget by $500.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2db7e489-1562-4746-87c3-a6f22b58fdbf"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Budget not  balanced - overbudget by {
ResearchProject::allInstances().budget->sum() - 
Sponsor::allInstances().contribution->sum()}$]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can insert a hyperlink.</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Defining hyperlinks" /><ac:link-body>L<span class="confluence-link">earn more about defining hyperlinks &gt;&gt;</span> </ac:link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><strong>Severity</strong>. Select the <strong>Severity </strong>property value and from the drop-down list select one of the following severity levels:</li></ul><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>debug</strong> - for missing information of a constrained element that is dedicated for the developer.</li><li><strong>info</strong> - for missing information of a constrained element that is dedicated mostly for the end-user.</li><li><strong>warning</strong> - for incorrect situations that can cause errors. It is used for less severe situations than the error.</li><li><strong>error</strong> - for incorrect situations that must be solved.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>fatal</strong> - for critical errors such as model corruption or model is invalid according to the UML metamodel. This severity level is mostly reserved for future use.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab9e91b2-a79a-4e36-ab79-0c0cecf3cf86"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The icons on invalid elements in the model depend on the selected severity level described above. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Validation#Validation-Invalidelementsrepresentation">Learn more about invalid elements representation &gt;&gt;</a> </p><p>You can customize severity levels. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to customize severity levels >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The validation rule properties are specified.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676204 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01075: Spell Checking as you type

- page_id: `169676204`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676204/Spell+Checking+as+you+type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:40:55.155+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Spelling Checker
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

264626882

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

264626885

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

264626884

Spell Checker checks if a word is correct as you type. If the word is incorrect, it is underlined with a red winding line. Right-clicking the underlined word brings up a shortcut menu menu with suggested edits and gives you the option to add the word to the dictionary or custom glossary.

Spell checking as you type is performed in the following locations in a modeling tool:

- symbol names on a diagram pane,
- for properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] ,
- in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Documentation tab' space='MD2024xR1'] ,
- in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024xR1'] ,
- In various locations on log messages, names, typing and comment boxes.

Right-click the underlined word to invoke the shortcut menu in the following locations:

- Diagram pane [ATTACHMENT filename='spelling_diagram_pane.png']
- Containment tree [ATTACHMENT filename='spelling_containment_tree.png']
- Specification windows [ATTACHMENT filename='spelling_Specification_window.png']

Correct the spelling error

Do one of the following:

- An incorrect word can be changed by typing or by selecting provided suggestion that is always correct syntactically, but not always correct semantically.
- The Ignore command. If you select Ignore , the incorrectly spelled word is treated as correct for this time but will be considered to be incorrectly spelled in other cases.
- The Add to dictionary command. If you select Add to dictionary , the incorrect word will be added to a custom dictionary. Next time it will not be treated as incorrect.
- The Add to New Glossary command. Select Add to New Glossary to create a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Glossary table' space='MD2024xR1'] of terms.

#### INFO: Note

Note

Incorrectly spelled words are underlined only in the edit mode of a particular component. If you turn edit mode off, the underline disappears and vice versa.

264626881

**Related Pages:**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spelling Checker' space='MD2024xR1']**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d59100b-26de-4989-8810-a15a07f69024"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">264626882</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9adb2cb2-23b0-454c-a133-23ae4a698123"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">264626885</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4f791b9-d39c-48bb-8cb0-e58ab6cd8a21"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">264626884</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Spell Checker checks if a word is correct as you type. If the word is incorrect, it is underlined with a red winding line. Right-clicking the underlined word brings up a shortcut menu menu with suggested edits and gives you the option to add the word to the dictionary or custom glossary.</p><p>Spell checking as you type is performed in the following locations in a modeling tool:</p><ul><li>symbol names on a diagram pane,</li><li>for properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification windows]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,</li><li>in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Documentation tab" /></ac:link>,</li><li>in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,</li><li>In various locations on log messages, names, typing and comment boxes.</li></ul><p>Right-click the underlined word to invoke the shortcut menu in the following locations:</p><ul><li>Diagram pane<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="spelling_diagram_pane.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Spell Checking as you type" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Containment tree<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="spelling_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Spell Checking as you type" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Specification windows<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="spelling_Specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Spell Checking as you type" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>Correct the spelling error</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>An incorrect word can be changed by typing or by selecting provided suggestion that is always correct syntactically, but not always correct semantically.</li><li>The <strong>Ignore</strong> command. If you select <strong>Ignore</strong>, the incorrectly spelled word is treated as correct for this time but will be considered to be incorrectly spelled in other cases.</li><li>The <strong>Add to dictionary</strong> command. If you select <strong>Add to dictionary</strong>, the incorrect word will be added to a custom dictionary. Next time it will not be treated as incorrect.</li><li>The <strong>Add to New Glossary</strong> command. Select <strong>Add to New Glossary </strong>to create a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Glossary table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[glossary]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of terms.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba6c68f7-eb13-4e98-b241-488152f95fec"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Incorrectly spelled words are underlined only in the edit mode of a particular component. If you turn edit mode off, the underline disappears and vice versa. </ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9603248f-c1ef-4014-8102-de6f98eda747"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">264626881</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="80636947-51f9-4dfa-bca3-c54b3bc9a6d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Spelling Checker" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676208 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01076: Spell checking for the entire project or a selected scope

- page_id: `169676208`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676208/Spell+checking+for+the+entire+project+or+a+selected+scope
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:26:14.054+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Spelling Checker
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Correct the error

265580468

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Correct the error

265580471

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Correct the error

265580470

To check spelling for the entire project or a selected scope

1. On the main menu, click Tools > Check Spelling . The Check Spelling dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='check_spelling_dialog.png']
2. In the Check Spelling For drop-down list, select the scope. For Selection , click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] to define a project scope to check. It may be a package, a diagram, or an element.
3. Click the Spelling Options button to specify spelling options in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024xR1']**Environment Options**dialog .
4. Click the Check button. Results are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='MD2024xR1']**Validation Results** panel .

To correct an error

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the Validation Results panel, select the error and right-click it to open the shortcut menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='spelling_error_validation_results_pane.png']
  - In the Containment tree, select the element with an error (marked with a red circle and white cross inside) and right-click it to open the shortcut menu. In the shortcut menu, select **Validation** > **<Error place> Spelling**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Correct the errorClick the element name to edit and right-click it to see a spelling solution.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - On the diagram pane, select the element with an error (highlighted with a red border) and click the failure indicator to open the shortcut menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='spelling_error_shape.png']
2. In the shortcut menu of an element with an error, select the Correct command. The Spell Checker dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='spell_checker_dialog.png']
3. In the Value area, all incorrectly spelled words are underlined. Correct the words.
4. Click Next to go to the next spelling error (or Previous to return to the previous spelling error) found during the validation.
5. To close the dialog and save changes, click OK . To close the dialog without saving changes, click Cancel .

265580467

**Related Pages:**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spelling Checker' space='MD2024xR1']**

It may be a package, a diagram, or an element.</li><li>Click the <strong>Spelling Options </strong>button to specify spelling options in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Check</strong> button. Results are displayed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validation Results panel" /><ac:link-body><strong>Validation Results</strong> panel</ac:link-body></ac:link>.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>To correct an error</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>In the <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel, select the error and right-click it to open the shortcut menu.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="spelling_error_validation_results_pane.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Spell checking for the entire project or a selected scope" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, select the element with an error (marked with a red circle and white cross inside) and right-click it to open the shortcut menu. In the shortcut menu, select <strong>Validation</strong> &gt; <strong>&lt;Error place&gt; Spelling</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="spelling_chacker_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Spell checking for the entire project or a selected scope" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5baa3a6-8c07-4384-9e07-4e1cc3584508"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Correct the error</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Click the element name to edit and right-click it to see a spelling solution.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="spelling_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Spell checking for the entire project or a selected scope" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><br /></p></li><li>On the diagram pane, select the element with an error (highlighted with a red border) and click the failure indicator to open the shortcut menu.<br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="209"><ri:attachment ri:filename="spelling_error_shape.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Spell checking for the entire project or a selected scope" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>In the shortcut menu of an element with an error, select the <strong>Correct</strong> command. The <strong>Spell Checker</strong> dialog opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="spell_checker_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Spell checking for the entire project or a selected scope" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the <strong>Value</strong> area, all incorrectly spelled words are underlined. Correct the words.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to go to the next spelling error (or <strong>Previous</strong> to return to the previous spelling error) found during the validation. </li><li>To close the dialog and save changes, click <strong>OK</strong>. To close the dialog without saving changes, click <strong>Cancel</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b87765a-5b94-4639-bbd2-def3913d50c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">265580467</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="0724c9f8-a2db-492f-8b71-8b168986de28"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Spelling Checker" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 01078: Spelling Checker

- page_id: `169676203`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676203/Spelling+Checker
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:27:27.913+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1122009104

1122009106

1122009105

Spelling Checker enables you to:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spell Checking as you type' space='MD2024xR1'] . A shortcut menu provides spelling options. Right-click the word underlined in red to enter the shortcut menu and display the Spelling commands. You can also enter words in a customized dictionary.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spell checking for the entire project or a selected scope' space='MD2024xR1'] You can list all the spelling errors found in a project and easily correct them.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting the spell checking options' space='MD2024xR1'] . You can set spelling checker options, such as skipping numbers or uppercase words, in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024xR1']**Environment Options** dialog .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining properties of the customized element to be spell checked' space='MD2024xR1'] . All "Open Office" supportive spelling languages can be added to the existing ones.

1122009103

**Related pages**

## PAGE 01079: Splitting images

- page_id: `169678221`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678221/Splitting+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-08T04:09:00.985+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

2050262061

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

2050262073

2050262059

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

2050262063

If you have an image that is larger than the paper size, you can split the image into smaller tiles.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

**$image.split()** supports only the JPG and PNG image formats.

###### **$image.split($diagram.image, columns, rows)**

Split an image into rows and columns. The returned array of the image contains chunks of images arranged from left-to-right and top-to-bottom.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object. |
|  | column | int | The number of columns. Specifies -1 to automate the calculated number of columns from the paper width. |
|  | row | int | The number of rows. Specifies -1 to automate the calculated number of rows from the paper height. |
| Return |  | java.util.List<com.nomagic.magicreport.Image> | The array of the split image ordered from left-to-right and top-to-bottom. |

For example, to split an image into 3x2 tiles, type the following lines:

```text

```

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The original image vs. 3x2 tiled image.

###### **$image.split($diagram.image)**

Split an image into rows and columns by automatically calculating the number of rows and columns based on the paper size. The returned array of the image contains divided images arranged from left-to-right and top-to-bottom.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object. |
| Return |  | java.util.List<com.nomagic.magicreport.Image> | The array of the split image ordered from left-to-right and top-to-bottom. |

The returned array of the image contains chunks of images arranged from left-to-right and top-to-bottom.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 88.0173%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 8.95816%;" /><col style="width: 5.89722%;" /><col style="width: 26.7902%;" /><col style="width: 58.4106%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>column </td><td> int </td><td>The number of columns. Specifies -1 to automate the calculated number of columns from the paper width. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>row</td><td>int </td><td>The number of rows. Specifies -1 to automate the calculated number of rows from the paper height.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td><br /></td><td>java.util.List&lt;com.nomagic.magicreport.Image&gt; </td><td>The array of the split image ordered from left-to-right and top-to-bottom.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example, to split an image into 3x2 tiles, type the following lines:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96dc5580-2e90-4e00-8b1c-55357ec5c880"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($d in $Diagram)
````html
#foreach ($chunk in $image.split($d.image, 3, 2))
$chunk
#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Original Image vs. 3x2 Tiled Image" ac:alt="Original Image vs. 3x2 Tiled Image"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Normal and Split Image.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Splitting images" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The original image vs. 3x2 tiled image.</h6><h4><strong>$image.split($diagram.image)</strong></h4><p>Split an image into rows and columns by automatically calculating the number of rows and columns based on the paper size. The returned array of the image contains divided images arranged from left-to-right and top-to-bottom.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 88.1654%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 8.94309%;" /><col style="width: 5.29857%;" /><col style="width: 27.0816%;" /><col style="width: 58.7328%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object. </td></tr><tr><td>Return </td><td><br /></td><td>java.util.List&lt;com.nomagic.magicreport.Image&gt;  </td><td>The array of the split image ordered from left-to-right and top-to-bottom.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678108 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01080: Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram

- page_id: `169678108`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678108/Spreadsheet+Report+Designer+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-15T09:25:10.347+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Designer
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

983474219

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

983474231

985834246

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

983474221

#### Using the Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram

In this section, you will learn how to create the Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram and generate it to an XLSX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.

To create the Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram and generate it to an XLSX template

1. Right-click a Package in the Containment tree and select Report Designer Diagrams > Spreadsheet Report Designer Diagram . [ATTACHMENT filename='Spreadsheet Repot Designer Diagram Menu.png']
2. Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.
3. Arrange diagram elements as needed. [ATTACHMENT filename='Arranging Diagram Elements2.png']
4. In the Containment tree, right-click **Spreadsheet Report Designer Diagram** and select **Generate Template**> **XLSX Template** to generate the template. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] NoteGenerated templates get only symbols on the diagram. Elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate templates.
5. Refine more layouts, style, and code logic in Microsoft Word.
6. Add the complete template to Report Wizard.
7. Generate the report from the added template. [ATTACHMENT filename='Generating Report from Added Template.png']

#### Diagram elements

The Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive.

###### Element Row

An Element Row is represented as the **#forrow** directive for fetching a collection of elements in rows. After creating the Element Row in the diagram, you need to specify an element type as well as column number and row number in the **Element Row** wizard dialog. You can also create the header and border for the table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Element Row wizard dialog.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

When you are back to update the element type, column number, or row number in Step 1, the table in Step 2 will be recreated with the blank content.

In Step 2 of the **Element Row** wizard dialog, the blank table is created where you can directly enter and edit text and Velocity code in the table cell. You can also press **CTRL** + **SPACE** to open the Property Guide pop-up dialog. Press **CTRL** + **SPACE** again to navigate you to Report Data Variables and Helper Tools. In this dialog, it guides you to available properties that you can select for the cell.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Property Guide pop-up dialog.

You can insert or delete columns and rows by right-clicking a cell to open the context menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The context menu for inserting and deleting rows and columns.

###### Updating an Element Row

After creating an Element Row, you can open the **Element Row** wizard dialog again for updating the **Element Row** table through the smart manipulator button or the **Element Row** editing context menu.

###### Element Page

An Element Page is represented as the **#forpage** directive for fetching a collection of elements in a table per worksheet. This element uses the **Element Row** wizard dialog. Only in Step 2, the **#forpage** directive will be created over the blank table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Element Page.

###### Page Break

You can place the Page Break element on the diagram to be in the template to start a new worksheet.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Page Break.

983474217

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Designer' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Document Report Designer diagram' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679112 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01081: Standard Expert Configuration

- page_id: `169679112`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679112/Standard+Expert+Configuration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-08-16T11:49:48.417+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Creating Customization Data > Customization Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the DSL element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'], properties are hidden or displayed by using Standard, Expert and All modes.

To customize Standard Expert mode

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating your First Customization' space='MD2024xR1'] for the DSL element or select an existing one.
2. In the element Specification window, click the Standard Expert Configuration property and then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] button.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dialog for customizing selected modeling tool area' space='MD2024xR1']**Customize Properties** dialog , click to select the mode in which the property will be displayed in the DSL element Specification window.

#### NOTE: Tip!

Tip!

- The Customize Properties dialog contains the list of used UML properties and custom properties. You can select which UML properties and DSL specific properties will be displayed in the DSL element Specification window.
- You can also customize property groups. In the Customize Properties dialog, property groups are displayed at the end of the list.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can select which UML properties and DSL specific properties will be displayed in the DSL element Specification window. </span></li><li>You can also customize property groups. In the <strong>Customize Properties</strong> dialog, property groups are displayed at the end of the list.</li></ul></div></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68d6f843-19aa-480d-81e1-df3547d11a1b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Customization Data" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using Customization Data" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
