<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675780 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01095: Stereotype properties

- page_id: `169675780`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675780/Stereotype+properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:16.769+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Quick search

253159177

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Quick search

253159190

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Quick search

253159180

The Stereotype properties can be applied only if the stereotype properties style is created in the **Project Options** dialog.

The Stereotype properties are derived from their base class. The Stereotype base class is defined in the label of the right pane of the **Project Options** dialog.

The same element can have several stereotypes assigned. In this case, the style of the first stereotype will be applied to the element symbol. If the stereotype is removed from the element, the next (first) stereotype properties are applied. If the last stereotype is removed from the element, the base class (shape or path) properties are applied to the element symbol.

Stereotypes may be extended by diagram.

All stereotypes that have defined symbol properties are included in the **Stereotypes** property group. The default style is created for these stereotypes.

Adding stereotypeTo add a stereotype to the Stereotypes option group

1. In the Project Options dialog > Symbol styles , select Stereotypes and from the shortcut menu, choose Add/Remove Stereotype . Alternatively, you can simply click the Add/Remove Stereotype button. The Select Stereotypes dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_options_stereotype.png']
2. Select the stereotype(s) you want to add and click the OK button. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_stereotypes.png']

The stereotypes are included into the **Stereotypes** option group, and you can now set the stereotype style properties in the right pane of the **Project Options** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once you are done customizing your stereotype style, click **Apply** to apply changes to existing shapes in the selected diagram type, or click **OK** to apply changes to future shapes.

To remove a stereotype from the options group

1. In the Project Options dialog > Symbol styles , expand the Stereotypes options group. The list of stereotypes opens.
2. Select a stereotype you want to remove and click the Remove button.

To apply the stereotype properties to a previously created symbol with the assigned stereotype

1. In the Project Options dialog, after changing the stereotype style properties, click the **Apply** button. The **Select Diagrams** dialog opens. Customized styleYour customized style will only be used in the diagrams of the selected type.
2. Select the diagrams to which the stereotype properties will be applied and click OK .
3. In the **Select Properties to Apply** dialog, select which properties will be applied to the stereotype. Click **OK**. Quick searchUse the [Quick search](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Quick+filter) feature to find the desired property.

253159176

## PAGE 01096: Stereotypes from Old Project Version

- page_id: `169679454`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679454/Stereotypes+from+Old+Project+Version
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:34:53.986+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > C++ Code Engineering > Conversion from an Earlier Project Version
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1398323595

1398323598

1398323597

##### «C++EnumerationLiteral»

This example shows anEnumClassclass with a literal value namedliteral. This literal value applies the«C++EnumerationLiteral» stereotype and sets C++Initializer tag value to 0.

Figure 86 -- «C++EnumerationLiteral» stereotype Example in Class Diagram

The «C++EnumerationLiteral» stereotype is shown in the figure below.

Figure 87 -- «C++EnumerationLiteral» stereotype

The C++Initializer tag value is shown in the figure below.

Figure 88 -- Enumeration Literal Tag Value

| Old value | Translation |
| --- | --- |
| Enumeration Literal applies the «C++EnumerationLiteral» stereotype and sets C++Initializer tag value to 0. | Apply the «C++LiteralValue» and set the value tag value to 0. Remove the «C++EnumerationLiteral» stereotype and C++Initializer tag value from Enumeration Literal. |

The Model shown in the figure below is a translation.

Figure 89 -- Translated Enumeration Literal in Class Diagram

##### «C++Namespace»

This example is theMyPackagepackage. It applies the«C++Namespace» stereotype in the old profile and sets theunique namespace name tag value to myNamespace.

Figure 90 -- «C++Namespace» stereotype Example in Class Diagram

The«C++Namespace» stereotype is shown in the figure below.

Figure 91 -- «C++Namespace» stereotype

Theunique namespace nametag value is shown in the figure below.

Figure 92 -- unique namespace name tag value

| Old value | Translation |
| --- | --- |
| Package applies the «C++Namespace» ste- reotype in the old profile (C++ Profile) and sets the unique namespace name tag value to myNamespace. | Apply the «C++Namespace» stereotype in a new profile (c++ ANSI profile) and set the unique namespace name tag value to myNamespace. Remove the «C++Namespace» stereotype (C++ Profile) and unique namespace name tag value. |

##### «C++Typename»

The«C++Typename» stereotype can apply to Template Parameters. Therefore, Elements with template parameters could apply this stereotype.

Figure 93 -- Elements that can have template parameters

In version 12.0, all elements that have template parameters must apply the«C++TemplateParameter» stereotype.

If elements from old version apply the«C++Typename» stereotype, the translation will apply the«C++TemplateParameter» stereotype and setthe type keywordtag value totypename. If not, the translation will apply the«C++TemplateParameter» stereotype and settype keywordtag value toclass.

Figure 94 -- «C++Typename» stereotype

The«C++TemplateParameter» stereotype is shown in the figure below.

Figure 95 -- «C++TemplateParamater» stereotype

| Old value | Translation |
| --- | --- |
| Template Parameter does not apply the «C++Typename» stereotype. | Apply the «C++TemplateParameter» stereotype and set type keyword tag value to class. |
| Template Parameter applies the «C++Typename» stereotype. | Apply the «C++TemplateParameter» stereotype and set type keyword tag value to typename. Remove the «C++Typename» stereotype. |

##### «constructor» and «destructor» in UML Standard Profile

These examples are UMLStandardConstructorClass classes that have an operation named myOperation(), which applies the «constructor» stereotype in a UML Standard Profile and UMLStandardDestructorClass class with an operation named myOperation() which applies the «destructor» stereotype in UML Standard Profile.

Figure 96 -- «constructor» and «destructor» stereotype Example in Class Diagram

**«constructor»**

The«constructor» stereotype is shown in the figure below.

Figure 97 -- «constructor» stereotype

| Old value | Translation |
| --- | --- |
| Operation applies the «constructor» stereotype in UML Standard Profile. | Apply the «C++Constructor» stereotype (c++ ANSI profile). Remove the «constructor» stereotype (UML Standard Profile). |

**«destructor»**

The «destructor» stereotype is shown in the figure below.

Figure 98 -- «destructor» stereotype

| Old value | Translation |
| --- | --- |
| Operation applies the «destructor» stereotype in UML Standard Profile. | Apply the «C++Destructor» stereotype (c++ ANSI profile). Remove the «destructor» stereotype (UML Standard Profile). |

1398323593

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677692 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01097: Stop using 3DEXPERIENCE project in your project

- page_id: `169677692`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677692/Stop+using+3DEXPERIENCE+project+in+your+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-01-19T07:03:28.533+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Managing project usages via 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you no longer need to use a project, you can easily remove it from your project usages.

Removal is complete; all references to the elements stored in the used project will be eliminated.

Before you attempt to stop using a project, make sure other users are not currently creating usages to this project.

To stop using a project in your project

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space='MD2022x'] you want to stop using.
2. On the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. Select the used project and click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. Shortcut menuThis step can be done via a shortcut menu. Right-click the project and then select **Remove**.**** [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Wait while the removal completes.
5. Click OK .

The project is no longer used in your project and will disappear from the Model Browser of your project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677463 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01098: Stop using the project in your project

- page_id: `169677463`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677463/Stop+using+the+project+in+your+project
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-08-28T11:51:32.004+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To stop using the project in your project, you must have[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR1']the **Administer Resources** permission.

If you no longer need to use a project, you can easily remove it from your project usages.

Removal can be:

- Complete, after which all references to the elements stored in the used project are eliminated.
- Incomplete, after which all references are preserved. Choose this method when you need to replace the used project with an equivalent project containing the same elements with slightly different content.

Before you attempt to stop using a project, make sure other users are not currently creating usages to this project.

To stop using a project in your project

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space=''] you want to stop using.
2. On the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. Select the used project and click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. Shortcut menuThis step can be done via shortcut menu. Right-click project and then select **Remove**.**** [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Wait while the removal completes.
5. Click OK .

The project is no longer used in your project. It will disappear from the Model Browser of your project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="660e2072-9c67-4bfd-a314-4d8ce037351b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To stop using the project in your project, you must have<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Permissions" /><ac:link-body> the <strong>Administer Resources</strong> permission</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If you no longer need to use a project, you can easily remove it from your project usages.</p><p>Removal can be:</p><ul><li>Complete, after which all references to the elements stored in the used project are eliminated.</li><li>Incomplete, after which all references are preserved. Choose this method when you need to replace the used project with an equivalent project containing the same elements with slightly different content.</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1bc44b2c-e21f-4931-96fe-ba85a84166b3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before you attempt to stop using a project, make sure other users are not currently creating usages to this project.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To stop using a project in your project</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Locking model for edit" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock the used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you want to stop using.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong> Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the used project and click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac0e76a5-8ae3-4829-8bdb-a3a08b2944bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Shortcut menu</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step can be done via shortcut menu. Right-click project and then select <strong>Remove</strong>.<strong> </strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_usages_remove.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>Wait while the removal completes.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>The project is no longer used in your project. It will disappear from the Model Browser of your project.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674834 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01099: Stopping to use diagram as layout template

- page_id: `169674834`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674834/Stopping+to+use+diagram+as+layout+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-10-02T10:48:36.707+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Layout templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you no longer want to use a diagram as a layout template, you need to remove the «layoutTemplate» stereotype from it. To do this, follow the steps below.

To stop using a diagram as a layout template

1. Ensure that nothing is selected on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR1']
2. Right click the diagram and select **Stereotype**.
3. In the **Stereotype** dialog, clear the **layoutTemplate** check box. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click **Apply**.

The «layoutTemplate» stereotype disappears from the header of the diagram, and it can no longer be applied as a layout template.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If you no longer want to use a diagram as a layout template, you need to remove the «layoutTemplate» stereotype from it. To do this, follow the steps below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To stop using a diagram as a layout template</p><hr /><ol><li>Ensure that nothing is selected on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><span>Right click the diagram and select <strong>Stereotype</strong>.</span></li><li><span>In the <strong>Stereotype</strong> dialog, clear the <strong>layoutTemplate</strong> check box.<br /><br /><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="stop_using_layout_template.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Stopping to use diagram as layout template" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span><br /></span></li><li><span>Click <strong>Apply</strong>.</span><span><br /></span></li></ol><p><span><br />The «layoutTemplate» stereotype disappears from the header of the diagram, and it can no longer be applied as a layout template.</span></p>
````

## PAGE 01100: Structure tab

- page_id: `169673274`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673274/Structure+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:48.003+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1422121065

1422121073

1422121068

This tab will appear in the Window menu only if the SysML plugin is installed.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Structure tab' space='SYSMLP2024xR1']true

##### Structure tab toolbar

The Structure tab toolbar contains the following buttons:

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all of the currently expanded elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Open in New Tree | Opens the contents of the selected element in a new tab of the Model Browser. The selected element becomes the root node of the new tree.You can choose to open elements in:New Containment TreeNew Structure TreeNew Inheritance Tree |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favorites Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Quick Find | Opens the Quick Find dialog. |
|  | Options | Click to open the Options menu and then select:Filter - opens the Select Element Type dialog wherein you can select element types to be visible in the Containment tree. Learn more on the page.Show Auxiliary Resources - shows or hides modules and profiles that are used in your project.Show Applied Stereotypes - shows or hides the applied stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree.Nest Satisfied Requirements - shows requirements nested within elements that satisfy them.Show Inherited Structure - shows properties that are inherited from the generalization classifier.Show Full Types - shows or hides the full types of typed elements (for example, operations, attributes, relationships, and other) in the Containment tree.Show Code Engineering Sets - shows or hides the code engineering sets. |

1422121064

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675280 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01101: Structured Activity Node

- page_id: `169675280`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675280/Structured+Activity+Node
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-03-18T07:35:08.069+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Node
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Structured Activity Node is an executable activity node that may have an expansion into subordinate nodes as an activity group. The subordinate nodes must belong to only one Structured Activity Node, although they may be nested.

To create a Structured Activity Node

1. Expand the Activity diagram palette and click the Structured Activity Node icon . [ATTACHMENT filename='select_structured_activity_node.png']
2. Click on the Activity diagram pane. The Structured Activity Node is created.
3. (Optional) To specify the Structured Activity Node, right-click the symbol and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A variable is defined in the scope of the Structured Activity Node. It has no value and may not be accessed outside the node.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>A Structured Activity Node is an executable activity node that may have an expansion into subordinate nodes as an activity group. The subordinate nodes must belong to only one Structured Activity Node, although they may be nested.</span></p><p>To create a <span>Structured Activity Node </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Expand the Activity diagram palette and click the <strong> <span>Structured Activity Node </span> </strong> <span>icon</span>.<br /> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_structured_activity_node.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Structured Activity Node" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click on the Activity diagram pane. The <span>Structured Activity Node</span> is created. </li><li>(Optional) To specify the Structured Activity Node, right-click the symbol and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link><span>.  </span></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Structured_activity_in_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Structured Activity Node" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A variable is defined in the scope of the Structured Activity Node. It has no value and may not be accessed outside the node.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679109 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01102: Sub Element Contents Included

- page_id: `169679109`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679109/Sub+Element+Contents+Included
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-08-16T11:49:48.380+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Creating Customization Data > Customization Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the **Sub Element Contents Included** property to specify whether the content of a desired element typeincludes only directly related elements or both directly and indirectly related ones.

The property value can be either:

- None : the content includes only directly related elements. [ATTACHMENT filename='not_include_subelements_in_content.png']

- All : the content includes both directly and indirectly related elements. [ATTACHMENT filename='include_subelements_in_content.png']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Element Properties Description' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>Use the </span> <strong>Sub Element Contents Included </strong> <span>property to specify whether the content of a desired element type </span>includes only directly related elements or both directly and indirectly related ones.</p><p>The property value can be either:</p><ul><li><em>None</em>: the content includes only directly related elements.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="not_include_subelements_in_content.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sub Element Contents Included" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><ul><li><em>All</em>: the content includes both directly and indirectly related elements.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="include_subelements_in_content.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sub Element Contents Included" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span> <br /> </span></p></div></div></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68d6f843-19aa-480d-81e1-df3547d11a1b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating Customization Data" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using Customization Data" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customization Elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customization Element Properties Description" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675459 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01103: Substitution

- page_id: `169675459`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675459/Substitution
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-02T15:03:47.864+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A substitution is a relationship between two [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the classifier' space='MD2024xR1']. In these cases, the substituting Classifier complies with the contract specified by the contract classifier. Instances of the substituting Classifier are runtime substitutable where instances of the contract classifier are expected. 
Specify the Substitution element in the Substitution [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'].

You can see which elements the Substitution is drawn between in the Substitution dialog, Source and Target boxes.

You can format the Substitution symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR1'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A substitution is a relationship between two <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying the classifier" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Classifiers]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. In these cases, the substituting Classifier complies with the contract specified by the contract classifier. Instances of the substituting Classifier are runtime substitutable where instances of the contract classifier are expected.  </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Specify the Substitution element in the Substitution <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">. </span></p><p>You can see which elements the Substitution is drawn between in the <span class="hcp1">Substitution</span> dialog, <span class="hcp1">Source</span> and <span class="hcp1">Target</span> boxes.</p><p>You can format the Substitution symbol properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675460 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01104: Subsystem

- page_id: `169675460`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A subsystem is treated as an abstract single unit. It groups model elements by representing the behavioral unit in a physical system.

To draw a subsystem

- On the Use Case diagram palette, click the Subsystem button, and then click the diagram pane.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024xR1']

#### Background

The shorthand property for the background property is 'background', which is used for setting an individual background style. Such attribute can be used in *RTF, ODT,* and *DOCX* report templates.

##### Color Specification

Color attributes such as background-color, can be specified by either a keyword or a numerical RGB specification (hexadecimal, integer, and percentage value). The list of Color Keywords and Corresponding RGB Specification is displayed in the table below.

| Color | RGB SpecificationHexadecimal Code | RGB Specification Integer | RGB Specification Percentage |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Maroon | #800000 | RGB(128,0,0) | RGB(50%,0%,0%) |
| Red | #FF0000 | RGB(255,0,0) | RGB(100%,0%,0%) |
| Orange | #FFA500 | RGB(255,165,0) | RGB(100%,65%,0%) |
| Yellow | #FFFF00 | RGB(255,255,0) | RGB(100%,100%,0%) |
| Olive | #808000 | RGB(128,128,0) | RGB(50%,50%,0%) |
| Purple | #800080 | RGB(128,0,128) | RGB(50%,0%,50%) |
| Fuchsia | #FF00FF | RGB(255,0,255) | RGB(100%,0%,100%) |
| White | #FFFFFF | RGB(255,255,255) | RGB(100%,100%,100%) |
| Lime | #00FF00 | RGB(0,255,0) | RGB(0%,100%,0%) |
| Green | #008000 | RGB(0,128,0) | RGB(0%,50%,0%) |
| Navy | #000080 | RGB(0,0,128) | RGB(0%,0%,50%) |
| Blue | #0000FF | RGB(0,0,255) | RGB(0%,0%,100%) |
| Aqua | #00FFFF | RGB(0,255,255) | RGB(0%,100%,100%) |
| Teal | #008080 | RGB(0,128,128) | RGB(0%,50%,50%) |
| Black | #000000 | RGB(0,0,0) | RGB(0%,0%,0%) |
| Silver | #C0C0C0 | RGB(192,192,192) | RGB(75%,75%,75%) |
| Gray | #808080 | RGB(128,128,128) | RGB(50%,50%,50%) |

The format of an RGB specification value in form of the hexadecimal notation is a '#' immediately followed by either three or six hexadecimal characters. The three-digit RGB notation (#rgb) is converted to the six-digit form (#rrggbb) by replicating digits. For example, #FB0 is converted to #FFBB00.

For example:

<p style = “background-color:#FB0”> 
<p style = “background-color:#FFBB00”>

The RGB specification Integer and Percentage form is "rgb" followed by RGB values in the same format in brackets. You cannot use both integer and percentage at the same time. In the case that an RGB value is more than a maximum value (255 or 100%) or less than a minimum (0 or 0%), the value will be set to the maximum or minimum value instead, for example:

<p style = "background-color:rgb(-100,500,0)">

<p style = "background-color:rgb(0,255,0)">

<p style = "background-colr:rgb(-100%,200%,0%)">

<p style = "background-colr:rgb(0%,100%,0%)">

The color used in all of the above examples is lime.

##### Supported Tags

The ‘background-color’ property can be used in the following tags:

```text
b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example:

#### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Border

The shorthand property for the border property is 'border', which is used for setting the same width, color, and style of all four borders on four sides of a box. Border can be used in *RTF, ODT*, and *DOCX* report templates.

##### Border Width

Border width properties specify the width of border areas. The value of the border property is length. The border width properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:

- 'border-top-width'
- 'border-right-width'
- 'border-bottom-width'
- 'border-left-width'

##### Length Specification

The format of a length value is a real number immediately followed by a unit identifier. There are two types of length units: relative and absolute. Relative length units specify a length relative to another length property of the displaying device.

*Relative units* are:

- 
  - px pixels, relative to the displaying device.

*Absolute units* are:

- 
  - in inches, 1 inch is equal to 2.54 centimeters.
  - cm centimeters.
  - mm millimeters.
  - pt points - the points used by CSS 2.1 are equal to 1/72nd of an inch.
  - pc picas - 1 pica is equal to 12 points.

##### Border Color

Border color properties specify the border colors of a box. The value of the border color has two meanings:

- Color Specifies a border color. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom renderers sample' space='MD2024xR1'] for more details on color.
- Transparent Referred to as a transparent border.

The border color properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:

- 'border-top-color'
- 'border-right-color'
- 'border-bottom-color'
- 'border-left-color'

##### Border Style

The Border style properties specify the style of a box's border lines. The border style may take one of the following 10 values:

1. none This value will create no border because the computed border width is zero.
2. hidden The same as 'none'.
3. dotted This value will create a dotted border.
4. dashed This value will create a dashed border.
5. solid This value will create a single line segment border.
6. double This value will create a two solid line border.
7. groove This value will create a border that looks as though it were carved into the rendering surface.
8. ridge The opposite of 'groove'. This value will create a border that looks as though it were coming out of the rendering surface.
9. inset This value will create a border that looks as though it were embedded in the rendering surface.
10. outset The opposite of 'inset'. This value will create a border that looks as though it were coming out of the rendering surface.

- The supported border values for ODF documents are none , hidden , solid , and double .
- The supported border values for RTF documents are none , hidden , dotted , dashed , solid , and double .
- The supported border values for DOCX documents are none , hidden , dotted , dashed , solid , double , outset , and inset ( groove and ridge will be rendered as solid).

The border style properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:

- 'border-top-style'
- 'border-right-style'
- 'border-bottom-style'
- 'border-left-style'

##### Supported Tags

Border properties can be used in the following tags:

```text
h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, td, th, p, and div.
```

For example, if you want to have a red border, type the following line of code, see the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Margin

Margin properties specify the width of margin area within a box. The 'margin' property is a shorthand property for setting the margin of all four sides while the other margin properties only set their respective side. The value of the margin property is length (See Length Specification for more details). Margin can be used in *RTF, ODT, ODP, DOCX* and *PPTX* report templates.

The margin properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:

- 'margin-top'
- 'margin-right'
- 'margin-bottom'
- 'margin-left'

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for margins are as follows:

```text
h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, table (supported only in RTF, ODT, and DOCX), p, and div.
```

For example, if you want to set a specific margin width for your cells, type the lines of code as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Padding

Padding properties specify the width of padding area within a box. The 'padding' property is a shorthand property for setting the padding for all four sides while the other padding properties only set their respective side. The value of the padding property is length (See Length Specification for more details) adding can be used in *RTF, ODT* and *DOCX* report templates.

The padding properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:

- 'padding-top'
- 'padding-right'
- 'padding-bottom'
- 'padding-left'

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for padding are as follows:

```text
h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, td, th, p, and div.
```

- The supported border values of table tags for RTF documents are margin-left and margin-right.for more details.
- The supported border values of table tags for DOCX documents are margin-left and margin-right.
- The supported border values of all tags for PPTX documents are margin-left, margin-top and margin-bottom.

For example, if you want to create specific padding styles for your cells, type the lines of code shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Color

The 'color' property specifies the foreground color of an element's text contents. Only color values are allowed in this property (See Color Specification for more details on color).

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for colors are as follows:

```text
b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example, if you want to have a green text color, type the lines of code indicated in figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Display

The 'display' property specifies how text will be displayed. The possible display values are:

- block : This value will make an element appear in the output report.
- none : This value will make an element disappear from the output report.

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for the display property are:

```text
br, b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li (supported only in RTF, DOCX, and PPTX), table, tr, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example, if you want to display an element but do not want to display the other(s), type the lines of code as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as indicated below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Font

The 'font' property is a shorthand property for setting, except the following:

- 'font family'
- 'font-style'
- 'font-variant'
- 'font-weight'
- 'font-size'

##### Font Family

Specify a prioritized list of font family names and/or generic family names.

##### Font Style

Specify either normal or italic face (within the specified font family).

##### Font Variant

Specify either normal or small-caps of variant (within the specified font family). Font variant is not supported in *XLSX* report templates.

##### Font Weight

Specifies the weight of the font. The following values are defined:

- normal , lighter , 100 , 200 , 300 , and 400 will be rendered as ‘ normal ’.
- bold , bolder , 500 , 600 , 700 , 800 , and 900 will be rendered as ‘ bold ’.

##### Font size

Specify the font size. Possible values include xx-small, x-small, small, medium, large, xx-large, and the integer number from 1 to 7.

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for the font property are as follows:

```text
b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example, if you want to have bold fonts in small caps, type the lines of code as displayed in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Text Align

The 'text align' property describes how inline content of a block is aligned. Possible values of the text align property include: **left**, **right**, **center** and **justify**. The ‘text align’ property can be used in *RTF, ODT, ODP, DOCX,* and *PPTX* report templates.

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for the text align property are as follows:

```text
table, tr, td, th, p, and div.
```

For example, if you want to align your paragraph to the left, center, and right, type the lines of code as shown in the Figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as displayed in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Text Transform

The 'text transform' property controls capitalization effects of an element's text. Possible values of the 'text transform' property include:

1. capitalize This will write the first character in an uppercase letter. Other characters will not be affected.
2. uppercase This will write each word in upper case letters.
3. lowercase This will write each word in lower case letters.
4. none This will not create any capitalization effects.

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for the text transform property are as follows:

```text
b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example, if you want to make all letters in a paragraph become uppercases, type the line of code displayed in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### White-Space

The 'white-space' property affects the vertical position of white-space inside a lined box. White-space can be used in *RTF, ODT, DOCX* and *PPTX* report templates. Possible values of the 'white-space' property include:

1. normal To collapse sequences of white-space and break lines as necessary to fill line boxes.
2. pre To prevent from collapsing sequences of white-space. Lines are only broken at newlines in the source or at occurrences of "\A" in the generated content. Report Wizard will render this value as the <pre> tag.
3. nowrap To collapse white-space as for 'normal' but suppressing line breaks within text.
4. pre-wrap To prevent user agents from collapsing sequences of white-space. Lines are broken at newlines in the source, at occurrences of "\A" in the generated content, and as necessary to fill line boxes. This value will not be rendered as pre.
5. pre-line To direct user agents to collapse sequences of white-space. Lines are broken at newlines in the source, at occurrences of "\A" in the generated content, and as necessary to fill line boxes. This value will not be rendered as pre.

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for the 'white-space' property are as follows:

```text
b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example, if you want to preserve some white-space, type these lines of code as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Width

The 'width' property specifies the contents width of each box generated by a block-level. The value of the width property is length (See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating runtime objects from instance specifications' space='CST'] for more details). Width can be used in *RTF, ODT,* and *DOCX* report templates.

The supported tag for the width property includes table.

For example, if you want to have a specific table width, type the lines of code as described in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Text Decoration

The 'text decoration' property describes decorations that are added to text. The possible values of the text decoration property are as follows:

- none To produce no text decoration.
- underline To underline each line of text.
- overline To add a line above each line of text.
- line-through To add a line that strikes through each line of text.
- blink To add the blinking effect to text (being visible and invisible alternatively). The blink text decoration supports only the ODT report template.

Report Wizard does not currently support the overline text decoration.

##### Supported Tags

The supported tags for the text decoration property are:

```text
b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.
```

For example, if you want to create a line-through text, type the line of code as displayed in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Vertical Align

The 'vertical align' property affects the vertical position of an element inside a lined box. This property can be used in *RTF, ODT* and *DOCX* report templates. Possible values of the vertical align property include:

- baseline This value will align the baseline of a box with the baseline of the parent box. Leave the vertical alignment to be default when you encounter this value.
- middle This value will align the vertical midpoint of a box.
- top This value will align the top of the aligned sub-tree with the top of a line box.
- bottom This value will align the bottom of the aligned sub-tree with the bottom of a line box.

The supported tags for the vertical align property are: table, tr, td, and th, for example, see figure below:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For example, #FB0 is converted to #FFBB00.</p><p>For example:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = “background-color:#FB0”&gt;<br />&lt;p style = “background-color:#FFBB00”&gt;</p><p>The RGB specification Integer and Percentage form is &quot;rgb&quot; followed by RGB values in the same format in brackets. You cannot use both integer and percentage at the same time. In the case that an RGB value is more than a maximum value (255 or 100%) or less than a minimum (0 or 0%), the value will be set to the maximum or minimum value instead, for example:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = &quot;background-color:rgb(-100,500,0)&quot;&gt;</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = &quot;background-color:rgb(0,255,0)&quot;&gt;</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = &quot;background-colr:rgb(-100%,200%,0%)&quot;&gt;</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = &quot;background-colr:rgb(0%,100%,0%)&quot;&gt;</p><p>The color used in all of the above examples is lime.</p><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The ‘background-color’ property can be used in the following tags:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub. </pre><p>For example:</p><h2><ac:image ac:title="Sample of Background Style" ac:alt="Sample of Background Style"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h2><h2><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Border</span></h2><p>The shorthand property for the border property is 'border', which is used for setting the same width, color, and style of all four borders on four sides of a box. Border can be used in <em>RTF, ODT</em>, and <em>DOCX</em> report templates.</p><h3>Border Width</h3><p>Border width properties specify the width of border areas. The value of the border property is length. The border width properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li><pre>'border-top-width'</pre></li><li><pre>'border-right-width'</pre></li><li><pre>'border-bottom-width'</pre></li><li><pre>'border-left-width'</pre></li></ul><h3>Length Specification</h3><p>The format of a length value is a real number immediately followed by a unit identifier. There are two types of length units: relative and absolute. Relative length units specify a length relative to another length property of the displaying device.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>Relative units</em> are:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>px</strong> <br />pixels, relative to the displaying device.</li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>Absolute units</em> are:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>in</strong> <br />inches, 1 inch is equal to 2.54 centimeters.</li><li><strong>cm<br /></strong>centimeters.</li><li><strong>mm<br /></strong>millimeters.</li><li><strong>pt<br /></strong>points - the points used by CSS 2.1 are equal to 1/72nd of an inch.</li><li><strong>pc<br /></strong>picas - 1 pica is equal to 12 points.</li></ul></li></ul><h3>Border Color</h3><p>Border color properties specify the border colors of a box. The value of the border color has two meanings:</p><ul><li><strong>Color<br /></strong>Specifies a border color. See <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Custom renderers sample" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Color Specification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for more details on color.</li><li><strong>Transparent<br /></strong>Referred to as a transparent border.</li></ul><p>The border color properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li><pre>'border-top-color'</pre></li><li><pre>'border-right-color'</pre></li><li><pre>'border-bottom-color'</pre></li><li><pre>'border-left-color'</pre></li></ul><h3>Border Style</h3><p>The Border style properties specify the style of a box's border lines. The border style may take one of the following 10 values:</p><ol><li><strong>none<br /></strong>This value will create no border because the computed border width is zero.</li><li><strong>hidden<br /></strong>The same as 'none'.</li><li><strong>dotted<br /></strong>This value will create a dotted border.</li><li><strong>dashed<br /></strong>This value will create a dashed border.</li><li><strong>solid<br /></strong>This value will create a single line segment border.</li><li><strong>double<br /></strong>This value will create a two solid line border.</li><li><strong>groove<br /></strong>This value will create a border that looks as though it were carved into the rendering surface.</li><li><strong>ridge<br /></strong>The opposite of 'groove'. This value will create a border that looks as though it were coming out of the rendering surface.</li><li><strong>inset<br /></strong>This value will create a border that looks as though it were embedded in the rendering surface.</li><li><strong>outset<br /></strong>The opposite of 'inset'. This value will create a border that looks as though it were coming out of the rendering surface.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="786398f3-dd2e-487f-91ee-979b67f3ca16"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The supported border values for <em>ODF</em> documents are <strong>none</strong>, <strong>hidden</strong>, <strong>solid</strong>, and <strong>double</strong>.</li><li>The supported border values for <em>RTF</em> documents are <strong>none</strong>, <strong>hidden</strong>,<strong>dotted</strong>, <strong>dashed</strong>, <strong>solid</strong>, and <strong>double</strong>.</li><li>The supported border values for <em>DOCX</em> documents are <strong>none</strong>, <strong>hidden</strong>,<strong>dotted</strong>, <strong>dashed</strong>,<strong> solid</strong>,<strong> double</strong>, <strong>outset</strong>, and <strong>inset</strong> (<strong>groove</strong> and <strong>ridge</strong> will be rendered as solid).</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The border style properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li><pre>'border-top-style'</pre></li><li><pre>'border-right-style'</pre></li><li><pre>'border-bottom-style'</pre></li><li><pre>'border-left-style'</pre></li></ul><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>Border properties can be used in the following tags:</p><pre>h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, td, th, p, and div.</pre><p>For example, if you want to have a red border, type the following line of code, see the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Border Style Example" ac:alt="Border Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Red Border Output" ac:alt="Red Border Output"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Margin</h2><p>Margin properties specify the width of margin area within a box. The 'margin' property is a shorthand property for setting the margin of all four sides while the other margin properties only set their respective side. The value of the margin property is length (See Length Specification for more details). Margin can be used in <em>RTF, ODT, ODP, DOCX</em> and <em>PPTX</em> report templates.</p><p>The margin properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li><pre>'margin-top'</pre></li><li><pre>'margin-right'</pre></li><li><pre>'margin-bottom'</pre></li><li><pre>'margin-left'</pre></li></ul><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for margins are as follows:</p><pre>h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, table (supported only in RTF, ODT, and DOCX), p, and div.</pre><p>For example, if you want to set a specific margin width for your cells, type the lines of code as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Margin Style Example" ac:alt="Margin Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_5.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Margin Style Outputs" ac:alt="Margin Style Outputs"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_6.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Padding</h2><p>Padding properties specify the width of padding area within a box. The 'padding' property is a shorthand property for setting the padding for all four sides while the other padding properties only set their respective side. The value of the padding property is length (See Length Specification for more details) adding can be used in <em>RTF, ODT</em> and <em>DOCX</em> report templates.</p><p>The padding properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li>'padding-top'</li><li>'padding-right'</li><li>'padding-bottom'</li><li>'padding-left'</li></ul><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for padding are as follows:</p><pre>h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, td, th, p, and div.</pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1a21422-2bf3-4c82-b1b3-047b6b18433a"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The supported border values of table tags for <em>RTF</em> documents are margin-left and margin-right.for more details.</li><li>The supported border values of table tags for <em>DOCX</em> documents are margin-left and margin-right.</li><li>The supported border values of all tags for <em>PPTX</em> documents are margin-left, margin-top and margin-bottom.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example, if you want to create specific padding styles for your cells, type the lines of code shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Padding Style Example" ac:alt="Padding Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_7.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Padding Style Outputs" ac:alt="The Padding Style Outputs"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_8.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Color</h2><p>The 'color' property specifies the foreground color of an element's text contents. Only color values are allowed in this property (See Color Specification for more details on color).</p><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for colors are as follows:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to have a green text color, type the lines of code indicated in figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Color Style Example" ac:alt="Color Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_9.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Color Style Outputs" ac:alt="Color Style Outputs"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Display</h2><p>The 'display' property specifies how text will be displayed. The possible display values are:</p><ul><li><strong>block</strong>: This value will make an element appear in the output report.</li><li><strong>none</strong>: This value will make an element disappear from the output report.</li></ul><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the display property are:</p><pre>br, b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li (supported only in RTF, DOCX, and PPTX), table, tr, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to display an element but do not want to display the other(s), type the lines of code as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Display Style Example" ac:alt="Display Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_11.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Display Style Output" ac:alt="Display Style Output"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_12.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Font</h2><p>The 'font' property is a shorthand property for setting, except the following:</p><ul><li><pre>'font family'</pre></li><li><pre>'font-style'</pre></li><li><pre>'font-variant'</pre></li><li><pre>'font-weight'</pre></li><li><pre>'font-size'</pre></li></ul><h3>Font Family</h3><p>Specify a prioritized list of font family names and/or generic family names.</p><h3>Font Style</h3><p>Specify either normal or italic face (within the specified font family).</p><h3>Font Variant</h3><p>Specify either normal or small-caps of variant (within the specified font family). Font variant is not supported in <em>XLSX</em> report templates.</p><h3>Font Weight</h3><p>Specifies the weight of the font. The following values are defined:</p><ul><li><strong>normal</strong>, <strong>lighter</strong>, <strong>100</strong>, <strong>200</strong>,<strong> 300</strong>, and <strong>400</strong> will be rendered as ‘<strong>normal</strong>’.</li><li><strong>bold</strong>, <strong>bolder</strong>, <strong>500</strong>, <strong>600</strong>, <strong>700</strong>,<strong> 800</strong>, and<strong> 900</strong> will be rendered as ‘<strong>bold</strong>’.</li></ul><h3>Font size</h3><p>Specify the font size. Possible values include xx-small, x-small, small, medium, large, xx-large, and the integer number from 1 to 7.</p><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the font property are as follows:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to have bold fonts in small caps, type the lines of code as displayed in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Font Style Example" ac:alt="Font Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_13.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Font Style Output" ac:alt="Font Style Output"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_14.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Text Align</h2><p>The 'text align' property describes how inline content of a block is aligned. Possible values of the text align property include: <strong>left</strong>, <strong>right</strong>, <strong>center</strong> and <strong>justify</strong>. The ‘text align’ property can be used in <em>RTF, ODT, ODP, DOCX,</em> and <em>PPTX</em> report templates.</p><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the text align property are as follows:</p><pre>table, tr, td, th, p, and div.</pre><p>For example, if you want to align your paragraph to the left, center, and right, type the lines of code as shown in the Figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Text-align Style Example" ac:alt="Text-align Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_15.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as displayed in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Text-align Outputs" ac:alt="Text-align Outputs"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_16.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Text Transform</h2><p>The 'text transform' property controls capitalization effects of an element's text. Possible values of the 'text transform' property include:</p><ol><li><strong>capitalize<br /></strong>This will write the first character in an uppercase letter. Other characters will not be affected.</li><li><strong>uppercase<br /></strong>This will write each word in upper case letters.</li><li><strong>lowercase<br /></strong>This will write each word in lower case letters.</li><li><strong>none<br /></strong>This will not create any capitalization effects.</li></ol><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the text transform property are as follows:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to make all letters in a paragraph become uppercases, type the line of code displayed in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Text-transform Style Example" ac:alt="Text-transform Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_17.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Text-transform Style Output" ac:alt="Text-transform Style Output"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_18.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>White-Space</h2><p>The 'white-space' property affects the vertical position of white-space inside a lined box. White-space can be used in <em>RTF, ODT, DOCX</em> and <em>PPTX</em> report templates. Possible values of the 'white-space' property include:</p><ol><li><strong>normal</strong> <br />To collapse sequences of white-space and break lines as necessary to fill line boxes.</li><li><strong>pre<br /></strong>To prevent from collapsing sequences of white-space. Lines are only broken at newlines in the source or at occurrences of &quot;\A&quot; in the generated content. Report Wizard will render this value as the &lt;pre&gt; tag.</li><li><strong>nowrap<br /></strong>To collapse white-space as for 'normal' but suppressing line breaks within text.</li><li><strong>pre-wrap<br /></strong>To prevent user agents from collapsing sequences of white-space. Lines are broken at newlines in the source, at occurrences of &quot;\A&quot; in the generated content, and as necessary to fill line boxes. This value will not be rendered as pre.</li><li><strong>pre-line<br /></strong>To direct user agents to collapse sequences of white-space. Lines are broken at newlines in the source, at occurrences of &quot;\A&quot; in the generated content, and as necessary to fill line boxes. This value will not be rendered as pre.</li></ol><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the 'white-space' property are as follows:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to preserve some white-space, type these lines of code as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="White-space Style Example" ac:alt="White-space Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_19.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="White-space Style Outputs" ac:alt="White-space Style Outputs"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_20.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Width</h2><p>The 'width' property specifies the contents width of each box generated by a block-level. The value of the width property is length (See <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST" ri:content-title="Creating runtime objects from instance specifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Length Specification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for more details). Width can be used in <em>RTF, ODT,</em> and <em>DOCX</em> report templates.</p><p>The supported tag for the width property includes table.</p><p>For example, if you want to have a specific table width, type the lines of code as described in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Width Style Example" ac:alt="Width Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_21.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Width Style Outputs" ac:alt="Width Style Outputs"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_22.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Text Decoration</h2><p>The 'text decoration' property describes decorations that are added to text. The possible values of the text decoration property are as follows:</p><ul><li><strong>none<br /></strong>To produce no text decoration.</li><li><strong>underline<br /></strong>To underline each line of text.</li><li><strong>overline<br /></strong>To add a line above each line of text. </li><li><strong>line-through<br /></strong>To add a line that strikes through each line of text.</li><li><strong>blink<br /></strong>To add the blinking effect to text (being visible and invisible alternatively). The blink text decoration supports only the <em>ODT</em> report template.<br /> </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5435e63f-2782-46e4-a4bc-0cccf7deac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Report Wizard does not currently support the overline text decoration.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the text decoration property are:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to create a line-through text, type the line of code as displayed in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Text-decoration Style Example" ac:alt="Text-decoration Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_23.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Text-decoration Style Output" ac:alt="Text-decoration Style Output"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_24.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Vertical Align</h2><p>The 'vertical align' property affects the vertical position of an element inside a lined box. This property can be used in <em>RTF, ODT</em> and <em>DOCX</em> report templates. Possible values of the vertical align property include:</p><ul><li><strong>baseline<br /></strong>This value will align the baseline of a box with the baseline of the parent box. Leave the vertical alignment to be default when you encounter this value.</li><li><strong>middle<br /></strong>This value will align the vertical midpoint of a box.</li><li><strong>top<br /></strong>This value will align the top of the aligned sub-tree with the top of a line box.</li><li><strong>bottom<br /></strong>This value will align the bottom of the aligned sub-tree with the bottom of a line box.</li></ul><p>The supported tags for the vertical align property are: table, tr, td, and th, for example, see figure below:</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Vertical-align Style Example" ac:alt="Vertical-align Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_25.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Vertical-Align Style Output" ac:alt="Vertical-Align Style Output"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_26.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported CSS" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678750 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01107: Supported file types

- page_id: `169678750`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678750/Supported+file+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-26T07:35:06.376+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Linking report templates to the modeling tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1351312429

1351312429

1351312429

Besides generating Rich Text Format templates and Rich Text Format reports, Report Wizard also generates other types of document:

1. *.html – Hyper Text Markup Language
2. *.rtf – Rich Text Format
3. *.odt – OpenDocument Text
4. *.ods – OpenDocument Spreadsheet
5. *.odp – OpenDocument Presentation
6. *.docx – Microsoft Word Document
7. *.xlsx – Microsoft Excel Spreadsheet
8. *.pptx – Microsoft PowerPoint Presentation
9. *.xml – Extensible Markup Language
10. *.txt – Text
11. *.docbook or *.dbk DocBook

To create a template for a specific type of document, simply open the editor designed for that particular type of document, and then save the template making sure that the filename extension is the same as that of the type of document.

1351312428

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
